acura-rdx.subtitle: Compact luxury SUV, 2 rows — 2015–2018 (2nd gen, facelifted 2016) · 2019–2026 (3rd gen, facelifted 2022)

engine.label: Engine
engine.text: 2015–2018: 3.5L J35Z2 SOHC i-VTEC V6, naturally aspirated, port-injected (2016+ adds VCM cylinder deactivation). 2019–2026: 2.0L K20C4 DOHC VTEC Turbo inline-4, direct-injected, single-scroll turbo.

transmission.label: Transmission
transmission.text: 2015–2018: Honda 6-speed torque-converter automatic. 2019–2026: Honda 10-speed planetary automatic (10AT), paddle shifters.

drivetrain.label: Drivetrain
drivetrain.text: 2015–2018: FWD standard; AWD with Intelligent Control optional all trims. 2019–2023: FWD standard, SH-AWD (4th-gen, true torque vectoring) optional; 2024–2026: SH-AWD standard on all trims.

reliability.label: Reliability
reliability.text: 2015–2018 gen is one of Acura's most durable powertrain combinations — the J35Z2 + Honda-built 6AT avoids the ZF 9-speed problems that hit the TLX/MDX (Transmission Audit, 2026). 2019+ gen: IIHS Top Safety Pick+ (2022 RDX, Acura Newsroom); early-build 2019 models had more electronics/infotainment complaints than powertrain failures (1A Auto complaint review).

knownIssues.label: Known issues
knownIssues.text: 2015–2018: torque-converter lock-up judder and DTC P0741 (typically fluid-condition related; multiple ATF drain-and-fills with DW-1 is the first-line fix); AWD propeller-shaft vibration (Transmission Audit). 2016+ J35 VCM: monitor active engine mounts and plug fouling on deactivated cylinders. 2019–2021: limp-mode drivability events and Apple CarPlay/True Touchpad infotainment connectivity problems (1A Auto). 2019–2024 RDX included in NHTSA recall for front-passenger seat weight sensor (airbag may deploy unintentionally — Honda recall, Center for Auto Safety).

maintenance.label: Maintenance
maintenance.text: Honda Maintenance Minder. Oil 0W-20 full synthetic, Minder-driven (~7,500–10,000 mi). J35Z2: timing BELT — replace ~105k mi / 7 yr (Minder code 4) with water pump; K20C4: timing CHAIN (no scheduled service). Iridium plugs ~105k mi (J35); K20C4 per Minder. ATF: DW-1 (6AT) / ATF Type 3.1 (10AT), Minder code 3 (~30k–60k mi; severe service 30k). SH-AWD/AWD rear differential fluid (DPSF/Dual Pump) Minder-driven, commonly due early then ~30k mi. Coolant Honda Long Life Type 2: 120k mi/10 yr initial, then 60k/5 yr. Brake fluid every 3 yr.

performance.label: Performance
performance.text: 0–60: 2013–2016 V6 ≈6.1–6.4 s; 2019–2022 2.0T ≈6.2–6.6 s (period instrumented tests compiled by ZeroTo60Times). No factory performance trim; A-Spec is appearance/chassis tuning only. Max tow 1,500 lbs.

mods.label: Modifications
mods.text: Modest tuner scene: Hondata/KTuner-style ECU tunes on the K20C4 (+40–60 hp on stage-1-style tunes), intakes (HPS bolt-on kit), downpipes, lowering springs, and 19–20 in wheels. V6 gen is largely left stock. Factory A-Spec and PMC Edition (2021) are the "enthusiast" factory variants.

engineering.label: Engineering
engineering.text: Gen 2 rode on a CR-V-derived platform with strut front/multilink rear and the port-injected J35Z2 — simple, conventional, durable. Gen 3 (2019) moved to an Acura-exclusive platform with 4th-gen SH-AWD torque vectoring (70% to rear, 100% of that side-to-side), the K20C4 turbo shared with the Accord 2.0T/TLX, Honda's 10AT, and the True Touchpad Interface. 2022 facelift revised styling, added sound insulation, and retuned the 10AT.

enthusiast.label: Enthusiast
enthusiast.text: The V6 gen is respected as a "set it and forget it" Honda product; the turbo gen is viewed as the better driver thanks to SH-AWD and midrange torque. K20C4 owners borrow heavily from Accord 2.0T/Civic tuning knowledge, so bolt-on support is decent for a crossover.

comparison.label: Comparison
comparison.text: Rivals: BMW X3, Audi Q5, Mercedes GLC, Lexus NX. The RDX undercuts the Germans on price and long-term repair cost while matching them on straight-line pace; it trails the X3 on steering feel but beats the Lexus NX on power and space. Reliability record is the strongest argument for it in the class.

acura-mdx.subtitle: Midsize luxury SUV, 3 rows — 2015–2020 (3rd gen YD3/YD4, facelifted 2017) · no 2021 model year · 2022–2026 (4th gen YD9, facelifted 2025)

engine.label: Engine
engine.text: 2015–2020: 3.5L J35Y5 SOHC i-VTEC V6, direct injection, VCM (290 hp). 2017–2020 Sport Hybrid: 3.0L J30 V6 + 3 electric motors (NSX-derived hybrid system). 2022–2026: 3.5L J35 V6 DI (290 hp); Type S: 3.0L J30AC DOHC V6, single twin-scroll turbo, direct injection.

transmission.label: Transmission
transmission.text: 2014–2015: Honda 6-speed automatic. 2016–2020 gas: ZF 9HP 9-speed automatic. 2017–2020 Sport Hybrid: 7-speed DCT (NSX-derived, motor-integrated). 2022–2026: Honda 10-speed automatic (Type S: sport-tuned 10AT).

horsepower.label: Horsepower
horsepower.text: 2015–2020 gas: 290 hp @ 6,200 rpm. Sport Hybrid: 321 hp system combined. 2022–2026: 290 hp @ 6,200 rpm; Type S 355 hp @ 5,500 rpm.

torque.label: Torque
torque.text: 2015–2020 gas: 267 lb-ft @ 4,700 rpm. Sport Hybrid: 289 lb-ft (engine) @ 5,000 rpm. 2022–2026: 267 lb-ft @ 4,700 rpm; Type S 354 lb-ft @ 1,400 rpm.

drivetrain.label: Drivetrain
drivetrain.text: 2015–2020: FWD standard, SH-AWD optional (Sport Hybrid is SH-AWD only, with twin-motor rear axle). 2022–2026: FWD standard, SH-AWD optional; Type S SH-AWD standard.

reliability.label: Reliability
reliability.text: 2015 (6AT) cars are the pick of the 3rd gen; 2016–2020 9-speed cars carry the ZF 9HP baggage (see Known issues). J.D. Power Quality & Reliability: 2022 MDX 73/100 ("Average"), 2024 MDX 80/100. 2017 MDX earned a 5-star NHTSA overall rating (Acura Newsroom); 2022 MDX earned IIHS Top Safety Pick+ (Acura Newsroom).

knownIssues.label: Known issues
knownIssues.text: 2014–2015 6AT: lock-up clutch judder tied to degraded ATF. 2016 9AT: shift-to-neutral events with DTC P0657 (TSB B15-034), harsh/jerky shifts, internal leak in the 9-speed transmission warmer allowing coolant/ATF mixing (Transmission Audit). ZF 9HP behavior (delayed/harsh shifts, neutral-drop) is the subject of a class action — Moore et al. v. American Honda (covers 2015 TLX and 2016 MDX). SH-AWD rear differential pump connector corrosion triggering AWD DTCs (Transmission Audit). 2017–2020 models included in Honda's Denso low-density fuel-pump-impeller recalls (NHTSA 21V215 and the Dec 2023 expansion, Honda). 2022–2026: front-passenger seat weight sensor recall (Honda/NHTSA via Center for Auto Safety).

maintenance.label: Maintenance
maintenance.text: Honda Maintenance Minder. Oil 0W-20 (~7,500–10,000 mi). J35 V6: timing BELT ~105k mi/7 yr with water pump; J30AC turbo: timing CHAIN. Iridium plugs ~105k mi (J35). ATF: DW-1 (6AT) / ZF Lifeguard 9–spec fluid via dealer (9HP) / ATF Type 3.1 (10AT); Minder code 3, ~30k mi severe service — 9HP fluid discipline is critical given its failure history. SH-AWD rear diff fluid (DPSF; hybrid rear twin-motor unit has its own fluid) Minder-driven, early first service then ~30k mi. Coolant Type 2: 120k/10 yr then 60k/5 yr. Brake fluid every 3 yr. Sport Hybrid: additional inverter/battery cooling checks per Minder.

performance.label: Performance
performance.text: 0–60: 2014–2020 gas ≈6.1–6.4 s; Sport Hybrid ≈5.7–5.8 s; 2022 gas ≈6.0–6.4 s (Car and Driver 6.4); Type S ≈5.4–5.5 s (ZeroTo60Times/C&D compilations, TopSpeed). Type S adds double-wishbone front suspension, Brembo front brakes, adaptive air suspension, Sport+ mode, quad exhaust. Tow rating: 3,500 lbs FWD / 5,000 lbs SH-AWD.

mods.label: Modifications
mods.text: Mostly appearance and utility — wheels (20–21 in), tint, roof racks, trailer hitches; the J35 has no meaningful aftermarket tune support. The Type S is the factory enthusiast variant; mild intake/exhaust mods exist for the J30AC but the platform is too new/heavy for a serious tuner scene.

engineering.label: Engineering
engineering.text: 3rd gen (2014) went on a lighter, more rigid Pilot-related platform with the direct-injected J35 and added the NSX-tech Sport Hybrid in 2017 (7-speed DCT + twin-motor rear unit, no mechanical driveshaft). 4th gen (2022) moved to a new light-truck platform with a double-wishbone front suspension (first for MDX), wider body, Honda 10AT replacing the ZF 9-speed, and — in Type S form — Acura's first turbocharged SUV engine, the J30AC. No 2021 model year was sold; 2025 brought a styling/infotainment facelift (touchscreen replacing the touchpad).

enthusiast.label: Enthusiast
enthusiast.text: The MDX is a family hauler first, but the 2017+ Sport Hybrid has a quiet cult following as an NSX-tech sleeper, and the Type S is genuinely respected as the best-driving 3-row in its price bracket. Forum consensus: avoid early 9-speed cars without documented transmission service.

comparison.label: Comparison
comparison.text: Rivals: BMW X5, Mercedes GLE, Audi Q7, Lexus GX/RX-L, Genesis GV80, Volvo XC90. The MDX wins on resale, reliability history (V6 + Honda 10AT cars), and price-per-feature; it gives up badge prestige and interior richness to the Germans, and off-road/towing credibility to the GX. The Type S undercuts an X5 M50i by a wide margin.

acura-tlx.subtitle: Midsize sport sedan — 2015–2020 (1st gen UB1/UB2/UB3, facelifted 2018) · 2021–2026 (2nd gen)

engine.label: Engine
engine.text: 2015–2020: 2.4L K24W7 DOHC i-VTEC inline-4, direct injection (FWD only); 3.5L J35Y6 SOHC i-VTEC V6, direct injection, VCM. 2021–2026: 2.0L K20C4 DOHC VTEC Turbo inline-4; Type S (2021+): 3.0L J30AC DOHC V6, single twin-scroll turbo.

transmission.label: Transmission
transmission.text: 2015–2020 2.4: Honda 8-speed dual-clutch (8DCT) with torque converter. 2015–2020 3.5: ZF 9HP 9-speed automatic. 2021–2026: Honda 10-speed automatic (both engines).

drivetrain.label: Drivetrain
drivetrain.text: 2015–2020: FWD standard with P-AWS (Precision All-Wheel Steer) on all FWD cars; SH-AWD available on V6 only. 2021–2026: FWD standard, SH-AWD optional on 2.0T; Type S SH-AWD standard.

mpg.label: Fuel economy (MPG)
mpg.text: 2015–2020: 2.4 FWD 24/35/28; V6 FWD 21/34/25; V6 SH-AWD 21/31/25. 2021–2026: 2.0T FWD 22/31/25 (A-Spec FWD 22/30/25); 2.0T SH-AWD 21/29/24; Type S 19/25/21. Premium fuel recommended (required for rated output on turbos).

reliability.label: Reliability
reliability.text: Gen 1 is defined by its transmissions: the Honda 8DCT is durable, the ZF 9HP is the weak link (class action below). Gen 2 shows improved early reliability over gen 1 with no systemic powertrain pattern as the fleet ages (Tedious Repairs, 2026). 2022 TLX earned IIHS Top Safety Pick+ (Acura Newsroom).

knownIssues.label: Known issues
knownIssues.text: 2015–2020 V6: ZF 9HP harsh/erratic shifts, delayed engagement, neutral-drop — subject of class action Moore et al. v. American Honda (2015 TLX, 2016 MDX) and widely documented complaints; speed-sensor recall on 2017 V6/9AT cars (Center for Auto Safety). 2.4 8DCT: low-speed hesitation/judder; VTC actuator cold-start rattle carried over from the TL (Tedious Repairs). 2021–2023: fuel tank assembly recall (Honda Part 573 filing, MY2021–2023); 2018–2021/2023 TLX included in front-passenger seat weight sensor recall (Center for Auto Safety).

maintenance.label: Maintenance
maintenance.text: Honda Maintenance Minder. Oil 0W-20 (~7,500–10,000 mi). K24W7: timing CHAIN; J35Y6: timing BELT ~105k mi/7 yr with water pump; K20C4/J30AC: timing CHAIN. Iridium plugs ~105k mi (K24/J35); turbos per Minder. 8DCT: Honda DCTF-1 fluid, Minder-driven (~30–60k mi). 9HP: dealer-specified ZF Lifeguard 9–equivalent ATF — service proactively (~30–40k mi) given failure history. 10AT: ATF Type 3.1, Minder code 3. SH-AWD rear diff (DPSF) early first service then ~30k mi. Coolant Type 2: 120k/10 yr then 60k/5 yr. Brake fluid every 3 yr.

performance.label: Performance
performance.text: 0–60: 2015 2.4 ≈7.0 s; V6 FWD ≈5.7 s; V6 SH-AWD ≈5.8 s (ZeroTo60Times compilations of period tests). 2021+ 2.0T ≈5.9–6.2 s; Type S ≈4.9–5.1 s (Car and Driver/MotorTrend period instrumented tests — flagged as press-test figures, not re-verified against the source articles this session). Type S adds Brembo front brakes, adaptive dampers, quicker steering ratio, Sport+ mode, quad exhaust, 20-in wheels.

mods.label: Modifications
mods.text: The gen-2 TLX is the current darling of the Honda/Acura tuner scene: KTuner/Hondata-supported ECU tunes for the K20C4 (sharing Accord 2.0T calibration knowledge), intakes, downpipes, intercoolers, lowering springs/coilovers, and 19–20 in wheels. Type S owners add exhausts and mild tunes. Gen-1 V6 cars see basic bolt-ons; the 2.4/8DCT has niche appeal (8DCT behaves like an automated manual).

engineering.label: Engineering
engineering.text: Gen 1 (2015) merged the TL and TSX lines onto an Accord-derived platform with P-AWS rear steering on FWD cars and Honda's in-house 8DCT (a DCT with a torque converter for smooth launches). Gen 2 (2021) moved to a dedicated Acura sedan platform with a double-wishbone front suspension, 56% more torsional rigidity, longer dash-to-axle proportions, the K20C4 turbo + 10AT powertrain shared with the RDX, and the J30AC turbo V6 in the Type S — the most rigid body in Acura sedan history.

enthusiast.label: Enthusiast
enthusiast.text: The community views the gen-2 Type S as the spiritual successor to the TL Type-S and the best-handling Acura sedan short of the NSX era. Tuner support is strong because the K20C4/J30AC ecosystem overlaps with Civic Type R/Accord 2.0T development. Gen-1 V6 SH-AWD cars are cheap used performance bargains — provided the 9-speed has been serviced.

comparison.label: Comparison
comparison.text: Rivals: BMW 3 Series, Audi A4/S4, Mercedes C-Class, Genesis G70, Lexus IS, Infiniti Q50. The TLX Type S matches the M340i/S4 on pace for less money with a better reliability outlook; the base 2.0T trails German turbo fours on fuel economy but wins on standard equipment and resale. Gen-1 cars trail all of the above on transmission refinement (9HP).

acura-integra.subtitle: Premium compact 5-door liftback — 2023–2026 (DE series, single generation; Type S added 2024)

engine.label: Engine
engine.text: 2023–2026: 1.5L L15C-series (L15CA) DOHC VTEC Turbo inline-4, direct injection, shared with the Civic Si. Type S (2024–2026): 2.0L K20C1 DOHC VTEC Turbo inline-4, shared with the Civic Type R.

transmission.label: Transmission
transmission.text: 1.5T: sport-tuned CVT with paddle shifters standard; close-ratio 6-speed manual with rev-matching and helical limited-slip differential available (A-Spec w/ Technology Package). Type S: 6-speed manual only, with LSD.

horsepower.label: Horsepower
horsepower.text: 1.5T: 200 hp @ 6,000 rpm (SAE net, Acura reveal press kit; period dyno tests suggest the rating is conservative). Type S: 320 hp @ 6,500 rpm.

drivetrain.label: Drivetrain
drivetrain.text: FWD only, all trims (no AWD offered). Type S adds standard adaptive dampers and the LSD.

dimensions.label: Dimensions
dimensions.text: L 185.8 in / W 72.0 in (Type S ≈74.8 in, 2.8 in wider) / H 55.5 in / WB 107.7 in; curb ≈3,070–3,150 lbs (1.5T; A-Spec CVT listed 3,109 lbs), 3,219 lbs (Type S).

mpg.label: Fuel economy (MPG)
mpg.text: Per fueleconomy.gov (2024): 1.5T CVT 30/37/33; A-Spec CVT 29/36/32; A-Spec 6MT 26/36/30; Type S 21/28/24. Premium fuel recommended.

reliability.label: Reliability
reliability.text: Too new for long-term data, but the powertrain is a known quantity — L15C and K20C1 are high-volume Honda engines with strong records in Civic duty. J.D. Power Quality & Reliability: 2024 Integra 78/100 (HotCars/JDP summary). IIHS ratings for the 2023 Integra: Not verified this session — needs OEM/IIHS source.

knownIssues.label: Known issues
knownIssues.text: NHTSA recall 24V-744 (Oct 2024, ~1.7M vehicles): steering gearbox worm wheel can swell and increase steering effort ("sticky steering") — covers 2023–2025 Integra and 2024–2025 Integra Type S; fix is a new worm-gear spring and re-grease (NHTSA/Consumer Reports/CarComplaints). No systemic engine or transmission failure pattern documented as of this writing; 6MT cars share the Civic Si/Type R's occasional 2nd-gear grind complaints at high rpm (forum-documented, no TSB verified).

maintenance.label: Maintenance
maintenance.text: Honda Maintenance Minder. Oil 0W-20 (~7,500–10,000 mi). Both engines: timing CHAIN — no belt service. Iridium plugs per Minder. CVT: Honda HCF-2 fluid, Minder code 3 (~30k mi severe service). 6MT: Honda Manual Transmission Fluid, Minder-driven; clutch wear is driver-dependent. Coolant Type 2: 120k mi/10 yr initial, then 60k/5 yr. Brake fluid every 3 yr (more often if the Type S sees track use). Type S: LSD is housed in the transaxle and shares MTF — no separate service.

performance.label: Performance
performance.text: 0–60: 1.5T ≈7.0–7.5 s (CVT/6MT, period tests); Type S 5.1 s (Car and Driver, cited by TopSpeed) / 5.5 s (MotorWeek instrumented test). Type S top speed 167 mph; skidpad ≈1.0 g+ on 265-section Michelin Pilot Sport 4S. Type S hardware: adaptive dampers, Brembo front brakes, vented aluminum hood, 19-in wheels, triple exhaust.

mods.label: Modifications
mods.text: Lives inside the Civic ecosystem, so support is deep: Hondata FlashPro/KTuner tunes for the L15C (Civic Si calibrations) and K20C1 (Civic Type R calibrations), intakes, downpipes, intercoolers, coilovers, big-brake kits. Nearly every Civic Type R suspension/aero part has a Type S analogue. This is currently Acura's most tuner-friendly car.

engineering.label: Engineering
engineering.text: The DE Integra revived the nameplate for 2023 as a premium 5-door liftback on the 11th-gen Civic platform (strut front/multilink rear), positioned as the brand's entry model after the ILX and RSX were gone. The 2024 Type S transplants the Civic Type R powertrain wholesale — K20C1, 6MT, LSD — into a wider body with more sound deadening and luxury equipment. Built at Marysville, Ohio.

enthusiast.label: Enthusiast
enthusiast.text: The community was initially skeptical of a CVT liftback wearing the Integra badge, but the 6MT A-Spec won people over and the Type S is broadly loved as "the Civic Type R for grown-ups." Cult trim is the A-Spec Technology 6MT; resale on manuals is strong.

comparison.label: Comparison
comparison.text: Rivals: Audi A3/S3, BMW 2 Series Gran Coupe, Mercedes CLA, Acura ILX (predecessor), Honda Civic Si/Type R (in-house). The Integra beats the German entry sedans on cargo space (24.3 cu ft liftback), features-per-dollar, and reliability outlook; the Type S trades the S3/Golf R's AWD launch traction for a lighter nose and a manual they don't offer.

acura-zdx.subtitle: Midsize 2-row battery-electric SUV — 2024 model year ONLY (single-year run; production Mar 2024–Sept 2025, no 2025 model year, discontinued)

transmission.label: Transmission
transmission.text: Single-speed direct drive (fixed reduction gear), all variants.

horsepower.label: Horsepower
horsepower.text: A-Spec RWD: 358 hp (early Acura pre-production estimate was 340 hp — final certified figure is 358). A-Spec AWD: ≈490 hp (GoodCarBadCar). Type S: 499 hp ("500 hp" in Acura launch materials — most powerful Acura SUV ever at launch).

torque.label: Torque
torque.text: A-Spec RWD: 324 lb-ft. A-Spec AWD: Not verified — needs OEM source. Type S: 544 lb-ft (TopSpeed spec table).

drivetrain.label: Drivetrain
drivetrain.text: RWD standard (A-Spec single motor); AWD via dual motors (A-Spec optional, Type S standard). No mechanical SH-AWD — rear/front torque split is by inverter.

dimensions.label: Dimensions
dimensions.text: L 197.7 in / W 77.0 in / H 64.4 in / WB 121.8 in (Honda Canada spec sheet; 8 in longer wheelbase than MDX); curb ≈5,421 lbs (A-Spec RWD, retailer spec listing); AWD/Type S heavier — exact figures Not verified — needs OEM source.

reliability.label: Reliability
reliability.text: Single model year, ~19,000 units delivered by Oct 2025 (EV Charging Stations), so the sample is small. Ultium-platform siblings (Lyriq, Blazer EV) had documented software/infotainment and charging-glitches in early builds; ZDX-specific pattern data is thin. No J.D. Power or Consumer Reports reliability score verified this session — needs OEM source. Recall status: not verified this session — check NHTSA by VIN before quoting to customers.

knownIssues.label: Known issues
knownIssues.text: Not verified as a systemic pattern — needs OEM source. Items to check by VIN: Ultium-platform software updates (infotainment/charging), and note the ZDX's driver-assist ("AcuraWatch 360+") is GM Super Cruise under Acura branding, with subscription-based features after the included trial (The Gentleman Racer review). Discontinuation context: Acura skipped the 2025 model year and confirmed production ended Sept 2025 when the Honda–GM EV program was cancelled (Carscoops/Driving); ~1.5 years of production.

maintenance.label: Maintenance
maintenance.text: No engine oil, no spark plugs, no timing belt/chain, no multi-gear transmission fluid, no fuel system — the entire ICE service menu is absent. Remaining schedule: brake fluid every 3 yr (regen braking means pads/rotors often last 100k+ mi, but watch for rust-jacking from disuse); cabin air filter; tire rotation ~7,500 mi (heavy EV torque chews tires); reduction-gear/drive-unit fluid per Minder; battery coolant per Minder; 12V battery. Brake hardware is larger at the rear than front on A-Spec (12.6 in front / 13.5 in rear) due to regen bias — note when ordering parts.

performance.label: Performance
performance.text: 0–60: A-Spec RWD ≈5.5 s (retailer spec); Type S ≈4.3–4.5 s (period instrumented tests — flagged as press-test figures, not re-verified against source articles this session). Type S adds adaptive air suspension, Brembo front brakes (15.3 in rotors, 6-piston calipers), 22-in wheel option, performance-tuned steering ratio (15.9 vs 18.4). Max tow 3,500 lbs.

mods.label: Modifications
mods.text: Effectively none — the Ultium software stack is locked, there is no tuner ecosystem, and the car was discontinued after ~1.5 years. Owner activity is limited to wheels/tires, tint, PPF, and DC-charging adapters. The Type S is the factory performance variant.

engineering.label: Engineering
engineering.text: The second use of the ZDX name (the first was the 2010–2013 ICE crossover coupe), this ZDX is a GM Ultium/BEV3 platform vehicle engineered by GM with Acura styling, interior, and chassis tuning — 102 kWh NCMA pouch-cell pack under the floor, near 50/50 weight distribution, rear-biased motor layout. It was Acura's first BEV and the first Acura with Google built-in and Bang & Olufsen audio. Honda's in-house BEV successor (RSX, 2026) replaces it, making this a one-year bridge product.

enthusiast.label: Enthusiast
enthusiast.text: Little enthusiast following — it is a lease-special luxury EV, remembered mainly for steep discounts (up to ~$20–30k off MSRP late in the run per Electrek/Carscoops reporting) and as a future used-EV bargain. The Type S's "most powerful Acura SUV" title is a trivia point more than a cult draw.

comparison.label: Comparison
comparison.text: Rivals: Tesla Model Y, Cadillac Lyriq (platform twin), BMW iX, Mercedes EQE SUV, Genesis Electrified GV70, Ford Mustang Mach-E. The ZDX matched the Lyriq's hardware with Acura tuning and undercut the Germans on price — massively so with late-run discounts — but resale will suffer from the orphaned platform and brand exit; parts support continues via Acura/GM channels.

alfa-romeo-giulia.subtitle: Compact luxury sport sedan, single US generation: Type 952 "Giorgio" — 2017–2022 (pre-facelift), 2023–2026 (facelift: new "3+3" LED headlights, 12.3" digital cluster, infotainment update). Quadrifoglio sold 2017–2024; US ICE Quadrifoglio production ended March 2025. 2025–2026 US lineup is 2.0T only.

engine.label: Engine
engine.text: 2.0L turbo I4 — FCA/Stellantis GME-T4 ("Global Medium Engine," MultiAir3 electro-hydraulic intake valve actuation, direct injection, timing chain), 1,995 cc. 2.9L twin-turbo V6 — Alfa internal code 690T, Ferrari F154-family derived, 2,891 cc, timing chain (Quadrifoglio only, 2017–2024).

transmission.label: Transmission
transmission.text: ZF 8-speed torque-converter automatic — 8HP50 behind the 2.0T (per Car and Driver 2026 spec sheet), 8HP75 behind the Quadrifoglio V6; steering-column paddle shifters on most trims. US-market Quadrifoglio was automatic-only (a 6-speed manual was offered in Europe, not the US).

horsepower.label: Horsepower
horsepower.text: 2.0T: 280 hp @ 5,200 rpm (all years). Quadrifoglio 2.9T: 505 hp @ 6,500 rpm.

torque.label: Torque
torque.text: 2.0T: 306 lb-ft @ 2,000–4,800 rpm. Quadrifoglio: 443 lb-ft @ 2,500–5,500 rpm.

drivetrain.label: Drivetrain
drivetrain.text: RWD standard on 2.0T; Q4 on-demand AWD optional on 2.0T (brake-actuated limited slip, 3.15 axle ratio). Quadrifoglio is RWD only, with mechanical/torque-vectoring limited-slip differential.

dimensions.label: Dimensions
dimensions.text: Length 182.5 in (QV), width 73.7 in, height 56.1 in, wheelbase 111.0 in. Curb weight ~3,521 lb (2.0T RWD) to ~3,632 lb (Q4 AWD) and 3,806 lb (Quadrifoglio, per HotCars spec table). Trunk 13.4 cu ft (Alfa Romeo USA).

mpg.label: Fuel economy (MPG)
mpg.text: 2.0T RWD 24 city / 33 hwy / 27 comb; 2.0T Q4 AWD 23/31/26; Quadrifoglio 17/24/~20 (EPA figures as republished by KBB and dealer spec sheets; premium fuel required). Canadian 2024 AWD rating cross-check: 10.5/7.7/9.2 L/100km ≈ 22/31/26 mpg (Driving.ca).

reliability.label: Reliability
reliability.text: Below average. Consumer Reports gave the 2025 Giulia a predicted reliability score of 30/100 ("poor") — reported via Driving.ca. NHTSA recall counts by model year (api.nhtsa.gov): 2017: 7, 2018: 9, 2019: 7, 2020: 9, 2021: 6, 2022–2023: 3 each, 2024: 4. 179 total NHTSA owner complaints logged 2017–2025 (The Weekly Driver). IIHS rates the Giulia "incomplete"; no NHTSA overall crash rating. Euro NCAP 5 stars (2016).

knownIssues.label: Known issues
knownIssues.text: (1) Early-build (2017–2019) electrical/software gremlins — phantom warning lights, infotainment faults, BCM issues; Car and Driver's long-term 2018 Quadrifoglio spent 80 days in the shop over 14 months (differential replacement, oil-level sensor fault, repeated reflashes — Jalopnik/C&D). (2) Engine wiring/misfire recall 18V636000 — misfires overheat the catalytic converter, damaging engine-bay wiring (fire/stall risk), 2017–2018 2.0L. (3) Brake fluid line contacting coolant hose clamp, leak onto exhaust — fire risk, 18V828000 (2017–2018 AWD). (4) Contaminated brake fluid from factory, 17V823000 (2018). (5) BSM software can prevent ACC cancellation, 19V148000 (2017–2019). (6) Fuel pump failure/loss of power, 25V586000 (2017–2019). (7) Inaccurate fuel gauge BCM recall 19V551000 (2018–2019). (8) Turbo wastegate actuator arm wear causing P0299 underboost on 2.0T — widely documented owner-forum fix. (9) Rearview camera PCB/software failure 25V667000 (2020–2025).

maintenance.label: Maintenance
maintenance.text: Oil & filter: max 10,000 miles / 12 months (oil-life monitor may call earlier; severe duty ~4,000 miles); 2.0T takes ~5.5 qt synthetic meeting FCA spec — oil quality is critical because MultiAir uses oil pressure for valve actuation (maintenance-schedule.com). Spark plugs: 30,000-mile interval listed for the 2018 2.0T (later years differ — verify against owner's manual for the specific MY). Timing: chain on both engines — no belt service. ZF 8HP transmission: no routine ATF interval published by Alfa (fill-for-life); ZF itself recommends fluid service roughly every 60,000–80,000 miles (aftermarket guidance, not OEM). Coolant and brake-fluid intervals: brake fluid every 2 years per dealer service guidance; coolant interval not verified — needs OEM source. Quadrifoglio adds expensive consumables: ~$170 oil filter and ~$2,000 drive-belt service every 40k miles (owner reports via The Autopian comments — treat as anecdotal).

performance.label: Performance
performance.text: 2.0T RWD 0–60 in ~5.1 sec (5.4–5.5 for Q4 AWD in instrumented tests); Quadrifoglio 0–60 in 3.6 sec, quarter-mile 11.9 sec, top speed 191 mph (Supercars.net/Top Speed instrumented data). Limited editions: GTA/GTAm (540 hp, carbon aero, ~220 lb lighter) — Europe only, not US-sold; 2024 Quadrifoglio Super Sport marked the US send-off.

mods.label: Modifications
mods.text: Strong tuner ecosystem: ECU tunes from Squadra Tuning and Eurocompulsion (Stage 1–2 on the 2.0T typically 320–340 hp; Alfissimo's hybrid-turbo package claims 360–385 hp with supporting mods), Burger Motorsports/BMS intakes, downpipes and intercoolers, KW/Bilstein suspension, 19-inch wheel swaps. Quadrifoglio owners add exhausts and tunes to ~600 hp. Note: 2018+ cars need an SGW (Secure Gateway) bypass to flash ECUs.

engineering.label: Engineering
engineering.text: The Giulia rides on the Giorgio platform — a rear-drive architecture with double-wishbone front and multilink rear suspension, extensive aluminum in the body/chassis, a carbon-fiber driveshaft on all models, and a 50/50 weight distribution target. Braking is brake-by-wire (Integrated Brake System), which caused several of the software recalls above. The Quadrifoglio's 2.9L V6 is essentially a Ferrari California T (F154) V8 with two cylinders removed, and it adds an active front aero splitter.

enthusiast.label: Enthusiast
enthusiast.text: The Giulia is widely regarded by enthusiasts as the best-driving compact sport sedan of its era — steering feel and chassis balance are consistently rated above the BMW 3 Series — and the Quadrifoglio has genuine cult status. Reliability horror stories scare off casual buyers, which has crushed resale values and made used Giulias a performance bargain; dedicated specialists (Squadra, Eurocompulsion, Alfissimo) keep the tuning scene active.

comparison.label: Comparison
comparison.text: Rivals: BMW 3 Series/M3, Mercedes C-Class/AMG C 43/C 63, Audi A4/S4, Genesis G70, Cadillac CT4/CT5-V. The Giulia matches or beats all of them on steering and chassis feel but trails BMW and Genesis badly on reliability ratings and dealer support; it depreciates harder than the Germans, so it's a value buy used but a costly new-car proposition.

alfa-romeo-stelvio.subtitle: Compact luxury SUV, single US generation: Type 949 "Giorgio" — 2018–2022 (pre-facelift), 2023–2026 (facelift: "3+3" LED headlights, digital cluster, interior update). Quadrifoglio sold 2018–2024, discontinued for 2025; 2025–2026 US lineup is 2.0T AWD only.

engine.label: Engine
engine.text: Same as Giulia: 2.0L GME-T4 turbo I4 (MultiAir3, DI, timing chain) standard; 2.9L 690T twin-turbo V6 (Ferrari F154-derived, timing chain) in the Quadrifoglio (2018–2024).

transmission.label: Transmission
transmission.text: ZF 8-speed automatic — 8HP50 with the 2.0T (per Car and Driver 2026 Stelvio spec sheet), 8HP75 with the Quadrifoglio V6; column-mounted paddle shifters. No manual offered in the US.

horsepower.label: Horsepower
horsepower.text: 2.0T: 280 hp @ 5,200 rpm. Quadrifoglio: 505 hp @ 6,500 rpm (KBB 2022 Stelvio spec page).

torque.label: Torque
torque.text: 2.0T: 306 lb-ft @ 2,000–4,800 rpm. Quadrifoglio: 443 lb-ft @ 2,500–5,500 rpm.

drivetrain.label: Drivetrain
drivetrain.text: Q4 AWD. Launch years (2018–2020) offered RWD standard with Q4 AWD optional on the 2.0T; by 2022 every US Stelvio (including the Quadrifoglio) was AWD per KBB's spec pages, and 2023+ is AWD-only. Unlike the Giulia Quadrifoglio (RWD), the Stelvio Quadrifoglio is AWD. 3.73 axle ratio (C&D 2026 spec), hill descent control standard.

dimensions.label: Dimensions
dimensions.text: Length 184.6 in (2.0T) / 185.1 in (QV), width 74.9 in, height ~66 in, wheelbase 110.9 in, ground clearance 8.1 in (7.9 QV). Curb weight 4,007 lb (2.0T AWD) / 4,313 lb (QV) per KBB; GVWR 5,247 lb. Cargo 56.5 cu ft max; towing 3,000 lb (2.0T).

mpg.label: Fuel economy (MPG)
mpg.text: 2.0T AWD 22 city / 28 hwy / 24 comb (KBB/EPA, 2022); 2.0T RWD (early years) 22/29/25; Quadrifoglio 17/23/19. Premium fuel required.

reliability.label: Reliability
reliability.text: Below average, same story as Giulia. NHTSA recall counts by model year (api.nhtsa.gov): 2018: 11, 2019: 8, 2020: 6, 2021: 6, 2022: 3, 2023: 4, 2024: 3. Most complaint areas on early models: electrical, fuel system, engine (NHTSA complaint aggregations via The Weekly Driver). Alfa Romeo brand has ranked near the bottom of Consumer Reports and J.D. Power reliability studies throughout this period.

knownIssues.label: Known issues
knownIssues.text: Shares Giulia powertrain and electronics, so most Giulia issues apply. Stelvio-specific recalls: (1) Water leaking into the body control module and connectors, 18V205000 (2018). (2) Water leaking into rear liftgate wiring connectors, 18V203000 (2018). (3) Wiper motor software misinterpretation, 18V147000 (2018). (4) Engine cooling hose with inadequate webbing on Quadrifoglio, 18V635000 (2018–2019 QV). (5) Contaminated brake fluid 17V823000, misfire/catalyst overheating 18V636000, BSM/ACC software 19V148000, fuel pump failure 25V586000, rear camera PCB 25V667000, side-curtain airbag clamp 21V221000 (2021). Owner forums also document the 2.0T wastegate actuator wear (P0299) and early electrical/warning-light gremlins.

maintenance.label: Maintenance
maintenance.text: Same schedule as Giulia: oil & filter max 10,000 miles / 12 months with FCA-spec synthetic (MultiAir is oil-pressure sensitive); early 2.0T spark-plug interval 30,000 miles (verify per-MY owner's manual); timing chains — no belt service; ZF 8HP listed as fill-for-life by Alfa (ZF recommends ~60,000–80,000-mile fluid service as aftermarket guidance); brake fluid every 2 years per dealer guidance; coolant interval not verified — needs OEM source. Quadrifoglio consumables (carbon-ceramic brakes where fitted) are very expensive; CCM-brake cars were covered by recall 23V382000.

performance.label: Performance
performance.text: 2.0T AWD 0–60 in 5.4 sec, top speed 144 mph; Quadrifoglio 0–60 in 3.6 sec, top speed 176 mph (KBB 2022 spec page). Stelvio QV held the Nürburgring production-SUV lap record (7:51.7, 2017). 2024 Quadrifoglio Super Sport was the final US special edition.

mods.label: Modifications
mods.text: Same aftermarket as Giulia since the platform and powertrains are shared: Squadra/Eurocompulsion ECU tunes (2018+ requires SGW bypass), Alfissimo hybrid turbo kit (~360–385 hp claim), BMS intakes, downpipes, lowering springs (popular on Stelvio to sharpen handling), 20/21-inch wheel fitments. Quadrifoglio exhaust and tune packages push past 600 hp.

engineering.label: Engineering
engineering.text: The Stelvio is the SUV derivative of the Giorgio platform — same double-wishbone front / multilink rear suspension, aluminum-intensive structure, carbon-fiber driveshaft, and near-50/50 weight distribution as the Giulia, which is why it handles more like a sport sedan than a typical SUV. The 2023 facelift brought full-LED matrix-style headlights and a 12.3-inch digital instrument cluster but no structural changes. Its Q4 system is rear-biased and sends torque forward only when needed.

enthusiast.label: Enthusiast
enthusiast.text: The Stelvio is the enthusiast pick among compact luxury SUVs — routinely called the best-handling vehicle in its class, and the Quadrifoglio is treated as a poor-man's super-SUV. Used values have dropped steeply due to the reliability reputation, so it's a popular "second Alfa" for Giulia owners; the same Squadra/Eurocompulsion tuner community supports it.

comparison.label: Comparison
comparison.text: Rivals: BMW X3/X3 M, Mercedes GLC/AMG GLC 43/63, Porsche Macan, Audi Q5/SQ5, Genesis GV70, Jaguar F-Pace. The Stelvio out-handles the X3 and GLC and undercuts the Macan on price, but it loses on reliability ratings, interior tech polish, and resale value; the QV matches the X3 M's pace with more character but far more ownership risk.

alfa-romeo-tonale.subtitle: Subcompact luxury SUV, single US generation (SCCS "Small Wide" platform) — 2024 (PHEV-only launch year in the US), 2025–2026 (2.0T gas added for 2025; PHEV dropped for 2026, leaving gas-only per fueleconomy.gov vehicle listings). No prior US generation before 2024. Mechanical twin of the Dodge Hornet, both built in Pomigliano, Italy.

engine.label: Engine
engine.text: Gas (2025–2026 US): 2.0L GME-T4 turbo I4, transverse application, engine code EC1 (per AMSOIL vehicle lookup), direct injection, timing chain. PHEV "eAWD" (2024–2025 US): 1.3L FireFly turbo I4 (180 hp, belt/alternator/starter) driving the front wheels + 89-kW AC induction motor on the rear axle, 15.5-kWh battery, 285 hp combined (Driving.ca, KBB, fueleconomy.gov).

transmission.label: Transmission
transmission.text: Gas: 9-speed 948TE automatic (ZF 9HP family transverse unit; confirmed on multiple dealer spec listings, e.g., Zeigler/Johnson Alfa Romeo showroom data: "9-Spd 948TE FWD/AWD Auto Trans"). PHEV: 6-speed torque-converter automatic (Driving.ca; fueleconomy.gov lists "Automatic 6-spd"). No manual.

horsepower.label: Horsepower
horsepower.text: Gas 2.0T: 268 hp (2025–2026 US, per dealer spec data and Driving.ca; early launch-era/Canadian 2024 documentation listed 256 hp — treat 256 as superseded). PHEV: 285 hp combined.

torque.label: Torque
torque.text: Gas 2.0T: 295 lb-ft. PHEV: 347 lb-ft combined (KBB 2024 spec page).

drivetrain.label: Drivetrain
drivetrain.text: AWD only in the US, both powertrains. Gas models use conventional mechanical Q4 AWD with a driveshaft; the PHEV is "eAWD" — the rear axle is driven solely by the electric motor, no mechanical connection (fueleconomy.gov drivetrain field: All-Wheel Drive; KBB: all trims AWD).

dimensions.label: Dimensions
dimensions.text: Length 178.3 in, height 63.2 in, wheelbase 103.8 in, width 82 in including mirrors (KBB; body-only width not verified — needs OEM source). Curb weight 4,133 lb for the PHEV (KBB); gas-model curb weight not verified — needs OEM source. Fuel tank 11.2 gal (PHEV); turning circle 37.9 ft. Towing: gas 3,300 lb / PHEV 2,000 lb (Driving.ca: 1,500 kg / 907 kg).

mpg.label: Fuel economy (MPG)
mpg.text: Gas 2.0T AWD: 21 city / 29 hwy / 24 comb (fueleconomy.gov, 2025 and 2026 entries). PHEV: 77 MPGe combined, 29 city / 29 hwy / 29 comb on gas only, 33-mile EPA electric range, ~360-mile total range, 4-hr Level 2 charge time (fueleconomy.gov vehicle ID 47274). Note: fueleconomy.gov certifies both on regular gasoline, while KBB/dealer materials say premium recommended — flagged discrepancy.

reliability.label: Reliability
reliability.text: Too new for meaningful Consumer Reports/J.D. Power model-level data — not verified. NHTSA recall counts (api.nhtsa.gov): 2024: 6, 2025: 2, 2026: 0 as of July 2026. Shared platform/powertrains with the Dodge Hornet mean Hornet recall history is directly relevant.

knownIssues.label: Known issues
knownIssues.text: All documented issues so far are NHTSA recalls, several shared with the Hornet: (1) Brake pedal may collapse causing loss of brake function, 24V752000 (2024–2025) — the most serious one; verify completion before any brake work. (2) Rearview camera image may not display, 25V246000 (2023–2025). (3) PHEV: 12V battery positive cable and/or high-voltage connector improperly tightened — overheating/fire risk, 23V623000 (2023–2024 PHEV). (4) PHEV pedestrian alert siren missing/disconnected, 23V696000 (2023–2024 PHEV). (5) Incorrect tire-placard capacity value 24V175000 and missing rim/tire-size label info 24V209000 (label compliance). No pattern-failure TSB or class-action history yet given the model's age.

maintenance.label: Maintenance
maintenance.text: Factory schedule highlights not fully verified for this model — needs OEM source. What is verified: the gas 2.0T is the chain-driven GME family (no timing belt); oil spec lookups (AMSOIL) list the 2.0L EC1 turbo with standard Stellantis synthetic requirements. Expect Stellantis-typical 10,000-mile/12-month maximum oil intervals, but confirm against the Tonale owner's manual. The 1.3L FireFly timing drive (belt vs chain) is not verified — needs OEM source before quoting interval work. PHEV adds standard hybrid-system checks (coolant loops, 12V battery, charge port); ZF 9HP-family 948TE service fluid guidance mirrors other Stellantis applications ("lifetime" per OEM, ~60k-mile fluid service per ZF/aftermarket practice).

performance.label: Performance
performance.text: PHEV 0–60 mph in 5.6 sec (The Car Connection), 0–100 km/h in 6.2 sec (Driving.ca); top speed 125 mph (KBB). Gas 2.0T 0–60 not verified — needs OEM source (expect low-6-second range based on 268 hp/AWD). No factory performance trim exists; Veloce is an appearance/suspension trim, and 2025+ simplified to Base/Intensa-style packages.

mods.label: Modifications
mods.text: Minimal enthusiast aftermarket so far. The gas 2.0T is a GME-family engine related to the Giulia/Stelvio unit, so some Alfa tuners (Eurocompulsion et al.) have ECU-calibration experience that transfers, but Tonale-specific tune support is thin. Most owner mods are cosmetic: wheels (18–20 in fitments), tint, roof accessories. Dodge Hornet parts-bin overlap helps availability.

engineering.label: Engineering
engineering.text: The Tonale sits on the FCA SCCS "Small Wide" platform shared with the Jeep Compass/Renegade — a transverse-engine, FWD-based architecture, fundamentally different from the longitudinal Giorgio platform under the Giulia/Stelvio. It was Alfa's first US plug-in hybrid: the PHEV puts a 90-kW motor on the rear axle for through-the-road eAWD with no propshaft. DNA drive-mode selector, brake-by-wire, and a 10.25-inch Uconnect 5 cockpit are standard; build quality is a step below the Giorgio cars.

enthusiast.label: Enthusiast
enthusiast.text: The enthusiast community is lukewarm — the Tonale is widely viewed as a styled-up Dodge Hornet rather than a "real" Alfa, and its FWD-based platform lacks the Giorgio cars' balance. Styling and interior design get praise, and discounted leases have made it a value play, but it has no cult trim and little tuner following.

comparison.label: Comparison
comparison.text: Rivals: BMW X1/X2, Audi Q3, Mercedes GLA/GLB, Volvo XC40, Lexus UX — and in-house, the Dodge Hornet GT/R/T, which offers the same powertrains for thousands less. The Tonale counters with sharper styling and a nicer cabin but can't match the X1's polish or the Germans' dealer networks; resale and long-term reliability are open questions, and the Hornet's existence caps its value story.

audi-a4.subtitle: Compact executive sedan; 2015–2016 (B8.5, Typ 8K facelift, MLB), 2017–2019 (B9, Typ 8W, MLB Evo), 2020–2025 (B9.5 facelift). No 2026 A4 — nameplate discontinued after 2025 and replaced by the new A5 (B10).

engine.label: Engine
engine.text: 2015–2016: 2.0L EA888 Gen 2 TFSI turbo I4 (CAEB/CPMB, flex-fuel capable), 1,984 cc. 2017–2019: 2.0L EA888 Gen 3 TFSI turbo I4 (standard 252 hp tune; "Ultra" 190 hp efficiency tune, Gen 3B Miller-cycle). 2020–2025: 2.0L EA888 Gen 3/Gen 4 TFSI turbo I4 with 12-volt mild hybrid (40 TFSI / 45 TFSI). S4 (2018–2025, B9): 3.0L EA839 TFSI single-turbo V6 (CWGD), hot-V turbo, 2,995 cc.

transmission.label: Transmission
transmission.text: 2015–2016: FWD multitronic CVT (8 simulated ratios); quattro: 6-speed manual or 8-speed ZF 8HP Tiptronic. 2017–2025: 7-speed S tronic wet-clutch dual-clutch (DL382/0CK); 6-speed manual available on quattro 2017–2018 only. S4: 8-speed ZF 8HP Tiptronic.

drivetrain.label: Drivetrain
drivetrain.text: 2015–2016: FWD standard (CVT only); quattro AWD optional (Torsen center diff). 2017–2019: FWD standard; quattro ultra (on-demand, decoupling rear axle) optional. 2020–2025: quattro AWD standard on all US A4s. S4: quattro permanent AWD with available torque-vectoring sport differential.

mpg.label: Fuel economy (MPG)
mpg.text: 2015 EPA: FWD CVT 24/31/26; quattro 8AT 21/30/24 (E85: 15/22/18); quattro 6MT 22/32/26. 2017 EPA: FWD 25/33/28; quattro 24/31/27; quattro 6MT 24/33/27; Ultra 27/37/31. 2021 45 TFSI quattro: ~24/31/27 (EPA listing).

reliability.label: Reliability
reliability.text: B8.5 rated below average early on due to EA888 Gen 2 oil consumption and timing-chain tensioner failures (both subjects of class-action settlements; see Known issues). B9 is widely regarded as the most reliable A4 generation, with no systemic engine defect; 2017 A4 had only 1 NHTSA recall campaign (21V874, seat-heater/PODS wiring). IIHS: 2017 A4 Top Safety Pick+ (with upgraded headlights); 2022 A4 Top Safety Pick. NHTSA overall 5 stars (2017).

knownIssues.label: Known issues
knownIssues.text: B8.5 (EA888 Gen 2): excessive oil consumption from stuck oil-control rings/pistons — class action (2009–2011 CAEB engines, warranty extension to 8 yr/80k mi); timing-chain tensioner failure causing valve/piston damage — separate class-action settlement with 10-yr/100k-mi extended warranty (settlement docs at timingchain-settlement.com); water pump and thermostat housing leaks; intake-valve carbon buildup (direct injection). Recalls affecting 2013–2016 A4: 17V002 & 18V229 (2.0T electric coolant pump fire risk), 17V622 (auxiliary heater connector fire risk). B9: thermostat/water-pump housing leaks (most common repair), DL382 mechatronic solenoid faults (jerky low-speed shifts, typically 60k–100k mi if fluid never changed), front control-arm bushing wear, carbon buildup at high mileage. No major systemic engine defect documented on Gen 3/4 cars.

maintenance.label: Maintenance
maintenance.text: Per Audi US factory schedule (NHTSA-published): oil/filter every 10,000 mi or 1 yr (minor service); standard service every 20,000 mi/2 yr; brake fluid every 2 years regardless of mileage; spark plugs first at 40,000 mi then every 40,000 mi; S tronic (DL382) gear oil per schedule — specialists recommend 60k km/~40k mi despite "lifetime" language; ZF 8HP (S4) officially lifetime fill, ZF recommends service ~60–80k mi. All engines timing chain (no belt); coolant G12/G13 — level/freeze-protection check at service, no fixed replacement interval.

performance.label: Performance
performance.text: 2015–2016 quattro 0–60 ~6.4 s. 2017–2019 quattro 0–60 5.7 s (KBB); Ultra ~7.1 s. 2021+ 45 TFSI ~5.2 s (manufacturer est.). S4 0–60 4.4 s, top speed 155 mph (limited). Performance trims: S4 (354 hp); no RS4 in the US for these years.

mods.label: Modifications
mods.text: B9 2.0T is a staple of the APR/Unitronic/034Motorsport tune scene — Stage 1 ECU flash to ~282–300 hp with no hardware (Bosch MG1 ECU); Stage 2 with high-flow downpipe ~360+ hp. Common bolt-ons: intake, intercooler, downpipe, dogbone/transmission mount inserts, lowering springs or coilovers, 18–19 in wheels. S4 EA839 responds strongly to Stage 1 (~400+ hp) and upgraded turbo packages.

engineering.label: Engineering
engineering.text: B8.5 rides on the original MLB (Modularer Längsbaukasten) longitudinal matrix with the engine hung ahead of the front axle. B9 moved to MLB Evo with mixed-material construction (~99 lb lighter), five-link suspension front and rear, and introduced the DL382 wet-clutch DCT and quattro ultra, which decouples the rear axle under cruise for fuel savings. B9.5 (2020) added the 12-V MHEV belt starter-generator, MIB3 infotainment, and standard quattro in the US.

enthusiast.label: Enthusiast
enthusiast.text: The B8.5 is respected as the last Torsen-quattro, hydraulic-feel A4 and manual cars are sought after, but its engine's oil/chain reputation scares casual buyers. The B9 is prized as a tuner bargain — big power per dollar from simple tunes — and the 6-speed-manual quattro (2017–2018) is a cult find. S4 B9 is considered one of the best-value used sport sedans.

comparison.label: Comparison
comparison.text: Rivals: BMW 3 Series (F30/G20), Mercedes C-Class (W205), Lexus IS, Genesis G70. The A4 splits the difference between the sportier 3 Series and the comfort-tuned C-Class; quattro traction and the DCT make it quicker than rivals in real-world pace, while the Lexus IS/G70 beat it on long-term reliability reputation and warranty/ownership cost.

audi-a6.subtitle: Midsize executive sedan; 2015 (C7 pre-facelift, Typ 4G), 2016–2018 (C7 facelift), 2019–2025 (C8, Typ 4K, MLB Evo), 2026 (C9, Typ 4L, PPC). No US Avant in these years except A6 allroad (2020–2025) and RS 6 Avant (2021+).

engine.label: Engine
engine.text: 2015: 2.0L EA888 Gen 2 TFSI turbo I4 (CHHB, 220 hp); 3.0L EA837 supercharged V6 (CREC/CTUD, 310 hp); 3.0L TDI turbo-diesel V6 (CRTD, 240 hp). 2016–2018: 2.0L EA888 Gen 3 (252 hp); 3.0L S/C V6 333 hp (340 hp A6 Competition 2017–2018); TDI 240 hp (2016 only). 2019–2025: 2.0L EA888 Gen 4 TFSI I4 12V MHEV (45 TFSI); 3.0L EA839 turbo V6 48V MHEV (55 TFSI); S6: 2.9L EA839 twin-turbo V6 (444 hp). 2026 (C9): 3.0L EA839 turbo V6 MHEV+ (362 hp) only at US launch.

transmission.label: Transmission
transmission.text: 2015: 2.0T FWD multitronic CVT; quattro and all V6s: 8-speed ZF 8HP Tiptronic. 2016–2018: 2.0T FWD 7-speed S tronic (DL501); quattro 2.0T and 3.0T: 8-speed Tiptronic. 2019–2026: 7-speed S tronic wet-clutch DCT (DL382-family) on 45/55 TFSI; S6/RS 6: 8-speed ZF 8HP Tiptronic.

drivetrain.label: Drivetrain
drivetrain.text: C7: 2.0T offered FWD or quattro (Torsen); 3.0T/TDI/S6 quattro only. C8: quattro standard across the US range — quattro ultra (on-demand) on 45 TFSI, permanent Torsen-based quattro on 55 TFSI/S6; available all-wheel steering. C9 2026: quattro standard.

reliability.label: Reliability
reliability.text: C7 is mid-pack: solid drivetrain on 3.0T cars but the 2.0T Gen 2 shares the EA888 oil-consumption/chain-tensioner history, and the TDI was swept into the VW 3.0L diesel settlement (buyback/fix program for 2014–2016). NHTSA recall campaigns: 2016 A6 = 3 (incl. 19V035 fuel-rail leak/fire, 18V370 PODS passenger-airbag mat, 18V862 auxiliary heater connector). C8 launched rough — 2019 A6 = 7 recall campaigns (starter-generator fire risk 20V153, gateway-module water ingress 22V861, fuel sender 22V155, brake-fluid cap 23V601, rearview-camera software 25V900/26V125, airbag score line 21V159) and early MMI/electronics glitches; dependability improved from 2021. IIHS: 2022 A6 Top Safety Pick+; C9 A6 earned 2025–26 Top Safety Pick+.

knownIssues.label: Known issues
knownIssues.text: C7: 2.0T oil consumption and timing-chain tensioner (class actions; Gonzalez v. VW covers 2012–2017 Audi 2.0T incl. A6); EA837 3.0T: water pump/thermostat failure, supercharger intercooler (brick) coolant leaks, timing-chain guide wear at high mileage; carbon buildup (DI engines); TDI: dieselgate emissions settlement, HPFP and EGR-cooler issues. C8: 48V belt starter-generator failures (recall), gateway control module water damage from rear-seat spills (recall), infotainment/MMI touch freezes, water pump on EA839, sticky fuel-level sender (recall).

maintenance.label: Maintenance
maintenance.text: Audi US schedule: oil/filter 10,000 mi/1 yr; standard service 20,000 mi/2 yr; brake fluid every 2 years; spark plugs at 40,000 mi then every 40,000 mi; DSG/S tronic fluid service per schedule (40k-mi specialist recommendation); ZF 8HP (C7 V6, S6) "lifetime" fill — service at 60–80k mi advised. All engines timing chain (no belt); air suspension (optional C7/standard range C8 allroad) has no scheduled service but inspect for leaks.

mods.label: Modifications
mods.text: EA837 supercharged C7 cars are hugely popular tune platforms — a simple pulley + ECU Stage 2 yields ~400 hp (APR/034/IE). C8 55 TFSI EA839 Stage 1 nets ~400 hp; S6 2.9TT Stage 1 ~500 hp. Typical cosmetic/chassis mods: lowering springs, spacers, 20–21 in wheels, black-optics trim. RS 6 Avant has become a halo tuner car (Stage 2 ~700 hp).

engineering.label: Engineering
engineering.text: C7 (MLB) emphasized lightweight steel/aluminum hybrid construction and offered the last US Audi sedan diesel. The 2016 facelift brought the Gen 3 EA888 and the A6 Competition. C8 (MLB Evo) moved to 48V electrical architecture with belt starter-generator coasting, MMI touch response dual screens, available rear-wheel steering, and five-link suspension front/rear. C9 (2026) switches to the PPC platform with MHEV+ and an all-digital interior.

enthusiast.label: Enthusiast
enthusiast.text: The C7 3.0T is a cult "poor man's S6" — cheap supercharged power that tunes like a muscle car. The TDI has a loyal hypermiling following post-settlement. C8 appreciation centers on the RS 6 Avant, the first US RS wagon, which has genuine collector buzz; the S6's switch to the 2.9TT is viewed as characterless but effective.

comparison.label: Comparison
comparison.text: Rivals: BMW 5 Series, Mercedes E-Class, Genesis G80, Lexus GS/ES. The A6 typically undercuts the Germans on price while matching tech; the 5 Series drives sharper, the E-Class rides plusher, and the G80/Lexus beat it on reliability scores and ownership cost. quattro makes it the bad-weather pick of the segment.

audi-q5.subtitle: Compact luxury SUV; 2015–2017 (8R, first gen facelift, MLB), 2018–2020 (FY, second gen, MLB Evo), 2021–2025 (FY facelift), 2026 (third gen, Type GU, PPC platform). Sportback body added 2021.

engine.label: Engine
engine.text: 2015–2017: 2.0L EA888 Gen 2 TFSI I4 (CAEB/CPMB, 220 hp); 3.0L EA837 supercharged V6 (CTUC/CTVA, 272 hp); 3.0L TDI V6 (CPNB, 240 hp); Q5 hybrid: 2.0T + e-motor, 245 hp combined; SQ5: 3.0L S/C V6 (CTUD, 354 hp US). 2018–2020: 2.0L EA888 Gen 3 TFSI (252 hp); SQ5: 3.0L EA839 turbo V6 (354 hp). 2021–2025: 2.0L EA888 Gen 4 12V MHEV (40 TFSI 201 hp / 45 TFSI 261 hp); 55 TFSI e PHEV: 2.0T + e-motor, 362 hp combined; SQ5: 3.0L EA839 (349 hp). 2026 (GU): 2.0L EA888 Gen 5 TFSI (268 hp); SQ5: 3.0L EA839 (362 hp).

transmission.label: Transmission
transmission.text: 8R: all US models 8-speed ZF 8HP Tiptronic (incl. SQ5, TDI, hybrid). FY 4-cyl: 7-speed S tronic wet-clutch DCT (DL382); SQ5: 8-speed ZF 8HP Tiptronic. 2026 GU: 7-speed S tronic DCT.

drivetrain.label: Drivetrain
drivetrain.text: 8R: quattro permanent AWD (Torsen) standard on all US models. FY: quattro ultra on-demand AWD standard (4-cyl); SQ5 permanent quattro with available sport differential. 2026 GU: quattro ultra standard.

dimensions.label: Dimensions
dimensions.text: 8R: L 182.6 in / W 74.0 / H 65.0 / WB 110.5; curb ~4,080 (2.0T) – 4,400 lb (TDI/SQ5). FY: L 184.3 / W 74.5 / H 65.4 / WB 111.0; curb ~4,045–4,400 lb (PHEV ~4,650). 2026 GU: slightly larger, L ~185.7; US curb weight Not verified — needs OEM source.

reliability.label: Reliability
reliability.text: 8R: 2015 Q5 = 6 NHTSA recall campaigns (auxiliary heater 17V622, sunroof-drain airbag-inflator corrosion 17V001, coolant-pump fire risk 17V002 & 18V229, fuel-pump flange cracks 17V298, side-airbag inflator 16V045); engine-level reputation hurt by Gen 2 EA888 oil consumption. FY: markedly better — 2018 Q5 = 6 recall campaigns but none powertrain (brake calipers 18V369, master cylinder 19V277, trim pieces, gateway-module water ingress 21V947, pretensioners 16V955); US News/CR-derived reliability verdict 4/5 for 2019. IIHS: 2022 Q5/Q5 Sportback Top Safety Pick+. 2026 GU: too new to rate.

knownIssues.label: Known issues
knownIssues.text: 8R: EA888 Gen 2 oil consumption (class action, Q5 named; warranty extension) and timing-chain tensioner (settlement, 10-yr/100k-mi coverage); water pump/thermostat housing leaks; 3.2 FSI (pre-2013) secondary-air/carbon issues; sunroof drain clogging leading to airbag-inflator corrosion (recall); TDI included in the 3.0L diesel settlement. FY: thermostat/water-pump housing leaks (most common engine repair), DL382 mechatronic faults at 60k+ mi, gateway control module water damage from rear-seat spills (recall), front control-arm bushings, occasional oil-dilution/PCV complaints on Gen 4. No systemic engine failure pattern on Gen 3/4.

maintenance.label: Maintenance
maintenance.text: Audi US schedule: oil/filter 10,000 mi/1 yr; standard service 20,000 mi/2 yr; brake fluid every 2 years; spark plugs at 40,000 mi then every 40,000 mi; S tronic fluid service (40k-mi specialist interval); ZF 8HP (8R all, SQ5) — service 60–80k mi despite "lifetime" claim. All engines timing chain (no belt). 55 TFSI e adds charging-cable/socket and HV-system checks at standard service. Coolant G12/G13 — check only, no fixed interval.

mods.label: Modifications
mods.text: FY 2.0T responds well to Stage 1 tunes (~290–300 hp, APR/IE/Unitronic) — the most common mod by far; SQ5 EA839 Stage 1 ~400 hp, with downpipes, intercoolers and turbo upgrades popular. 8R 3.0T supercharged cars take pulley + tune to ~350 hp. Typical: 20 in wheels, lowering springs, roof boxes, brake upgrades on SQ5. SQ5 is the de facto "performance trim"; no RS Q5 exists.

engineering.label: Engineering
engineering.text: The 8R was Audi's first MLB SUV and used the Torsen-based full-time quattro with 40:60 default bias. FY moved to MLB Evo (lighter, stiffer) with quattro ultra, the DL382 DCT, and Mexico (San José Chiapa) production for North America. The 2021 facelift added 12V MHEV and MIB3; the 55 TFSI e pairs the 2.0T with a 105-kW e-motor and ~14-kWh battery. The 2026 GU generation rides on PPC (Premium Platform Combustion) with a 48V MHEV+ system (dropped on US-market cars), all-digital triple-screen interior, and Gen 5 EA888.

enthusiast.label: Enthusiast
enthusiast.text: The Q5 is Audi's volume seller, so the community is huge; 8R SQ5s (supercharged) are becoming sought-after young-timer performance SUVs. FY tuning support is mature and cheap, making the 45 TFSI a popular "sleeper" family car. Purists prefer the 8R's Torsen quattro over the FY's on-demand system, a common forum debate.

comparison.label: Comparison
comparison.text: Rivals: BMW X3, Mercedes GLC, Volvo XC60, Acura RDX, Lexus NX/RX. The Q5 is the all-rounder — less sporty than an X3 M40i but quicker than base rivals, more engaging than the GLC, and noticeably more refined than the RDX. The Lexus beats it on reliability and resale; the German rivals match it on price and tech.

audi-q7.subtitle: Three-row midsize luxury SUV; 2015 (4L, first gen final year — no 2016 US model year), 2017–2019 (4M, second gen, MLB Evo), 2020–2026 (4M facelifts: major 2020 refresh, second facelift 2025).

engine.label: Engine
engine.text: 2015 (4L): 3.0L EA837 supercharged V6 (CTWA/CTWB, 280 hp base; 333 hp S line); 3.0L TDI V6 (CATA/CNRB, 240 hp). 2017–2019 (4M): 2.0L EA888 Gen 3 TFSI I4 (CYMC, 252 hp); 3.0L EA837 supercharged V6 (CREC, 333 hp). 2020–2026: 2.0L EA888 Gen 4 TFSI 12V MHEV (45 TFSI: 248 hp 2020, 261 hp 2021+); 3.0L EA839 turbo V6 48V MHEV (55 TFSI, 335 hp); SQ7 (2020+): 4.0L EA825 twin-turbo V8 (500 hp).

transmission.label: Transmission
transmission.text: All US Q7s 2015–2026: 8-speed ZF 8HP Tiptronic torque-converter automatic (no DCT, no manual).

drivetrain.label: Drivetrain
drivetrain.text: quattro permanent AWD (Torsen/self-locking center diff) standard on all US Q7s, all years. Available all-wheel steering and adaptive air suspension (standard on SQ7; 2025 facelift made air suspension standard on more trims).

dimensions.label: Dimensions
dimensions.text: 4L (2015): L 200.3 in / W 78.1 / H 68.4 / WB 118.2; curb ~5,200–5,500 lb (approx. — confirm against OEM spec sheet for a specific trim). 4M: L 199.6 (199.3 from 2020) / W 77.5 / H 68.5 / WB 117.9; curb 4,729–4,982 lb (2.0T lightest, SQ7 heaviest).

reliability.label: Reliability
reliability.text: 4L ended its run well-sorted but old-tech; the TDI was covered by the VW 3.0L diesel settlement. 4M had a rough launch — 2017 Q7 = 7 NHTSA recall campaigns (third-row seat 16V641, EPAS steering rack 16V648, airbag module 16V519, pretensioners 16V955, side-marker software 16V642, fuel-rail leak/fire 19V035, steering-shaft bolt 20V149) and early MMI/virtual-cockpit glitches. Reliability improved steadily; 2021+ models rate average. NHTSA crash: 5-star overall (2017); IIHS Top Safety Pick+ (2017 with specific headlights).

knownIssues.label: Known issues
knownIssues.text: 4M: fuel-rail leaks (recall 19V035, fire risk — check completion); adaptive air-suspension compressor and strut failures (widely documented owner complaints, costly out of warranty); EA837 3.0T (2017–2019): water pump/thermostat, timing-chain tensioner/guide wear at high mileage; EA839 3.0T (2020+): water pump, 48V system faults; ZF 8HP low-speed 1–2/2–3 shift harshness reported (fluid level/adaptation; service helps); MMI/electronics gremlins on early cars; 2.0T Gen 3 oil consumption largely resolved vs Gen 2. 4L: EA837 chain tensioners, oil leaks (valve covers, timing covers), air suspension age failures, TDI emissions-settlement history.

maintenance.label: Maintenance
maintenance.text: Audi US schedule: oil/filter 10,000 mi/1 yr; standard service 20,000 mi/2 yr; brake fluid every 2 years; spark plugs at 40,000 mi then every 40,000 mi; ZF 8HP fluid officially lifetime — independent specialists (and ZF itself) recommend 60–80k-mi fluid/filter service. All engines timing chain (no belt). Air suspension and all-wheel-steering systems: inspection only, no scheduled service; alignment critical after suspension work. Coolant G12/G13 — check only.

performance.label: Performance
performance.text: 2015 3.0T (280 hp) 0–60 6.9 s (Edmunds tested); S line 333 hp ~6.1 s. 2017 2.0T 0–60 7.1 s; 3.0T 5.7 s (KBB). 2020+ 55 TFSI ~5.6 s; SQ7 0–60 4.3 s, top speed 155 mph (limited). SQ7 is the performance trim (no RS Q7); 2025 facelift SQ7 continues at 500 hp.

mods.label: Modifications
mods.text: 4M 3.0T supercharged (2017–2019) takes Stage 1/2 tunes to ~380–400 hp (shared EA837 tuning ecosystem). 55 TFSI EA839 Stage 1 ~400 hp; SQ7 EA825 Stage 1 ~600 hp. Common: 21–22 in wheels, lowering links for the air suspension, roof racks/boxes, towing packages (4M rated to 7,700 lb with V6/V8). The 2.0T is rarely tuned — owners typically trade up.

engineering.label: Engineering
engineering.text: The 4L shared its PL71 platform with the first-gen VW Touareg/Porsche Cayenne and was famously heavy. The 4M (MLB Evo) cut up to ~700 lb with an aluminum-intensive body, added available four-wheel steering, adaptive air suspension, and the virtual cockpit, and introduced a four-cylinder option in a full-size Audi SUV. The 2020 facelift swapped the supercharged EA837 for the 48V-MHEV turbo EA839 and dual-touchscreen MMI; the 2025 second facelift brought new lighting tech (HD Matrix LED, OLED tails) and expanded standard equipment.

enthusiast.label: Enthusiast
enthusiast.text: The Q7 is a family hauler first, but the SQ7 has genuine sleeper cred — a 500-hp V8 three-row with rear steer that flies under the radar next to an X7 or GLS. 4L TDIs have a die-hard towing/road-trip following (they were bought back or fixed under the diesel settlement, so documentation matters). The 2017–2019 supercharged 3.0T is the used-market sweet spot for tuners.

comparison.label: Comparison
comparison.text: Rivals: BMW X5/X7, Mercedes GLE/GLS, Volvo XC90, Acura MDX, Genesis GV80. The Q7 offers the best third-row packaging and bad-weather traction of the German set, with a calmer ride than the X5 and better tech than the XC90. It trails the MDX/GV80 on reliability and value, and the X5 on steering feel; the SQ7 undercuts an X7 M60i by a wide margin.

audi-q3.subtitle: Subcompact/compact luxury SUV; 2015–2018 (8U, first gen, PQ35 platform), 2019–2025 (F3, second gen, MQB), 2026 (third gen, MQB Evo). US lineup is 2.0T gasoline only; no RS Q3 in the US.

engine.label: Engine
engine.text: 2015–2018: 2.0L EA888 Gen 2 TFSI turbo I4 (CCTA/CPSA), 1,984 cc, transverse. 2019–2025: 2.0L EA888 Gen 3 TFSI (45 TFSI, 228 hp; 40 TFSI detune, 184 hp, added 2022). 2026: updated 2.0L EA888 TFSI (255 hp). No diesel, no hybrid in the US.

transmission.label: Transmission
transmission.text: 2015–2018: 6-speed Tiptronic torque-converter automatic (09M-family; EPA code S6). 2019–2025: 8-speed Tiptronic automatic (EPA code S8). 2026: 7-speed S tronic wet-clutch dual-clutch. No manual in the US.

drivetrain.label: Drivetrain
drivetrain.text: 2015–2018: FWD standard; quattro (Haldex-type on-demand) optional. 2019–2025: quattro standard on all US Q3s (on-demand with rear decoupling). 2026: quattro standard.

dimensions.label: Dimensions
dimensions.text: 8U: L 172.6 in / W 72.1 / H 62.5 / WB 102.5; curb 3,494 (FWD) – 3,682 lb (quattro). F3: L 176.6 / W 72.8 / H 62.9 / WB 105.5; curb ~3,902–4,000 lb. 2026 (3rd gen): ~1.8 in longer than F3; exact US curb weight Not verified — needs OEM source.

mpg.label: Fuel economy (MPG)
mpg.text: 2015 EPA: FWD and quattro both 20/28/23. 2019 EPA (45 TFSI quattro): 19/27/22. 40 TFSI quattro (2022+): 23/30/~26 (dealer-published EPA). 2026 EPA (mfr-published): 22/29/25.

reliability.label: Reliability
reliability.text: 8U: simple, proven drivetrain; main demerit is the Gen 2 EA888 oil-consumption history (Q3 not named in the original class action, which covered 2009–2011, before US Q3 launch, but the engine family is the same). F3: strong record — 2019 Q3 = 3 NHTSA recall campaigns, none powertrain (steering-rack circlip 21V027 — one vehicle, passenger-airbag tear seam 20V273, TPMS software 22V815); 2025 Q3 cited at 1 recall / 1 complaint with an 86/100 quality-reliability score (J.D. Power data via Top Speed). 2026: too new to rate.

knownIssues.label: Known issues
knownIssues.text: 8U: water pump/thermostat housing leaks (EA888 family pattern), carbon buildup at high mileage, occasional PCV valve failures; few systemic complaints otherwise. F3: no systemic engine defect documented; isolated early-build items (airbag tear seam, steering rack — both recalls), some infotainment/MMI reboots, sunroof drain clogs. DL382 does not apply here (torque-converter automatics through 2025) — a reliability point in the Q3's favor vs longitudinal Audis.

maintenance.label: Maintenance
maintenance.text: Audi US schedule: oil/filter 10,000 mi/1 yr; standard service 20,000 mi/2 yr; brake fluid every 2 years; spark plugs at 40,000 mi then every 40,000 mi. 8U 6-speed and F3 8-speed automatics: "lifetime" fill per Audi — fluid service at 60–80k mi recommended by specialists; 2026 S tronic: DSG fluid per schedule (~40k-mi specialist interval). All engines timing chain (no belt). Haldex/quattro rear coupling: fluid/filter service every ~3 years recommended (often skipped — check on used cars).

performance.label: Performance
performance.text: 2015–2018 quattro 0–60 ~7.8–8.2 s. 2019–2025 45 TFSI 0–60 7.0 s (auto-data); 40 TFSI ~8.6 s. 2026: 0–60 5.5 s (Audi "best in class" claim; MotorWeek first drive). Top speed 130 mph (limited). No factory performance trim in the US (SQ3/RS Q3 not sold here).

mods.label: Modifications
mods.text: Light scene — 45 TFSI responds to Stage 1 ECU tunes (~260–270 hp) from the usual EA888 shops (APR, Unitronic, IE); 8U cars get intake/downpipe/coilovers on occasion. Most owners stick to wheels (18–20 in), spacers, and roof accessories. Enthusiasts wanting speed buy an S3 instead (same platform family).

engineering.label: Engineering
engineering.text: The 8U rode on the older PQ35 architecture (Golf Mk5/6-derived) — the last non-MQB car in Audi's US line — which is why it felt dated by 2018. F3 moved to MQB with a transverse EA888, ~96 mm more length, modern MIB infotainment, and quattro ultra-style decoupling. The 2026 third generation adopts MQB Evo, swaps the 8-speed automatic for a 7-speed S tronic, and adds the newest digital cockpit and driver-assistance stack.

enthusiast.label: Enthusiast
enthusiast.text: The Q3 is a sensible-shoes entry Audi, not an enthusiast darling; the community views 8U as basic but durable and F3 as genuinely competitive. The 2026 reset (255 hp, 5.5 s) has drawn positive press as a "mini-Q5." Tuner mindshare stays with the A3/S3/RS 3 sedans that share its platform.

comparison.label: Comparison
comparison.text: Rivals: BMW X1/X2, Mercedes GLA/GLB, Volvo XC40, Lexus UX, Acura ADX. The Q3 matches the X1 on driving polish and beats the GLA on cabin quality; the Lexus wins on reliability and the XC40 on design/value. The 2026's power bump makes it the straight-line pick of the segment per Audi's own class claim.

audi-e-tron.subtitle: Battery-electric midsize SUV; 2019–2023 e-tron / e-tron Sportback (GE, MLB Evo-derived BEV), 2024–2025 renamed Q8 e-tron / Q8 Sportback e-tron (facelift, bigger battery). Not to be confused with the unrelated Q4 e-tron (MEB) or e-tron GT (J1). e-tron line ends after 2025 in the US.

engine.label: Engine
engine.text: Dual asynchronous (induction) motors, one per axle — front ~184 hp / rear ~224 hp (2019–2023, 95 kWh pack). System output: 355 hp normal / 402 hp in boost mode (e-tron 55 quattro). Q8 e-tron (2024+): same layout, revised rear motor with more windings; 335 hp normal / 402 hp boost. SQ8 e-tron (2024+): tri-motor (one front, two rear), 496 hp.

transmission.label: Transmission
transmission.text: Single-speed fixed-ratio reduction gear per axle (EPA code A1; A2 on Q8 e-tron). No multi-gear transmission, no clutch.

horsepower.label: Horsepower
horsepower.text: 355 hp combined (402 hp boost) — 2019–2025 e-tron 55 / Q8 e-tron 55. SQ8 e-tron: 496 hp.

torque.label: Torque
torque.text: 414 lb-ft combined normal / 490 lb-ft boost (664 Nm). SQ8 e-tron: 718 lb-ft (boost). Front motor ~228 lb-ft, rear motor ~262 lb-ft individually.

drivetrain.label: Drivetrain
drivetrain.text: Electric quattro AWD standard — rear-biased torque split, front axle engages on demand; no mechanical connection between axles. SQ8 e-tron adds electric torque vectoring via dual rear motors.

dimensions.label: Dimensions
dimensions.text: e-tron SUV: L 193.0 in / W 76.3 / H 65.5 / WB 115.1; curb ≈5,750 lb US spec (EU DIN 2,490 kg — exact US curb by trim Not verified, needs OEM source). Sportback: ~2 in lower roof, same footprint. Q8 e-tron: same basic dimensions (L ~193.0, WB 115.1).

mpg.label: Fuel economy (MPG)
mpg.text: EPA MPGe — 2019 e-tron: 74 city / 73 hwy / 74 combined (46 kWh/100 mi). 2024 Q8 e-tron SUV: 80/83/81 (41 kWh/100 mi); Q8 Sportback e-tron: 84/90/87; Sportback ultra: 84/89/86. EPA range: 204 mi (2019–2020); 222 mi (2021–2023 SUV); Q8 e-tron SUV 285 mi (2024); Sportback rated higher (~296–300 mi depending on aero package — confirm trim-specific figure on fueleconomy.gov).

reliability.label: Reliability
reliability.text: Mixed. 2019 e-tron = 5 NHTSA recall campaigns: 19V434 (charging-socket seal → moisture in HV battery, fire risk), 23V867 (HV battery modules may overheat — diagnostic software + module replacement), 23V842 (240V charging-cable overheating), 24V621 (brake-pedal-to-booster fastener may detach), 25V900 (rearview-camera software). Beyond recalls, owners report 12V battery failures, charging-door actuator faults, and occasional drive-unit bearing noise; no widespread motor or pack degradation pattern. The Q8 e-tron's larger, revised pack (114 kWh) has a cleaner early record.

knownIssues.label: Known issues
knownIssues.text: HV battery moisture ingress via charge-port seal (recall 19V434 — verify completion on any 2019); battery module overheating population (recall 23V867 covers 2019–2022); 12V battery drains causing no-start; electric brake booster / pedal fastener (recall 24V621); charge-port door actuator failures; MMI/software glitches; air-suspension compressor wear on optioned cars. Battery thermal-management coolant system requires leak checks at service.

maintenance.label: Maintenance
maintenance.text: No engine oil, spark plugs, timing components, or fuel system. Audi EV schedule: brake fluid every 2 years (regen means friction brakes wear slowly but fluid still hygroscopic — also inspect for caliper seizure from disuse); battery/electronics coolant level and freeze protection at standard service (20k mi/2 yr); cabin air filter; reduction-gear (drive unit) oil — check level, no fixed interval published; tire rotation critical (heavy EV, ~5,700+ lb); 12V battery test at each service. HV battery warranty 8 yr/100k mi.

performance.label: Performance
performance.text: e-tron 55 0–60 5.5 s (boost); e-tron Sportback 5.5 s; Q8 e-tron 5.3–5.6 s; SQ8 e-tron 4.5 s. Top speed 124 mph (limited; SQ8 130). DC fast charge 150 kW (95 kWh) / 170 kW (Q8 e-tron 114 kWh), 10–80% in ~31 min; tow rating 4,000 lb.

mods.label: Modifications
mods.text: Minimal — BEV powertrain is not tunable in the aftermarket; the scene is limited to wheels (aero-friendly 20–22 in), lowering links for the air suspension, vinyl/wraps, and brake/tire upgrades. The SQ8 e-tron tri-motor is the factory "performance mod." Some owners retrofit plug-and-play range/display software tweaks, but no power gains exist.

engineering.label: Engineering
engineering.text: The e-tron was Audi's first US BEV, built on an MLB Evo-derived skateboard with a 95-kWh gross (86–88 kWh usable, ~12% reserve for longevity) liquid-cooled pack under the floor and asynchronous motors chosen for durability over rare-earth-free permanent magnets. It pioneered 150-kW sustained DC charging with a flat charge curve, electrohydraulic brake blending, and (2019 US-first) camera-based virtual mirrors — though US cars kept conventional mirrors. The 2024 Q8 e-tron rename brought a 114-kWh pack (106 usable), revised rear motor (14 vs 12 windings), better aero, and 170-kW charging.

enthusiast.label: Enthusiast
enthusiast.text: Viewed as the "drive it like a normal Audi" EV — praised for refinement, build quality, and charging curve, criticized for range versus Tesla/Rivian. Depreciation has been savage, making used 2019–2021 cars a value play for shop customers; the community's main advice is verifying recall 19V434/23V867 completion and battery-health reports before purchase. SQ8 e-tron has a small but loyal following as a stealth performance SUV.

comparison.label: Comparison
comparison.text: Rivals: Tesla Model X/Y, Jaguar I-PACE, Mercedes EQE SUV, BMW iX, Rivian R1S. The e-tron loses decisively on range and efficiency (74 MPGe vs 100+ for most rivals) but wins on cabin quality, ride isolation, and conventional ergonomics. On reliability it trails the Tesla drivetrain but has fewer build-quality complaints; on value it is strong only as a used buy.

audi-s4.subtitle: Compact sport sedan (A4-based); 2015–2016 (B8.5, Typ 8K facelift), 2018–2025 (B9/B9.5, Typ 8W). No US 2017 S4 model year. Sedan only in the US. Distinct from the A4: supercharged (B8.5) or turbo (B9) V6, sport suspension, available sport differential.

engine.label: Engine
engine.text: 2015–2016: 3.0L EA837 supercharged V6 (CGXC/CREC), 2,995 cc, Eaton TVS1320 Roots-type blower in the V. 2018–2025: 3.0L EA839 single-turbo V6 (CWGD), twin-scroll turbo in the hot V, aluminum block; US cars have no 48V MHEV.

transmission.label: Transmission
transmission.text: 2015–2016: 6-speed manual standard; 7-speed S tronic dual-clutch (DL501) optional. 2018–2025: 8-speed ZF 8HP Tiptronic torque-converter automatic only (no manual, no DSG).

drivetrain.label: Drivetrain
drivetrain.text: quattro permanent AWD standard all years — Torsen/self-locking center diff, 40:60 rear-biased default; available torque-vectoring sport rear differential (option on both gens).

dimensions.label: Dimensions
dimensions.text: 2015–2016: L 185.1 in / W 71.9 / H 55.5 / WB 110.6; curb ≈3,850–3,950 lb (approx. — confirm trim-specific figure with OEM). 2018–2025: L 186.8 / W 72.5 / H 55.3 / WB 111.0; curb ≈3,850–3,900 lb (approx. — confirm with OEM).

reliability.label: Reliability
reliability.text: Better than the 2.0T A4s of the same eras — neither V6 has a systemic defect. B8.5's EA837 is considered one of Audi's sturdier engines (chain-driven, but far fewer tensioner failures than the EA888; watch water pump/thermostat). 2018 S4 = 2 NHTSA recall campaigns, both the PODS passenger-airbag wiring issue shared with A4/A5 (19V547, 21V874) — no powertrain recalls. IIHS ratings mirror the A4 body (2018 A4 Top Safety Pick+ with upgraded headlights).

knownIssues.label: Known issues
knownIssues.text: B8.5: water pump and thermostat housing failure (most common repair), supercharger intercooler core ("brick") coolant leaks, intake-valve carbon buildup (walnut-blast service by ~80k mi), DL501 S tronic mechatronic faults and clutch wear on DSG cars, timing-chain guide rattle at very high mileage, sport-diff fluid seepage. B9: water pump/thermostat leaks on the EA839, occasional rocker-arm wear (valvetrain tick) at high mileage, ZF 8HP low-speed shift harshness cured by fluid service/adaptation, PODS recall (see above), control-arm bushings. No oil-consumption epidemic on either engine.

maintenance.label: Maintenance
maintenance.text: Audi US schedule: oil/filter 10,000 mi/1 yr (5,000-mi interval strongly advised on tuned or hard-driven cars); standard service 20k/2 yr; brake fluid every 2 years; spark plugs at 40,000 mi then every 40,000 mi; DL501 S tronic fluid (B8.5 DSG) at 40k mi; ZF 8HP fluid (B9) at 60–80k mi despite "lifetime" claim; sport rear differential fluid ~40k mi. Both engines timing chain (no belt). Carbon-cleaning intake valves by 80–100k mi on both (direct injection).

performance.label: Performance
performance.text: 2015–2016: 0–60 4.9 s (both transmissions; manual tested as quick as 4.5 s by some outlets), ¼-mile ~13.2, top speed 155 mph (limited). 2018–2025: 0–60 4.4 s, top speed 155 mph. The S4 is the performance trim between the A4 45 TFSI (261 hp) and the (Europe-only) RS 4 Avant.

mods.label: Modifications
mods.text: One of the most-tuned Audi platforms in the US. B8.5: dual-pulley (crank + supercharger) Stage 2 kits to ~420–450 hp (APR, IE, 034Motorsport), heat exchanger upgrades, downpipes, and DL501 TCU tunes are standard practice. B9: Stage 1 ECU to ~400 hp, Stage 2 with downpipe ~430 hp, upgraded-turbo Stage 3 kits 500+ hp; ZF 8HP TCU tunes, intercoolers, and dogbone inserts common. Chassis: lowering springs/coilovers, sway bars, 19 in wheels.

engineering.label: Engineering
engineering.text: The B8.5 S4 kept the MLB layout with the supercharged EA837 — throttle response and a linear powerband were the design goals over peak efficiency. The B9's switch to the EA839 turbo V6 (hot-V, single twin-scroll) traded the blower's immediacy for a fatter midrange (369 vs 325 lb-ft at 1,370 rpm) and better economy; it also moved from the DL501 DSG to the more durable ZF 8HP. B9.5 (2020+) received MIB3 electronics and mild styling updates; the mechanical package was unchanged through the 2025 finale.

enthusiast.label: Enthusiast
enthusiast.text: The B8.5 manual is a genuine cult car — last stick-shift S4, supercharged soundtrack, Torsen quattro — and clean examples hold money. The B9 is the definitive "Q-ship" daily: fast, understated, reliable, and cheap to tune; forums regard it as the best used sport-sedan value this side of a BMW M340i. Both have deep APR/034/IE/Unitronic support.

comparison.label: Comparison
comparison.text: Rivals: BMW M340i/335i, Mercedes C43 AMG, Genesis G70 3.3T, Cadillac CT4-V/ATS-V. The S4 is typically the reliability and value pick — the B9 is cheaper to buy used than an M340i and its ZF 8HP is stouter than the BMW's early B58 cooling ancillaries; the C43 feels sharper but costs more to maintain; the Cadillac out-handles it but trails on interior quality. All-weather pace per dollar is the S4's calling card.

bmw-3-series.subtitle: Compact luxury sedan (wagon/Gran Turismo excluded), 2015–2018 (F30, incl. 2016 LCI), 2019–2026 (G20, LCI from 2023)

engine.label: Engine
engine.text: F30 (2015–2018): 320i/320i xDrive — N20B20 2.0L turbo I4; 328i (2015) — N20B20 2.0L turbo I4; 328d — N47D20 2.0L turbo-diesel I4; 335i (2015) — N55B30 3.0L turbo I6; 330i (2016–2018, LCI) — B48B20 2.0L turbo I4; 340i (2016–2018) — B58B30 3.0L turbo I6. G20 (2019–2026): 330i/330i xDrive — B48B20 2.0L turbo I4; 330e/330e xDrive — B48 2.0L + electric motor (PHEV); M340i/M340i xDrive (2020–2026) — B58B30 3.0L turbo I6 (48V mild-hybrid added with 2023 LCI). All direct-injected, single twin-scroll turbo (N20/N55/B48/B58); all use timing chains.
