transmission.label: Transmission
transmission.text: F30: 6-speed manual (available RWD 320i/328i/330i/335i/340i — confirmed in fueleconomy.gov option lists through 2018) or ZF 8HP 8-speed "Steptronic" automatic (GA8HP45Z on 4-cyl, GA8HP50Z on 6-cyl). G20: ZF 8HP51 8-speed Steptronic automatic only in the US (no manual).

drivetrain.label: Drivetrain
drivetrain.text: F30: RWD standard; xDrive AWD optional on 320i/328i/330i/340i (328d xDrive standard on wagon, RWD/AWD sedan). G20: 330i RWD or xDrive; 330e RWD or xDrive; M340i RWD (through ~2021) or xDrive — US range has been effectively xDrive-only on M340i in recent years.

dimensions.label: Dimensions
dimensions.text: F30 sedan: length 182.5 in / width 71.3 in / height 56.3 in / wheelbase 110.6 in; curb ~3,295–3,760 lb (340i xDrive sedan listed at 3,695 lb). G20 sedan: length 185.7 in / width 71.9 in / height ~56.4 in / wheelbase 112.2 in; curb ~3,582 lb (330i RWD) to ~3,900 lb (M340i xDrive).

mpg.label: Fuel economy (MPG)
mpg.text: (EPA city/hwy/combined, fueleconomy.gov) 2018 330i auto RWD: 24/34/27; manual: 21/32/25. 2017 340i auto RWD: 21/32/25. 2016 328d RWD: 31/42/35. 2021 330i RWD: 26/36/30. 2021 M340i RWD: 23/32/26. 2021 330e: 25/33/28 gasoline, 45 MPGe combined, ~320 mi total range. xDrive typically costs 1–2 mpg.

reliability.label: Reliability
reliability.text: Split story. 2012–2015 N20 cars carry the well-documented timing-chain defect (see Known issues); post-LCI B48/B58 cars (2016+) are materially better. G20 is regarded as one of the more reliable modern BMWs; Consumer Reports has rated recent G20 model years average-to-above-average. NHTSA recall counts (api.nhtsa.gov): 2021 330i — 7; 2021 M340i — 6 (mostly minor software/electrical campaigns). IIHS rated G20 "Good" in core crashworthiness tests; TSP/TSP+ awards vary by year and headlight fitment — verify per model year at iihs.org.

knownIssues.label: Known issues
knownIssues.text: F30: (1) N20/N26 timing chain + oil-pump drive chain wear/failure — BMW SIB B11 03 17 extended coverage to 7 yr/70k mi, followed by a class-action settlement (SIB B11 02 21) for cars produced up to Feb 2015; failure can destroy the engine. (2) N20 NVLD evaporative leak module — warranty extended to 10 yr/120k mi (SIB B01 07 16). (3) Electric water pump/thermostat failures; oil filter housing gasket and valve cover gasket leaks (all engines). (4) N55 wastegate rattle. (5) EPS steering rack failures reported. G20: cooling-system coolant loss on B48/B58 (expansion tank, water pump, heat-management module) is the dominant pattern; oil filter housing/cooler gasket seepage; early iDrive 7 software bugs. Sources below.

maintenance.label: Maintenance
maintenance.text: Oil: Condition Based Service (CBS), max ~10,000 mi/12 months (N20/N55: BMW LL-01; B48/B58: LL-01 FE / LL-17 FE+ 0W-20/0W-30). Transmission: BMW calls the 8HP fluid "lifetime fill," but ZF (the gearbox maker) recommends fluid + pan/filter every ~40k–60k mi — the accepted independent-shop practice. Spark plugs: ~60,000 mi on turbo engines per BMW NA service info (shorter on tuned cars). Coolant: long-life; no fixed factory interval (inspect; typical shop practice 4–6 yr). Brake fluid: every 2 years. Timing: chain on all engines — no belt service; on N20 cars the chain/tensioner is a wear item (see Known issues).

performance.label: Performance
performance.text: 0–60 mph: 328i ~5.6 s; 340i ~4.4–4.6 s (auto); G20 330i ~5.6 s (manufacturer); M340i 3.8 s as instrument-tested by Car and Driver (2020 RWD test car; BMW claimed 4.4). Performance trims: F30 M Sport packages; 340i "Track Handling Package"; G20 M340i is a factory M Performance model (M Sport diff, brakes, suspension); M3 (F80/G80) sits above this data set.

mods.label: Modifications
mods.text: Huge tuner support. N55/B58: MHD and Bootmod3 flash tunes (Stage 1 B58 ≈ +40–60 hp), downpipes, charge pipes (factory plastic B58 charge pipe is a known failure point under boost — aftermarket aluminum units are common), intercoolers/heat exchangers (CSF), intakes. N20: downpipes and JB4 piggybacks. Chassis: H&R/KW/Bilstein suspension, M Sport brake upgrades; F-chassis wheels are 5x120, G-chassis 5x112. Factory performance variants: 340i, M340i (and M3 above them).

engineering.label: Engineering
engineering.text: F30 (2012–2018) moved the 3 Series to full turbocharging and electric power steering; the 2016 LCI swapped the N20/N55 for the modular B48/B58 engines, which fixed the worst N20 weaknesses. G20 (2019+) rides on the CLAR cluster architecture with aluminum-intensive body panels, increased rigidity, and standard 8HP51; 2023 LCI added the curved iDrive 8 display and 48V mild-hybrid assist on the M340i. The B58's closed-deck block and water-to-air intercooler integrated into the intake manifold are why it tunes so well.

enthusiast.label: Enthusiast
enthusiast.text: The G20 M340i is the community darling — widely considered the best daily-drivable BMW of the modern era, with strong B58 tuner headroom. F30 340i is a value performance buy; N20 cars (320i/328i) are viewed warily unless the timing chain has been done. Manual F30s are increasingly collectible.

comparison.label: Comparison
comparison.text: Rivals: Mercedes C-Class (W205), Audi A4 (B9), Lexus IS, Genesis G70. The 3 Series remains the driver's benchmark of the segment; the G20 beats the C-Class and A4 on powertrain durability (B48/B58 vs. M264/EA888 oil/consumption quirks) but a Lexus IS 350 outlasts all of them. Maintenance costs run higher than Japanese rivals — cooling-system parts and 8HP services are the recurring shop tickets.

bmw-5-series.subtitle: Mid-size luxury sedan (GT excluded), 2015–2016 (F10, post-LCI), 2017–2023 (G30, LCI from 2021), 2024–2026 (G60)

engine.label: Engine
engine.text: F10 (2015–2016): 528i — N20B20 2.0L turbo I4; 535i — N55B30 3.0L turbo I6; 535d — N57D30 3.0L turbo-diesel I6; 550i — N63B44TU 4.4L twin-turbo V8. G30 (2017–2023): 530i — B48B20 2.0L turbo I4; 530e — B48 + e-motor PHEV; 540i — B58B30 3.0L turbo I6 (48V from 2021 LCI); M550i — N63B44TU2 4.4L TT V8 (2018–2019), N63B44TU3 (2020–2023). G60 (2024–2026): 530i/530i xDrive — B48 2.0L turbo + 48V; 540i xDrive — B58 3.0L turbo + 48V; 550e xDrive — B58-based PHEV (2025); i5 eDrive40/M60 (EV, SSB/HB motors) share the body. All chain-driven.

transmission.label: Transmission
transmission.text: All gens: ZF 8HP 8-speed Steptronic automatic (GA8HP45Z on 4/6-cyl, GA8HP70/8HP75 on V8; 8HP51 on G30, 8HP60 on G60). 2015–2016 F10 US cars are automatic-only per fueleconomy.gov option lists (early F10 535i 6MT ended before 2015). No manual on G30/G60.

drivetrain.label: Drivetrain
drivetrain.text: F10: RWD standard; xDrive AWD optional on 528i/535i, standard on 550i xDrive (RWD 550i also offered); 535d RWD or xDrive. G30: 530i/540i RWD or xDrive; 530e RWD or xDrive; M550i xDrive-only. G60: 530i RWD or xDrive; 540i xDrive-only; 550e/i5 xDrive (M60) or RWD (eDrive40).

dimensions.label: Dimensions
dimensions.text: F10 sedan: 192.9 L x 73.2 W x 57.6 H in, WB 116.9 in; curb ~3,814 lb (528i) to ~4,431 lb (550i xDrive). G30 sedan: 194.6 L x 73.5 W x 58.2 H in, WB 117.1 in; curb ~3,765 lb (530i RWD) to ~4,275 lb (M550i). G60 sedan: 199.2 L x 74.8 W x 59.6 H in, WB 117.9 in; curb ~4,031 lb (530i) to ~4,369 lb (540i xDrive).

reliability.label: Reliability
reliability.text: F10: dragged down by the N63 V8 (550i) — subject of multiple oil-consumption class actions; 4-cyl/6-cyl cars are average for the class. 2015 528i shows just 1 NHTSA recall. G30: one of BMW's better reliability performers; Consumer Reports has rated it average-to-above-average; NHTSA counts: 2018 530i — 2 recalls, 2021 530i — 7, 2024 530i — 6 (G60 launch-year campaigns, incl. brake-system software). IIHS crashworthiness "Good" across gens; TSP awards vary by year/headlights.

knownIssues.label: Known issues
knownIssues.text: F10: (1) N63/N63TU oil consumption — valve stem seals, turbo oil lines, timing chains; BMW's own SIB allowed up to 1 qt per 750 miles; class actions Bang v. BMW (2009–2014 N63) and Isley v. BMW (N63TU) produced settlements with free consumption tests/repairs; N63TU1 settlement service campaign documented at NHTSA. (2) N20 timing chain (same SIB B11 03 17 as 3 Series). (3) Electric water pump/thermostat failure. (4) N55 wastegate rattle. (5) Battery drain/IBS issues. G30: B48/B58 coolant loss (water pump, expansion tank, heat-management module); 48V MHEV battery failures on 2021+ cars; a 2021 M550i/540i xDrive software bug limiting turbo boost was fixed by BMW update (documented by BimmerLife/C&D). G60: too new for a failure pattern; recall campaigns to date are largely software/brake-system related.

maintenance.label: Maintenance
maintenance.text: Oil: CBS, max ~10k mi/12 mo (N63 owners should halve that — the V8 cooks oil). Transmission: "lifetime fill" per BMW; ZF-recommended 8HP service ~40k–60k mi is standard shop practice. Spark plugs: ~60k mi turbo engines; N63 plugs/coils often needed earlier due to oil fouling (per class-action documentation). Brake fluid every 2 years. Coolant: long-life, inspect; no fixed interval. Timing: chains on all; N20 and N63 chains are known wear items (see Known issues).

performance.label: Performance
performance.text: 0–60: 528i ~6.2 s; 535i ~5.5 s; 550i ~4.5 s; G30 540i xDrive ~4.4 s (manufacturer claim); M550i 3.6 s (BMW claim for 523-hp LCI; early LCI press cars tested slower until the software fix); G60 540i xDrive ~4.4 s; i5 M60 ~3.7 s. Performance trims: M Sport packages; M550i is the factory M Performance model; M5 (F90/G90) sits above this data set.

mods.label: Modifications
mods.text: F10 550i: downpipes + tune (N63 responds strongly but heat/reliability suffer — the community warns about tuned N63s). G30 540i: MHD/Bootmod3 Stage 1 (~+40 hp), downpipe, charge pipe, heat exchanger; M550i: intakes/downpipes. Suspension: KW/ST coilovers, H&R springs (ST XA kits cataloged for G30 RWD). Wheels: F10 5x120; G30/G60 5x112. Factory performance variants: M550i, M5, Alpina B5 (not US).

engineering.label: Engineering
engineering.text: F10 (2011–2016) introduced turbocharging across the US range and electric power steering. G30 (2017–2023) moved to CLAR, cutting ~220 lb with aluminum/magnesium body structure, added gesture control/iDrive 6-7 and the 8HP51. The 2021 LCI brought 48V mild-hybrid to the 540i and the N63TU3 with 523 hp. G60 (2024+) shares CLAR with the i5 EV on one line, uses 48V across gas engines, iDrive 8.5 curved display, and no longer offers a V8 in the standard range (M5 G90 is a PHEV V8).

enthusiast.label: Enthusiast
enthusiast.text: The G30 540i is the sweet spot in the community — B58 tuning headroom in a stealthy package; M550i (2020+, 523 hp) is loved as a cut-price M5. F10 550i has a poor reputation ("the N63 tax") and cheap examples scare experienced owners. F10 535d has a small cult following for torque/MPG.

comparison.label: Comparison
comparison.text: Rivals: Mercedes E-Class (W212/W213), Audi A6 (C7/C8), Lexus GS/ES, Genesis G80. The G30 is generally considered the best-driving of the Germans; on reliability the Lexus outclasses it, and the W213 E-Class is comparable once past its own M274 four-cylinder issues. The F10 550i is one of the riskiest used buys in the segment because of the N63.

engine.label: Engine
engine.text: F25 (2015–2017): sDrive28i/xDrive28i — N20B20 2.0L turbo I4; xDrive28d — N47D20 2.0L turbo-diesel I4; xDrive35i — N55B30 3.0L turbo I6. G01 (2018–2024): sDrive30i/xDrive30i — B48B20 2.0L turbo I4; xDrive30e — B48 + e-motor PHEV (2020–2024); M40i — B58B30 3.0L turbo I6 (48V added 2022); X3 M/X3 M Competition — S58B30 3.0L twin-turbo I6 (2020–2024). G45 (2025–2026): 30 xDrive — B48 2.0L turbo + 48V; M50 xDrive — B58 3.0L turbo + 48V. All chain-driven.

transmission.label: Transmission
transmission.text: All gens: ZF 8HP 8-speed Steptronic automatic (GA8HP45Z on F25 4-cyl; 8HP51 on G01/G45; 8HP76 on X3 M). No manual in the US on any X3.

horsepower.label: Horsepower
horsepower.text: F25: 28i 240 hp; 28d 180 hp; 35i 300 hp. G01: 30i 248 hp; 30e 288 hp combined; M40i 355 hp (2018–2019), 382 hp (2020–2024); X3 M 473 hp / Competition 503 hp. G45: 30 xDrive 255 hp; M50 xDrive 393 hp.

torque.label: Torque
torque.text: F25: 28i 255 lb-ft; 28d 280 lb-ft; 35i 300 lb-ft. G01: 30i 258 lb-ft; 30e 310 lb-ft combined; M40i 365 lb-ft (2018–19), 369 lb-ft (2020–24); X3 M 442 lb-ft / Comp 479 lb-ft. G45: 30 xDrive 295 lb-ft; M50 xDrive 428 lb-ft.

drivetrain.label: Drivetrain
drivetrain.text: F25: sDrive28i RWD; all others xDrive AWD. G01: sDrive30i RWD (2019+); xDrive30i/30e/M40i/X3 M all AWD. G45: xDrive AWD standard on both trims.

dimensions.label: Dimensions
dimensions.text: F25: 183.3 L x 74.1 W x 65.4 H in, WB 110.6 in; curb ~3,950–4,235 lb. G01: 185.9 L x 74.4 W x 66.0 H in, WB 112.8 in; curb ~4,150–4,390 lb (M40i heavier). G45: ~187.2 L x 75.6 W x 65.4 H in, WB 112.8 in; curb ~4,176 lb (30 xDrive) to ~4,535 lb (M50) per BMW dealer spec sheets.

reliability.label: Reliability
reliability.text: F25: average; the N20 timing-chain issue (see Known issues) taints 28i cars through the Feb 2015 production cutoff — the 2015–2017 in-scope cars are mostly post-fix, but verify chain history. 2016 X3: 5 NHTSA recalls. G01: improved markedly after the 2018–2019 launch years (2019 X3: 10 NHTSA recalls — early-build steering/brake/electrical campaigns); 2021+ cars are considered the pick of the range. G45: 8 recall campaigns already on file for 2025 (mostly software) — normal for a launch year, but too new to judge. CR has rated recent G01 years average-to-above-average.

knownIssues.label: Known issues
knownIssues.text: F25: (1) N20 timing chain/oil-pump chain (SIB B11 03 17 + class settlement — same as 3 Series). (2) xDrive28d: SCR/DEF system faults — urea quality, metering, and "no-start countdown" warnings are heavily documented (fault codes 2C4E00, 4746–474C, 4D08); DPF/EGR service needs. (3) Transfer case actuator gear wear (xDrive). (4) Electric water pump failures. G01: (1) B48/B58 coolant loss — expansion tank, water pump, heat-management module (flagged as a top weak point in used-buyer guides). (2) Oil filter housing gasket leaks. (3) Early-build recalls (2018–2019) for steering and brake components. (4) 48V battery issues on 2022+ M40i. G45: no pattern yet — too new.

maintenance.label: Maintenance
maintenance.text: Same BMW CBS framework as 3/5 Series: oil ~10k mi/12 mo max; 8HP "lifetime" fluid per BMW — ZF-recommended service ~40k–60k mi; spark plugs ~60k mi; brake fluid every 2 years; coolant long-life/inspect; xDrive transfer-case fluid service ~60k mi is prudent (wear documented on F25). All engines chain-driven — no belts. Diesel (28d): DEF refills per CBS; DPF/SCR system inspection.

performance.label: Performance
performance.text: 0–60: F25 28i ~6.2 s; 35i ~5.5 s; 28d ~7.8 s. G01 xDrive30i ~6.3 s (tested); M40i 4.4 s (tested, 1/4 mi 13.0); X3 M Competition ~3.3 s. G45: 30 xDrive ~6.0 s; M50 xDrive 4.4 s (manufacturer). Performance trims: M Sport packages; M40i/M50 are factory M Performance models with M Sport brakes/diff; X3 M sits above.

mods.label: Modifications
mods.text: G01 M40i is a tuner favorite: B58 downpipes, MHD/Bootmod3 tunes, heat exchangers (CSF cataloged), charge pipes. 30i: B48 tunes (+~30 hp), intake/exhaust. Suspension: H&R springs, Bilstein/KW kits, X3 M sway/brake parts swap onto M40i. Diesel 28d: aftermarket DDE tunes exist (+50 hp claimed) — note SCR/DPF deletes are not emissions-legal and tuners restrict them by state. Wheels: F25 5x120; G01/G45 5x112.

engineering.label: Engineering
engineering.text: F25 shared its platform with the F30 3 Series (longitudinal engines, near-50:50 weight distribution). G01 moved to CLAR — lighter, stiffer, with the B48/B58 modular engines and standard 8HP51; 2022 LCI added 48V to the M40i and refreshed iDrive 7. G45 (2025) is a new CLAR evolution with iDrive 9, standard 48V on both engines, a physically larger body, and notably better EPA numbers (up to ~5 mpg combined better than G01).

enthusiast.label: Enthusiast
enthusiast.text: The G01 M40i is one of the most respected performance SUVs in the community — "the X3 that handles like a 3 Series" — and tunes well on the B58. The X3 M is a track-capable outlier. F25s are valued as simple, hydraulic-steering-era BMWs but the 28d's emissions hardware frustrates owners.

comparison.label: Comparison
comparison.text: Rivals: Mercedes GLC, Audi Q5, Volvo XC60, Lexus NX/RX, Acura RDX, Genesis GV70. The X3 is consistently the driver's pick of the class; the GV70 undercuts it on value/warranty and the Lexus on long-term reliability. Vs. GLC/Q5, the G01's B48/B58 powertrains are at least as durable; recurring shop work is cooling system, 8HP service, and transfer case.

engine.label: Engine
engine.text: F15 (2015–2018): sDrive35i/xDrive35i — N55B30 3.0L turbo I6; xDrive35d — N57D30 3.0L turbo-diesel I6 (through 2018, confirmed in fueleconomy.gov model lists); xDrive50i — N63B44TU 4.4L twin-turbo V8; xDrive40e — N20 2.0L turbo + e-motor PHEV (2016–2018). G05 (2019–2026): sDrive40i/xDrive40i — B58B30 3.0L turbo I6; xDrive50i — N63B44TU2 4.4L TT V8 (2019–2020); M50i — N63B44TU3 (2020–2023); xDrive45e — B58 + e-motor PHEV (2021–2023); LCI (2024–2026): xDrive40i — B58 + 48V; xDrive50e — B58-based PHEV; M60i — S68B44 4.4L TT V8 + 48V; X5 M (F95) — S63/S68 4.4L TT V8. All chain-driven.

transmission.label: Transmission
transmission.text: ZF 8HP 8-speed Steptronic automatic throughout (GA8HP45Z on sixes, GA8HP70/75 on V8/diesel in F15; 8HP51/8HP76 in G05; 8HP60 in LCI). PHEVs use the same 8HP with an integrated e-machine. No manual.

horsepower.label: Horsepower
horsepower.text: F15: 35i 300 hp; 35d 255 hp; 50i 445 hp; 40e 308 hp combined. G05: 40i 335 hp (2019–2023); 50i 456 hp; M50i 523 hp; 45e 389 hp combined; LCI: 40i 375 hp @ 5,200–6,250; 50e 483 hp combined; M60i 523 hp; X5 M Competition 617 hp.

torque.label: Torque
torque.text: F15: 35i 300 lb-ft; 35d 413 lb-ft; 50i 480 lb-ft; 40e 332 lb-ft combined. G05: 40i 330 lb-ft (2019–2023); 50i 479 lb-ft; M50i 553 lb-ft; 45e 443 lb-ft combined; LCI: 40i 383 lb-ft (up to 398 lb-ft with 48V boost) @ 1,850–5,000; 50e 516 lb-ft; M60i 553 lb-ft.

drivetrain.label: Drivetrain
drivetrain.text: F15: sDrive35i RWD; all others xDrive AWD. G05: sDrive40i RWD (from 2021); everything else xDrive AWD.

mpg.label: Fuel economy (MPG)
mpg.text: (EPA, fueleconomy.gov) 2016 xDrive35i: 18/24/20. 2016 xDrive35d: 23/29/25. 2016 xDrive50i: 15/21/17. 2016 xDrive40e: 23/25/24 gasoline, 59 MPGe combined. 2020 xDrive40i: 20/26/22. 2020 xDrive50i: 16/22/18. 2022 xDrive45e: 19/22/20 gasoline, 63 MPGe. 2025 xDrive40i (LCI, 48V): 23/27/25. 2025 xDrive50e: 22/23/22 gasoline, 58 MPGe, ~40 mi EV range (EPA, per Edmunds).

reliability.label: Reliability
reliability.text: F15: engine-dependent — N55 35i and 35d are the durable picks; the N63 50i carries the same oil-consumption/timing-chain baggage as the 550i (class actions below). 2016 X5: 5 NHTSA recalls. G05: Consumer Reports rates recent G05 years above average for the class; 2020 X5 had 11 NHTSA recalls (launch-year electrical/software campaigns), 2024 X5: 8. The 45e/50e PHEV hardware has held up reasonably well. IIHS crashworthiness "Good"; TSP+ awarded in multiple years (varies by headlights — verify per year at iihs.org).

knownIssues.label: Known issues
knownIssues.text: F15: (1) N63 (50i) oil consumption/valve stem seals/timing chains — same class actions (Bang v. BMW; Isley v. BMW) and NHTSA-documented settlement campaigns as the 550i. (2) N55: oil filter housing gasket, valve cover, electric water pump. (3) 35d: SCR/DEF/EGR service needs (same diesel architecture as 535d). (4) Rear air suspension spring leaks. (5) Coolant transfer pipe in the N63 "V" (labor-intensive). G05: (1) B58 coolant loss (expansion tank, water pump, heat-management module) — the top pattern in used-buyer guides. (2) 48V MHEV battery issues (2022+). (3) Air suspension faults on equipped cars. (4) Oil leaks/sensor failures reported past ~60k mi. (5) 2020–2021 PHEV (45e) high-voltage battery recall campaign (cell debris/fire risk — NHTSA campaign on early 45e). LCI: too new for a pattern.

maintenance.label: Maintenance
maintenance.text: Oil: CBS ~10k mi/12 mo max; halve it on N63 cars. 8HP "lifetime" fluid per BMW — ZF-recommended service ~40k–60k mi; transfer case fluid ~60k mi; rear diff fluid on air-suspension cars per CBS inspection. Spark plugs ~60k mi (N63 sooner if oil-fouled). Brake fluid every 2 years. Coolant long-life/inspect. All engines chain-driven — no belts. PHEV: plus HV system checks; 45e/50e electric range degrades with battery age.

performance.label: Performance
performance.text: 0–60: F15 35i ~5.9–6.0 s (tested); 35d ~6.0 s; 50i ~4.9 s; 40e ~6.2 s. G05 40i ~5.3 s (manufacturer; C/D-tested 2021 40i ~4.8 s with launch control); M50i ~3.9 s; 45e ~5.3 s; LCI 40i ~5.2 s; 50e ~4.6 s; M60i ~3.6 s (BMW claim); X5 M Competition ~3.7 s. Performance trims: M Sport packages; M50i/M60i are factory M Performance models (M Sport diff, brakes, exhaust); X5 M above.

mods.label: Modifications
mods.text: G05 40i: B58 MHD/Bootmod3 tunes, downpipes, intakes, heat exchangers. M50i/M60i: intakes and tunes on N63TU3/S68. F15 50i tuning exists but the community steers people to the sixes for reliability. Cosmetic: wheels up to 22", 5x120 (F15) / 5x112 (G05) bolt patterns; carbon mirror caps/spoilers widely cataloged (RW Carbon etc.). Factory performance variants: M50i, M60i, X5 M.

engineering.label: Engineering
engineering.text: F15 (2014–2018) kept BMW's longitudinal layout with optional third row and introduced the 40e PHEV and aluminum-intensive bodywork. G05 (2019+) moved to CLAR — shared with G30/G20 — adding standard air-suspension options, off-road packages, and the 8HP51; the 2024 LCI brought 48V across the range, the new 375-hp B58TU2, the 483-hp 50e (25.7 kWh battery, e-motor integrated in the gearbox), the S68 V8 in the M60i, and the iDrive 8 curved display. Built in Spartanburg, SC.

enthusiast.label: Enthusiast
enthusiast.text: The G05 40i is considered one of the best all-around SUVs BMW has ever made; the M50i/M60i have a strong following as "M5 with seats up." F15 35d has a diesel-torque cult; F15 50i trades cheap because of the N63 reputation. X5 M (F95) is the track-party variant with real tuner support.

comparison.label: Comparison
comparison.text: Rivals: Mercedes GLE, Audi Q7, Porsche Cayenne, Volvo XC90, Lexus RX/GX, Genesis GV80. The G05 is routinely ranked at or near the top of the class for driving dynamics and powertrain refinement; the Cayenne is sharper at much higher cost, the Lexus GX/RX more durable but less athletic. On reliability the G05 sixes beat the GLE's early M256 cars; avoid F15 50i unless documented chain/seal work is done.

bmw-x1.subtitle: Subcompact luxury SUV, 2016–2022 (F48), 2023–2026 (U11). No 2015 US model — the previous RWD-based E84 ended in 2015; F48 arrived as a 2016 model.

engine.label: Engine
engine.text: F48 (2016–2022): sDrive28i/xDrive28i — B46B20 2.0L turbo I4 (B46 is the US SULEV variant of the B48), transverse mounted. U11 (2023–2026): xDrive28i — B48B20 2.0L turbo I4; M35i xDrive (2024–2026) — B48B20 high-output 2.0L turbo I4. Both chain-driven, direct injection, twin-scroll turbo.

transmission.label: Transmission
transmission.text: F48: Aisin-sourced 8-speed automatic (GA8F22AW / AWF8F35 family — required by the transverse layout; NOT the ZF 8HP used in longitudinal BMWs). U11: 7-speed dual-clutch automatic (Getrag 7DCT300 family, "Steptronic" DCT). No manual on either generation.

horsepower.label: Horsepower
horsepower.text: F48 28i: 228 hp. U11 xDrive28i: 241 hp; M35i xDrive: 312 hp.

torque.label: Torque
torque.text: F48 28i: 258 lb-ft. U11 xDrive28i: 295 lb-ft; M35i xDrive: 295 lb-ft.

drivetrain.label: Drivetrain
drivetrain.text: F48: sDrive28i FWD (front-biased UKL platform) or xDrive28i AWD (on-demand, front-biased). U11: xDrive AWD standard on both trims; no FWD US model.

dimensions.label: Dimensions
dimensions.text: F48: 175.4 L x 71.7 W x 62.9 H in, WB 105.1 in; curb ~3,560 lb (sDrive) to ~3,660 lb (xDrive). U11: ~177.2 L x 72.6 W x 64.2 H in (43 mm longer, 24 mm wider, 44 mm taller than F48 per Driving.ca), WB ~106.0 in; curb ~3,750–3,900 lb.

mpg.label: Fuel economy (MPG)
mpg.text: (EPA, fueleconomy.gov) 2018 sDrive28i FWD: 23/32/26. 2018 xDrive28i: 22/31/25. 2023 xDrive28i (U11): 25/34/28. 2024 M35i xDrive: 23/31/26.

reliability.label: Reliability
reliability.text: The F48 is one of BMW's better reliability stories of its era — the B46/B48 four-cylinder avoided the N20's timing-chain defect, and Consumer Reports rated most F48 years average or better. NHTSA recalls: 2018 X1 — 4; 2023 X1 — 6 (launch-year software/airbag campaigns typical for U11). The Aisin gearbox has a documented (not epidemic) harsh-shift complaint pattern — see Known issues. IIHS crashworthiness "Good"; TSP status varies by year/headlights.

knownIssues.label: Known issues
knownIssues.text: F48: (1) Aisin 8-speed low-speed jerk/harsh 2–3 shift and delayed reverse engagement, worse when hot — widely reported on owner forums; fluid service and software updates are the first-line fix. (2) B46/B48 cooling system: water pump, thermostat, minor coolant leaks at plastic fittings. (3) Upper engine mount wear (documented in reliability reports). (4) xDrive transfer case clutch wear (front-biased Haldex-style system). (5) Sunroof rattles/drains. U11: too new for a pattern; launch recalls are mostly software/airbag related. 7DCT long-term behavior is not yet proven — flag for service records.

maintenance.label: Maintenance
maintenance.text: Oil: CBS, max ~10k mi/12 mo (BMW LL-01 FE / LL-17 FE+). Transmission: F48 Aisin — BMW "lifetime fill"; independent practice is fluid service ~60k mi (Aisin itself specifies fluid changes; harsh-shift complaints often respond to it). U11 DCT — fluid/filter service per BMW schedule (~60k mi shop practice). Spark plugs ~60k mi. Brake fluid every 2 years. Coolant long-life/inspect. Timing chain on both engines — no belts.

performance.label: Performance
performance.text: 0–60: F48 xDrive28i ~6.3 s; U11 xDrive28i ~6.2 s (manufacturer); M35i xDrive ~5.2 s (manufacturer). Performance trims: M Sport package on 28i; the M35i is the factory M Performance variant (M-specific chassis tuning, adaptive dampers, quad exhaust).

mods.label: Modifications
mods.text: Light scene. B46/B48 tunes (Stage 1 ≈ +30–40 hp), M35i downpipes/intakes, H&R springs, spacers; most owners stop at wheels/tires and cosmetic aero. No meaningful gearbox or AWD upgrades — the transverse Aisin/DCT hardware limits serious tuning. Factory performance variant: M35i.

engineering.label: Engineering
engineering.text: The big story is the platform switch: the F48 abandoned the E84's longitudinal RWD architecture for the transverse, front-wheel-drive-based UKL platform shared with MINI — which is why it uses an Aisin gearbox instead of the ZF 8HP and why the engine sits sideways. The U11 (2023) is an evolution (UKL2) with a stiffer body, the curved iDrive 9 display, a switch to a 7-speed DCT, and standard AWD in the US. Near-50:50 balance of the old car is gone, but cabin space and efficiency improved significantly.

enthusiast.label: Enthusiast
enthusiast.text: Purists dismiss the F48/U11 as "not a real BMW" because of the FWD platform; owners counter that it drives better than anything else in the class. The M35i gets genuine respect as a hot-hatch alternative. Tuner support is thin compared with the longitudinal cars — this is a practical daily, not a build platform.

comparison.label: Comparison
comparison.text: Rivals: Mercedes GLA/GLB, Audi Q3, Volvo XC40, Acura ADX, Lexus UX, Cadillac XT4. The X1 is routinely ranked the driver's pick of the subcompact class, and the B46/B48 powertrain is more durable than early GLA/Q3 fours. It loses on interior richness to the GLA and on long-term cost to the Lexus UX. Shop-relevant: the Aisin/DCT services and cooling-system plastics are the recurring tickets.

bmw-m3.subtitle: High-performance compact sport sedan, 2015–2018 (F80), 2021–2026 (G80, LCI from 2025). No 2019–2020 US M3 — the model skipped two model years between generations.

engine.label: Engine
engine.text: F80 (2015–2018): S55B30 3.0L twin-turbo I6 (two mono-scroll turbos, closed-deck block, ~7,300 rpm redline). G80 (2021–2026): S58B30 3.0L twin-turbo I6 (successor to S55 — revised crank drive/hub, stronger bottom end). Variants: base M3, M3 Competition, M3 Competition xDrive, M3 CS (limited, 2024). Chain-driven valvetrain, direct injection.

transmission.label: Transmission
transmission.text: F80: 6-speed manual or 7-speed M DCT dual-clutch (Drivelogic). G80: 6-speed manual (base M3 only, RWD) or ZF 8HP76-based 8-speed M Steptronic automatic with Drivelogic (Competition/xDrive; DCT dropped).

horsepower.label: Horsepower
horsepower.text: F80: 425 hp; 444 hp with Competition Package (2016+). G80: M3 473 hp; M3 Competition 503 hp; M3 Competition xDrive 503 hp (2022–2024), 523 hp @ 6,250 rpm (2025+ LCI); M3 CS 543 hp.

torque.label: Torque
torque.text: F80: 406 lb-ft (both base and Competition Package). G80: M3 406 lb-ft; Competition/xDrive 479 lb-ft @ 2,750–5,730 rpm (2025); M3 CS 479 lb-ft.

drivetrain.label: Drivetrain
drivetrain.text: F80: RWD only. G80: RWD (base + Competition) or M xDrive AWD with 2WD mode (Competition xDrive, 2022+; CS).

dimensions.label: Dimensions
dimensions.text: F80: 183.9 L x 73.9 W x 56.1 H in, WB 110.7 in; curb ~3,590 lb. G80: 189.1 L x 74.3 W x 56.4 H in, WB 112.5 in; curb ~3,840 lb (base manual), 3,891 lb (Competition RWD), 3,990 lb (Competition xDrive).

mpg.label: Fuel economy (MPG)
mpg.text: (EPA, fueleconomy.gov) 2016 M3 DCT: 17/24/19; manual: 17/26/20. 2021 M3 manual (G80): 16/23/19. 2023 M3 Competition xDrive: 16/22/18.

reliability.label: Reliability
reliability.text: Better than their reputation if maintained, with one big asterisk on the F80: the S55 crank-hub issue (below). NHTSA recalls: 2016 M3 — 3; 2021 M3 — 3 (both modest counts; campaigns largely minor). The S58 in the G80 has so far proven substantially more robust than the S55 — no systemic engine failure pattern documented through 2025. These are track-capable cars: condition and abuse history matter more than odometer.

knownIssues.label: Known issues
knownIssues.text: F80/S55: (1) Crank hub failure — the single-bolt crank hub can spin under shock load or harmonics, skipping valve timing and bending valves; BMW extended warranty coverage on early cars, and the aftermarket fix (one-piece/pinned crank hub, ~$1,500 parts/labor preventive) is standard practice. (2) Turbo wastegate rattle. (3) Charge-pipe/boost-leak failures under stock and tuned boost. (4) Oil filter housing gasket leaks. G80/S58: no systemic pattern through 2025; scattered oil filter housing seepage and early-build software recalls; S58 uses a redesigned crank drive — the F80 hub issue is not considered applicable. Track-driven cars of both gens: check rod-bearing condition history, brake wear, and differential fluid.

maintenance.label: Maintenance
maintenance.text: Oil: CBS ~10k mi max, but 5k–7.5k mi is the accepted interval for these engines (S55/S58 run hot and shear oil; track use = shorter). Transmission: DCT fluid/filter ~60k mi (F80); M Steptronic 8HP "lifetime" per BMW — ZF/shop practice ~40k–60k mi. Spark plugs ~40k–60k mi (shorter than standard BMWs; more often on tuned/track cars). Brake fluid every 2 years (annually for track use). Coolant long-life/inspect. Differential fluid ~30k–60k mi for tracked cars. Chain-driven — no belts.

performance.label: Performance
performance.text: 0–60: F80 3.8 s (DCT) / 4.1 s (manual) per instrumented tests. G80: base manual ~4.1 s; Competition RWD 3.6 s; Competition xDrive ~3.0 s (2025 spec; C/D tested earlier xDrive cars in the 2.8–3.0 s range); CS ~3.2 s. Top speed 155 mph (180 mph with M Driver's Package). Performance trims are the model itself; M3 CS is the limited factory flagship (543 hp, CFRP aero, track alignment).

mods.label: Modifications
mods.text: One of the strongest tuning ecosystems in the industry. S55: Stage 1–2 flash tunes (MHD/Bootmod3, ~480–530 hp), downpipes, charge pipes, intercoolers, and the preventive crank-hub upgrade — effectively mandatory on tuned cars. S58: Stage 1 ~570+ hp, downpipes, intakes, heat exchangers (CSF). Chassis: KW/H&R/Bilstein suspension, camber plates, AP/StopTech brake kits, Michelin Cup 2 rubber. Wheels: F80 5x120, G80 5x112.

engineering.label: Engineering
engineering.text: F80 was the first turbocharged M3, replacing the E90/E92's 4.0L V8 with the S55 twin-turbo six — closed-deck block, forged crank, magnesium oil sump, active M differential, carbon-fiber driveshaft and roof. G80 moved to the CLAR-based S58 with 3D-printed head cores, 350-bar injection, a redesigned crank drive, and — for the first time in M3 history — available AWD (M xDrive with a rear-drive mode) plus the switch from DCT to a torque-converter 8-speed. The controversial vertical grille houses the cooling pack the S58 demands.

enthusiast.label: Enthusiast
enthusiast.text: The F80 manual is already collectible (last of the small, RWD-only, hydraulic-feel M3s — 16,057 US units), and clean examples hold value. The G80 won skeptics over with pace and adjustability — the xDrive cars are the default fast choice, the manual base car the purist's. Crank-hub status is the first question in any F80 sale thread.

comparison.label: Comparison
comparison.text: Rivals: Mercedes-AMG C63 (W205 V8 through this era), Alfa Romeo Giulia Quadrifoglio, Audi RS 5/RS 4, Cadillac CT4-V Blackwing, Tesla Model 3 Performance (straight-line only). The M3 is the segment benchmark for balance and track durability; the Alfa is more charismatic but less reliable, the W205 C63 sounds better but eats rear tires and head bolts (M177 early cars), and the Blackwing out-steers everything but is rarer than honest politicians.

bmw-i4.subtitle: Battery-electric Gran Coupe (liftback), 2022–2026 (G26). First US model year 2022 — no earlier generation. BEV adaptation: "Engine" = drive units; "Transmission" = single-speed; MPG = EPA MPGe.

engine.label: Engine
engine.text: (Drive units — no combustion engine) G26, BMW Gen5 eDrive, 400V architecture: eDrive35 — single rear permanently excited synchronous motor, 66.0 kWh usable / 68.7 kWh battery (2023+); eDrive40 — single rear motor, 81.5 kWh usable / 84.3 kWh battery; xDrive40 — dual motor AWD, same 84.3 kWh pack (2024+); M50 xDrive — dual motor AWD performance tune, 84.3 kWh. Pack: NCM-811 prismatic cells (CATL/Samsung SDI), liquid-cooled, 8 yr/100k mi warranty with 70% capacity floor. Max DC fast charge ~200 kW.

transmission.label: Transmission
transmission.text: Single-speed fixed-ratio reduction drive (front and/or rear axle, per trim). No multi-gear transmission, no clutch, no torque converter.

horsepower.label: Horsepower
horsepower.text: eDrive35 282 hp; eDrive40 335 hp; xDrive40 396 hp; M50 xDrive 536 hp (Sport Boost mode; ~469 hp continuous).

torque.label: Torque
torque.text: eDrive35 295 lb-ft; eDrive40 317 lb-ft; xDrive40 443 lb-ft; M50 xDrive 586 lb-ft (Sport Boost).

drivetrain.label: Drivetrain
drivetrain.text: eDrive35/eDrive40: RWD (rear motor). xDrive40/M50: dual-motor AWD with rear bias.

dimensions.label: Dimensions
dimensions.text: G26: 188.5 L x 72.9 W x 57.0 H in, WB 112.4 in; curb ~4,553 lb (eDrive40) to ~5,018 lb (M50). Liftback body — no front trunk.

mpg.label: Fuel economy (MPG)
mpg.text: (EPA MPGe city/hwy/combined + range, fueleconomy.gov) 2023 eDrive35 (18"): 115/111/113 MPGe, 256 mi range. 2022/2024 eDrive40 (18"): 109/108/109 MPGe, 301 mi range. 2024 xDrive40 (18"): 107/111/109 MPGe, 307 mi range. 2022/2024 M50 (19"): 94/98/95–96 MPGe, 269–270 mi range. Larger wheels cut range ~5–10%.

reliability.label: Reliability
reliability.text: Mechanically simple powertrain, but the Gen5 high-voltage system has a documented recall history (below). NHTSA recalls: 2022 i4 — 8; 2023 i4 — 7 (nearly all software/electrical campaigns, several HV-battery related; many remedied OTA). Consumer Reports has rated the i4 around average; owner complaints cluster on charging/software, not motors. IIHS: shares 4 Series Gran Coupe structure — "Good" crashworthiness; verify TSP status per year.

knownIssues.label: Known issues
knownIssues.text: (1) NHTSA recall 22V-541 (2022–2023 i4/iX, 367 units): traction-battery internal damage, fire risk — owners told to park outside and stop driving; packs replaced under warranty. (2) NHTSA 22V-944 (2022–2023, 14,086 units): HV battery ECU software can interrupt drive power — software fix. (3) NHTSA 25V-395 (2022–2025 i4/iX/i7/i5): electric drive motor software may shut down the HV system causing loss of drive — OTA/dealer software remedy. (4) DC fast-charge taper/heat-soak after sustained high-speed running. (5) 12V battery and iDrive software glitches typical of the era. No motor or reduction-drive failure pattern documented through 2025.

maintenance.label: Maintenance
maintenance.text: No engine oil, spark plugs, timing components, fuel system, or multi-gear transmission service. Schedule highlights: brake fluid every 2 years (regen braking means pads/rotors last long but corrode from disuse — inspect annually); cabin microfilter per CBS; reduction-gear oil is a "lifetime" fill (inspect at HV service); battery coolant circuit — level/condition inspection at service; tire rotation ~10k mi (heavy EV, high torque — rear tires wear fast on RWD trims); brake caliper slide lubrication recommended in rust-belt states. HV battery warranty 8 yr/100k mi, 70% capacity floor — warranty decisions require dealer capacity test.

performance.label: Performance
performance.text: 0–60: eDrive35 ~5.8 s; eDrive40 ~5.5 s; xDrive40 ~4.9 s; M50 xDrive ~3.7 s (manufacturer claims; independent tests of the M50 match ~3.7). Top speed 118–130 mph trim-dependent. M50 is the factory M Performance variant: M-specific chassis tuning, adaptive dampers, variable steering, launch control with Sport Boost.

mods.label: Modifications
mods.text: Minimal powertrain modification possible — drive-unit software is locked; there are no mainstream power tunes for the i4 as of 2026. Scene is wheels/tires (5x112), lowering springs, aero/cosmetic parts shared with the G26 4 Series, and brake upgrades for M50 track use. Range "mods" are wheel/tire choices, not software.

engineering.label: Engineering
engineering.text: The i4 is a CLAR-platform conversion of the 4 Series Gran Coupe rather than a clean-sheet EV — the battery sits in the floor/T-shaped tunnel area of a body designed for combustion, which preserves the familiar cabin but costs it a frunk and some packaging efficiency. Gen5 eDrive motors use no rare-earth magnets (electrically excited synchronous design on the rear unit). 400V architecture with ~200 kW DC peak; 11 kW AC onboard charger. The 2025 facelift brought iDrive 8.5 and revised lighting.

enthusiast.label: Enthusiast
enthusiast.text: Reception is "the normal person's BMW EV" — praised for driving like a 4 Series rather than a science project; the M50 gets genuine respect as an M3-adjacent daily. Skeptics cite weight (5,000 lb) and the conversion-platform compromises. Minimal tuner culture; the community treats it as an appliance-with-talent, not a build platform.

comparison.label: Comparison
comparison.text: Rivals: Tesla Model 3, Polestar 2, Hyundai Ioniq 6, Genesis GV60, Mercedes EQE sedan. The i4 beats the Model 3 on build quality and ride, loses on charging network and efficiency-per-dollar; the Polestar 2 is its closest character match. On reliability the recall-heavy Gen5 HV software story is the main caveat — verify all campaigns closed before delivery. Shop-relevant: work is brakes, tires, suspension, and 12V/HV diagnostics — no engine work exists.

chevrolet-silverado.subtitle: Full-size half-ton pickup, 2015–2018 (3rd gen, K2XX platform), 2019–2026 (4th gen, T1XX platform; mid-cycle refresh for 2022.5)

engine.label: Engine
engine.text: K2XX (2015–2018): 4.3L LV3 EcoTec3 V6, NA, DI, AFM (285 hp @ 5,300 rpm / 305 lb-ft @ 3,900 rpm); 5.3L L83 EcoTec3 V8, NA, DI, AFM (355 hp @ 5,600 / 383 lb-ft @ 4,100); 5.3L L8B eAssist mild-hybrid V8 (355 hp, 2016–2018, limited markets); 6.2L L86 EcoTec3 V8, NA, DI, AFM (420 hp / 460 lb-ft). T1XX (2019–2026): 4.3L LV3 V6 (285/305, 2019–2021); 2.7L L3B "TurboMax" turbo I4 (310 hp @ 5,600; 348 lb-ft 2019–2021, raised to 430 lb-ft @ 1,500 from 2022); 5.3L L82 V8 (AFM, fleet/WT, 2019–2021, 355/383); 5.3L L84 V8 (Dynamic Fuel Management, 355/383); 6.2L L87 V8 (DFM, 420/460); 3.0L LM2 Duramax turbo-diesel I6 (277 hp @ 3,750 / 460 lb-ft @ 1,500, 2020–2022); 3.0L LZ0 Duramax I6 (305 hp / 495 lb-ft, 2023–2026).

transmission.label: Transmission
transmission.text: K2XX: Hydra-Matic 6L80 6-speed automatic (4.3L/5.3L); 8L90 8-speed automatic (standard with 6.2L from 2015, optional on select 5.3L trims 2015–2018, and eAssist). T1XX: 6L80 6-speed (4.3L, 2019–2021); 8-speed automatic (RPO MFC, with 2.7L); Hydra-Matic 10L80 10-speed automatic (RPO MHS/MQC, with 5.3L, 6.2L, and both diesels).

drivetrain.label: Drivetrain
drivetrain.text: RWD standard, 4WD (part-time, AutoTrac auto 4WD on upper trims) available on all configurations both generations. Z71 off-road package (both gens); Custom Trail Boss / LT Trail Boss (2019+); ZR2 / ZR2 Bison (2022+) with front/rear locking differentials. 6.2L is 4WD-only in most applications.

dimensions.label: Dimensions
dimensions.text: K2XX: length 205.6–239.6 in (cab/bed dependent); wheelbase 119.0–153.5 in; width 80.0 in; height 73.5–74.2 in; curb weight 4,515–5,461 lbs. T1XX: length 210.9–241.4 in; wheelbase 126.5–157.0 in (139.6–157.0 on crew/double cabs 2019–2021); width 81.1–81.2 in; height 75.4–76.7 in; curb weight 4,257–5,620 lbs.

reliability.label: Reliability
reliability.text: K2XX: mechanically durable 6L80/LV3/L83 core, but the generation is defined by AFM valvetrain failures and 8L90 complaints (see Known issues). T1XX: poor early record — NHTSA has logged ~3,414 owner complaints and 31 recall campaigns across 2019–2025; the 5.3L L84 alone accounts for 1,719 complaints (transmission 559, engine 498, electrical 378), and the 6.2L L87 is under recall plus two federal investigations (The Weekly Driver / NHTSA data). The 3.0L diesels carry far lower complaint volume. Consumer Reports / J.D. Power scores: Not verified — needs OEM source.

knownIssues.label: Known issues
knownIssues.text: K2XX: AFM lifter collapse and related oil consumption on 5.3L L83/6.2L L86 (peak complaint years 2015–2017; GM TSB 18-NA-355); 8L90 torque-converter shudder and harsh 1-2/2-3 shifts — subject of multiple class actions (Francis v. GM, Speerly v. GM, Ulrich v. GM; class certified in part), with TSB fluid-flush remedies ( Mobil 1 Synthetic LV ATF HP ); A/C condenser/refrigerant line failures; exhaust manifold bolt breakage on V8s. T1XX: DFM lifter failures on L84/L87 (same valvetrain family, updated design, failures continue); L87 connecting-rod/crankshaft defect — NHTSA recall 25V-274 (April 2025, ~598,000 US vehicles, 2021–2024, engines built 3/1/2021–5/31/2024), plus NHTSA Recall Query RQ26001 (Jan 2026, failures after the recall fix) and Engineering Analysis EA25007 (Oct 2025, ~286,000 additional 2019–2021 vehicles); 10L80 valve-body/harsh-shift complaints; 2.7L L3B early turbo/PCV complaints; LM2 long-crank (fuel-system) complaints.

maintenance.label: Maintenance
maintenance.text: Oil per Oil Life Monitoring system; dexos1 Gen 2 (later Gen 3) — 5W-30 for 4.3/5.3/6.2 gas V8s (L87 recall remedy updated fill spec to Mobil 1 Supercar 0W-40 on inspected engines), 0W-20 for 2.7L L3B; dexosD 0W-20 for 3.0L diesels. Spark plugs: iridium, ~97,500 mi. Coolant: Dex-Cool OAT, 5 yr/150,000 mi. Transmission fluid: 6L80/8L90/10L80 — GM "fill for life" under normal service; 45,000-mi severe-service interval (towing duty makes these trucks severe-service by definition; 8L90 shudder TSB mandates a specific fluid exchange). Transfer case and front/rear axle fluids per severe-service schedule (50,000-mi range). Brake fluid: no fixed interval in GM schedule — inspect annually. All engines are timing-chain; no belts.

performance.label: Performance
performance.text: 0–60: 2.7L Turbo — 6.8 sec (GM's own 2020 brochure claim); other powertrains Not verified — needs OEM source (period instrumented tests put 6.2L crew cabs in the mid-5s). Performance trims: Z71 (both gens), Trail Boss (2019+, 2-in factory lift), ZR2/ZR2 Bison (2022+, Multimatic DSSV dampers, front/rear lockers), 6.2L optional on RST/LTZ/High Country. Max trailering up to 13,300 lbs (6.2L/diesel, Max Trailering Pkg).

mods.label: Modifications
mods.text: One of the most-modified vehicles in North America: ECM/TCM tunes (Black Bear, HP Tuners — typical +15–30 hp on 5.3/6.2), AFM/DFM disablers and full AFM-delete cam/lifter kits (a reliability mod as much as a performance one), cold-air intakes, cat-back exhausts (incl. GM's own Sport Exhaust Calibration, RPO RWQ, 2025+), leveling/lift kits and 33–35-in tires on Z71/Trail Boss/ZR2. Aftermarket superchargers (Whipple, Magnuson, ProCharger) are common on 5.3/6.2.

engineering.label: Engineering
engineering.text: K2XX moved GM's full-size trucks to the Gen V EcoTec3 small-block family — direct injection, aluminum blocks, and Active Fuel Management across the line, with a fully boxed high-strength-steel frame. T1XX (2019) is a clean-sheet architecture ~450 lbs lighter in crew-cab form via mixed materials (aluminum hood/doors/tailgate over a steel cab and bed), added the 2.7L turbo four and 3.0L Duramax inline-six diesel, replaced AFM with Dynamic Fuel Management (17 cylinder-deactivation patterns), and adopted the 10L80 10-speed co-developed with Ford. 2022.5 brought a revised interior with Google-based infotainment and the ZR2 off-road flagship.

enthusiast.label: Enthusiast
enthusiast.text: The truck scene treats the 6.2L (L86/L87) as the one to have and the 6L80-era trucks as the safe used buy; AFM/DFM lifter failure is the dominant forum topic and drives a huge delete-kit industry. The ZR2 has genuine off-road credibility (DSSV dampers shared with the Colorado ZR2 lineage). Diesel LM2/LZ0 trucks have a growing following for 30-mpg highway road-trip use. Trail Boss is the volume "factory mod" trim.

comparison.label: Comparison
comparison.text: Direct rivals: Ford F-150, Ram 1500, Toyota Tundra, GMC Sierra (mechanical twin). Silverado typically undercuts F-150 on price with comparable capability, but 2019+ reliability (DFM lifters, L87 recall) trails the 5.0L Coyote F-150 and the Tundra in current complaint data; the K2XX 5.3/6L80 combo is regarded as one of the better-value used half-tons. Driving feel is traditional-truck — solid axle, body-on-frame — vs. the Ram 1500's coil-sprung ride.

chevrolet-equinox.subtitle: Compact crossover SUV, 2015–2017 (2nd gen, Theta platform), 2018–2024 (3rd gen, D2XX platform), 2025–2026 (4th gen)

engine.label: Engine
engine.text: 2nd gen (2015–2017): 2.4L LEA Ecotec I4, NA, DI (182 hp @ 6,700 rpm / 172 lb-ft @ 4,900); 3.6L LFX High-Feature V6, NA, DI (301 hp @ 6,500 / 272 lb-ft). 3rd gen (2018–2024): 1.5L LYX turbo I4 (170 hp @ 5,600 / 203 lb-ft @ 2,000–4,000); 2.0L LTG turbo I4 (252 hp @ 5,500 / 260 lb-ft, 2018–2020); 1.6L LH7 turbo-diesel I4 (137 hp / 240 lb-ft, 2018–2019). 4th gen (2025–2026): 1.5L LSD turbo I4 (175 hp @ 5,600; 184 lb-ft @ 2,000 with CVT/FWD, 203 lb-ft with 8-speed/AWD).

transmission.label: Transmission
transmission.text: 2nd gen: Hydra-Matic 6T40 (2.4L) / 6T70 (3.6L) 6-speed automatic. 3rd gen: 6T40 6-speed (1.5L and diesel), 9T50 9-speed automatic (2.0L). 4th gen: CVT (FWD) or 8-speed automatic (AWD).

horsepower.label: Horsepower
horsepower.text: 182 (LEA) / 301 (LFX) / 170 (LYX) / 252 (LTG) / 137 (LH7) / 175 (LSD). (Some overseas/internet listings quote metric-horsepower figures of 184/305/173 — SAE net US ratings are as stated.)

drivetrain.label: Drivetrain
drivetrain.text: FWD standard with optional AWD on all generations (LS 3rd-gen FWD-only in some years). 4th gen: FWD + CVT or AWD + 8-speed; ACTIV trim adds off-road-styled tuning. Towing: 1,500 lbs (1.5L/AWD 4th gen), up to 3,500 lbs (2.0L 3rd gen).

dimensions.label: Dimensions
dimensions.text: 2nd gen: 187.8 L x 72.5 W x 66.3 H in; wheelbase 112.5 in; curb 3,758–4,007 lbs. 3rd gen: 183.1 L x 72.6 W x 65.4 H in; wheelbase 107.3 in; curb 3,274–3,682 lbs. 4th gen: 183.2 L x 74.9 W x 65.6 H in; wheelbase 107.5 in; curb 3,583–3,690 lbs.

mpg.label: Fuel economy (MPG)
mpg.text: (EPA city/hwy/combined, fueleconomy.gov) 2nd gen: 2.4L FWD 21–22/31/25–26; 2.4L AWD 20/28/23; 3.6L FWD 17/24/20; 3.6L AWD 16/23/18. 3rd gen: 1.5T FWD 26/31–32/28; 1.5T AWD 24–25/30/26–27; 2.0T FWD 22/29/25; 2.0T AWD 22/28/24; 1.6L diesel FWD 28/39/32; diesel AWD 28/38/32. 4th gen: FWD CVT 26/28/27; AWD 8-spd 24/29/26.

reliability.label: Reliability
reliability.text: 2nd gen's 2.4L Ecotec is a known oil-burner (class-action level, below); the 3.6L LFX is the more durable option. 3rd gen is mid-pack for the segment; the 1.5L LYX shows a pattern of owner complaints (below) but no major recall crisis; diesel LH7 was discontinued after 2019 on low demand, not defect. 4th gen too new for meaningful data (2025+). Consumer Reports / J.D. Power / IIHS scores: Not verified — needs OEM source.

knownIssues.label: Known issues
knownIssues.text: 2nd gen: 2.4L Ecotec excessive oil consumption from stuck/worn piston rings — multiple class actions (Berman v. GM, Buchholz v. GM) with warranty extensions and settlements; dealer fix is ring/piston replacement; low-oil engine damage is the common kill mode (no low-oil warning before knock). Timing-chain wear on high-mileage 2.4s. 3rd gen: 1.5L LYX — documented pattern of cold-climate turbocharger oiling faults/check-engine lights and sensor failures (service-trade documentation); oil dilution/consumption complaints on early builds; 9T50 9-speed (2.0L) harsh-shift complaints. 4th gen: no significant pattern as of mid-2026 — monitor.

maintenance.label: Maintenance
maintenance.text: Oil per OLM; dexos1 Gen 2/Gen 3 — 5W-30 (2.4L/3.6L), 0W-20 (1.5T LYX/LSD); dexos2 for the 1.6L diesel. Oil capacity 5.3 qt (LYX, per lubricant lookup data), 5.0 qt (LEA), 6.0 qt (LFX). Spark plugs: iridium ~97,500 mi. Coolant: Dex-Cool 5 yr/150,000 mi. Transmission fluid: 6T40 uses GM AF6 fluid (total fill ~9 qt); severe-service 45,000 mi; 9T50/CVT/8-spd per severe-service schedule. Brake fluid: inspect; no fixed GM interval. All engines timing-chain (no belt).

performance.label: Performance
performance.text: 0–60: Not verified — needs OEM source (2.0T 3rd gen was the quick one, period tests ~6.5–7 sec; 1.5T models ~9 sec). No factory performance trim; RS (2022+) is appearance-only. The 1.6L diesel was the efficiency play (577-mile highway range advertised).

mods.label: Modifications
mods.text: Light scene: Trifecta and other OBD flash tunes exist for 2.4L/3.6L 2nd gen and 1.5T/2.0T 3rd gen (Stage 1 tunes for the 2.0T), drop-in filters, cat-back exhausts on the 2.0T. Mostly cosmetic mods (wheels, leveling). The 4th-gen ACTIV has spawned the usual light off-road dress-up parts.

engineering.label: Engineering
engineering.text: 2nd gen rode the Theta platform shared with the GMC Terrain — conventional transverse four/V6 with a 6-speed. The 3rd gen (2018) moved to the D2XX architecture (shared with Terrain/Envision), cutting ~400 lbs and switching to an all-turbo lineup including the only diesel in the US compact-crossover class at the time; 2022 brought a facelift with revised fascia and the RS trim. The 4th gen (2025) keeps the 1.5L turbo formula but splits drivetrains — CVT for FWD, a conventional 8-speed for AWD — and moves to an 11.3-inch Google-based cockpit; the all-electric Equinox EV is a separate Ultium-based model line.

enthusiast.label: Enthusiast
enthusiast.text: Not an enthusiast vehicle in any generation — the community is owner-DIY oriented, focused on the 2.4L oil-consumption fix and keeping the 1.5T on strict oil intervals. The 2.0T (2018–2020) has a small tuning following as the "sleeper" family crossover. Diesel 3rd-gens have a niche hypermiler following.

comparison.label: Comparison
comparison.text: Rivals: Honda CR-V, Toyota RAV4, Ford Escape, Mazda CX-5, Hyundai Tucson. The Equinox sells on price and interior room, not driving polish — the CR-V and CX-5 out-drive it, and the RAV4 (especially hybrid) beats it on reliability reputation and resale. The 2nd-gen 3.6L and 3rd-gen 2.0T are the picks for power; avoid neglected 2.4L cars.

chevrolet-malibu.subtitle: Midsize sedan, 2015 (8th gen final year, Epsilon II), 2016–2025 (9th gen, E2XX platform; production ended November 2024 — 2025 was the last model year)

engine.label: Engine
engine.text: 8th gen (2015): 2.5L LKW Ecotec I4, NA, DI (196 hp @ 6,300 rpm / 191 lb-ft @ 4,400); 2.0L LTG turbo I4 (259 hp @ 5,300 / 295 lb-ft). 9th gen (2016–2025): 1.5L LFV turbo I4 (163 hp / 184 lb-ft); 2.0L LTG turbo I4 (250 hp / 258–260 lb-ft, 2016–2022, Premier); 1.8L LKN I4 + two-motor electric drive Hybrid (182 hp combined, 2016–2019). Note: one GM database summary mislabels the LFV as naturally aspirated — it is turbocharged.

transmission.label: Transmission
transmission.text: 8th gen: Hydra-Matic 6T40/6T70 6-speed automatic. 9th gen: 6T40 6-speed (1.5T, 2016–2018); CVT (1.5T, 2019–2025); 8L45 8-speed automatic (2.0T, 2016–2018); 9T50 9-speed automatic (2.0T, 2019–2022); Voltec-style EVT two-motor drive unit (Hybrid).

horsepower.label: Horsepower
horsepower.text: 196 (LKW) / 259 (LTG 8th gen) / 163 (LFV) / 250 (LTG 9th gen) / 182 combined (Hybrid).

drivetrain.label: Drivetrain
drivetrain.text: FWD only, all years, all trims. No AWD ever offered on US 8th/9th gen.

dimensions.label: Dimensions
dimensions.text: 2015 (8th gen): 191.5 L x 73.0 W x 57.6 H in; wheelbase 107.8 in; curb ~3,393–3,660 lbs (2013 figures, same body). 9th gen: 193.8 L (194.2 in from 2019 facelift) x 73.0 W x 57.3–57.6 H in; wheelbase 111.4 in; curb 3,086–3,388 lbs (2016–2018), 3,126–3,223 lbs (2019+).

reliability.label: Reliability
reliability.text: 9th gen is one of GM's more trouble-free passenger cars of the era — the 1.5T/6-speed and Hybrid powertrains have modest complaint counts vs. the trucks; the headline defect is the "Shift to Park" shifter fault (below), which reached a class-action settlement. 2.0T/9T50 cars carry the usual GM 9-speed shift complaints. 8th gen (2015) is a simple, durable powertrain. Consumer Reports / J.D. Power scores: Not verified — needs OEM source.

knownIssues.label: Known issues
knownIssues.text: "Shift to Park" message / no-shutoff defect (shifter microswitch and harness) on 2016–2019 Malibu — class action Emmrich v. GM; settled (Jefferson v. GM settlement: $500 cash + up to $375 repair reimbursement); complaint pattern continued on 2020–2022 cars per later filings (Green v. GM). 1.5L LFV: electronic wastegate actuator/turbo faults and sensor issues documented in the service trade; CVT (2019+) shudder/complaints on high-mile cars. Hybrid: Voltec-family electronics generally solid; 12V battery/BECM-family faults shared with Volt-era parts. 8th gen: electric power-steering and intake-actuator faults at high mileage.

maintenance.label: Maintenance
maintenance.text: Oil per OLM; dexos1 Gen 2/Gen 3 — 5W-30 (2.5L LKW, 2.0T LTG), 0W-20 (1.5T LFV, Hybrid). Spark plugs: iridium ~97,500 mi. Coolant: Dex-Cool 5 yr/150,000 mi. Transmission fluid: 6T40 uses AF6; CVT/9T50/8L45 per severe-service 45,000-mi schedule; Hybrid drive unit fluid per schedule. Brake fluid: inspect, no fixed GM interval. All engines timing-chain (LKW/LTG/LFV/LKN all chain-driven).

performance.label: Performance
performance.text: 0–60: Not verified — needs OEM source (period tests: 2.0T Premier ~6.4 sec; 1.5T ~8.5 sec). No factory performance trim; RS (2019+) is appearance-only. Hybrid was the tech flagship ( Volt-derived drive unit, 1.5 kWh pack ).

mods.label: Modifications
mods.text: Minimal scene. Trifecta/ZZP-style OBD tunes exist for the 2.0T (LTG is shared with Camaro/ATS, so bolt-ons — intake, downpipe, intercooler — cross over); 1.5T tunes exist but gains are modest. Otherwise wheels, tint, and cosmetics. No factory performance variant.

engineering.label: Engineering
engineering.text: The 8th-gen car (Epsilon II, shared with Buick Regal) ended its run in 2015 with the direct-injected 2.5L replacing the old port-injected 2.4L. The 9th gen (2016) moved to the lighter E2XX architecture (~300 lbs lighter, 3.6-in-longer wheelbase), introduced the 1.5T/CVT small-displacement formula, and borrowed the Volt's two-motor drive unit for a genuinely efficient Hybrid (46 mpg combined). The 2019 facelift swapped the 1.5T's 6-speed for a CVT and made the 2.0T Premier-only; GM ended Malibu production in November 2024, ending Chevy's US sedan line.

enthusiast.label: Enthusiast
enthusiast.text: Little enthusiast cachet — the Malibu is fleet/family transportation. The LTG 2.0T cars get mild tuner attention because the engine is shared with the Camaro 2.0T. The Hybrid has a small efficiency-nerd following as a "Volt sedan without the plug." In the used market it's valued as cheap, simple transportation.

comparison.label: Comparison
comparison.text: Rivals: Toyota Camry, Honda Accord, Nissan Altima, Hyundai Sonata, Kia K5. The Malibu undercuts all of them on used-market price and the 1.5T matches segment fuel economy, but the Camry/Accord beat it on resale, interior quality, and long-term reliability reputation; the 2.0T Premier is an underrated value as a quick used sedan. Discontinued — parts and service support continue via GM.

chevrolet-traverse.subtitle: Three-row midsize/full-size crossover SUV, 2015–2017 (1st gen, Lambda platform), 2018–2023 (2nd gen, C1XX platform), 2024–2026 (3rd gen, C1-2 platform)

engine.label: Engine
engine.text: 1st gen (2015–2017): 3.6L LLT High-Feature V6, NA, DI — 281 hp / 266 lb-ft (single exhaust) or 288 hp / 270 lb-ft (dual exhaust). 2nd gen (2018–2023): 3.6L LFY V6, NA, DI (310 hp @ 6,800 / 266 lb-ft @ 2,800); 2.0L LTG turbo I4 (257 hp / 295 lb-ft, RS trim only, 2018–2019). 3rd gen (2024–2026): 2.5L LK0 turbo I4 (328 hp @ 5,500 / 326 lb-ft @ 3,500).

transmission.label: Transmission
transmission.text: 1st gen: Hydra-Matic 6T75 6-speed automatic. 2nd gen: 9T65 9-speed automatic. 3rd gen: 8-speed automatic (GM's 9-speed architecture with one forward gear removed, per GM Authority).

horsepower.label: Horsepower
horsepower.text: 281/288 (LLT, exhaust-dependent) / 310 (LFY) / 257 (LTG) / 328 (LK0). (Third-party listings quoting 315–316 hp for the LK0 reflect metric or preliminary figures; 328 hp is the US SAE rating.)

drivetrain.label: Drivetrain
drivetrain.text: FWD standard, AWD optional on 1st and 2nd gen (2nd-gen High Country AWD-only; LS FWD-only). 3rd gen: FWD standard, AWD optional; Z71 gets a twin-clutch off-road-capable AWD system with Terrain Mode and hill-descent control.

dimensions.label: Dimensions
dimensions.text: 1st gen: 203.7 L x 78.5 W x 69.9 H in; wheelbase 118.9 in; curb 4,647–4,956 lbs. 2nd gen: 204.3 L (205.9 in for 2022–2023 facelift) x 78.6 W x 70.7 H in; wheelbase 120.9 in; curb 4,310–4,362 lbs FWD (AWD ~4,500+ lbs). 3rd gen: 204.5 L x 79.6 W (87.9 with mirrors) x 69.6–69.9 H in; wheelbase 121.0 in; curb ~4,793 lbs (AWD per KBB 2026 specs; FWD lower — exact FWD figure Not verified — needs OEM source).

mpg.label: Fuel economy (MPG)
mpg.text: (EPA city/hwy/combined, fueleconomy.gov) 1st gen: FWD 15/22/18; AWD 15/22/17. 2nd gen: 3.6L FWD 18/27/21; 3.6L AWD 17/25/20; 2.0T RS FWD 20/26/22. 3rd gen: FWD 20/27/23; AWD 19/24/21.

reliability.label: Reliability
reliability.text: 1st gen: the LLT timing-chain issue (below) is the defining fault; otherwise the 6T75/LLT powertrain is durable when maintained, and 2013+ builds had updated chains/tensioners. 2nd gen: mid-pack; 9T65 shift-quality complaints are the main pattern; LFY chain drive is improved over LLT. 3rd gen: too new for statistical data — the LK0 is a new engine family (a shorter-stroke relative of the Silverado's 2.7L L3B), so longevity is unproven as of mid-2026. Consumer Reports / J.D. Power scores: Not verified — needs OEM source.

knownIssues.label: Known issues
knownIssues.text: 1st gen: 3.6L LLT timing-chain stretch (codes P0008/P0016/P0017) — the well-documented GM 3.6 HFV6 issue; chain/tensioner/phaser replacement is a common big-ticket job; wave-plate/shift faults on high-mile 6T75s; power-steering and water-pump failures at mileage. 2nd gen: 9T65 9-speed jerking/hesitation/rough shifting (widely reported by owners and service publications); "Shift to Park" shifter defect on 2018–2019 (included in the Jefferson v. GM settlement); some LFY timing-chain and front-camera/module faults. 3rd gen: early-build software/infotainment recalls reported; no mechanical pattern established as of mid-2026.

maintenance.label: Maintenance
maintenance.text: Oil per OLM; dexos1 — 5W-30 (LLT, LFY), 0W-20 (LK0, dexos1 Gen 3). Oil capacity 5.3 qt (LK0 class), 6.0 qt (3.6L). Spark plugs: iridium ~97,500 mi. Coolant: Dex-Cool 5 yr/150,000 mi. Transmission fluid: 6T75/9T65/8-spd — severe-service 45,000 mi (AF6-family fluids; 6T75 total fill ~9 qt class). AWD transfer case/rear drive module fluids per severe schedule. Brake fluid: inspect, no fixed GM interval. All engines timing-chain.

performance.label: Performance
performance.text: 0–60: 3rd gen 2.5T — ~7.6 sec 0–100 km/h (≈7.3 sec 0–60) per published 2025–2026 spec listings; other generations Not verified — needs OEM source (period tests: 2nd-gen 3.6L ~6.7–7.0 sec). No factory performance trim; RS is appearance; Z71 (2024+) is the off-road trim with 5,000-lb tow rating.

mods.label: Modifications
mods.text: Minimal — this is a family hauler. Typical owner mods: trailer hitches/wiring, brake upgrades for towing, all-terrain tires and small lifts on Z71s, OBD tunes for the 2.0T RS (LTG) and interest in LK0 tuning as the 2.7L L3B aftermarket ecosystem grows. No factory performance variant.

engineering.label: Engineering
engineering.text: 1st gen was GM's Lambda-platform minivan replacement (shared with Enclave/Acadia/Outlook): transverse 3.6L HFV6, 6-speed, up to 8 seats. The 2nd gen (2018) moved to the long-wheelbase C1XX architecture with meaningful weight reduction (~350 lbs), the updated LFY V6 and a 9-speed; a 2022 facelift added standard active-safety content. The 3rd gen (2024) is the biggest change: V6 dropped for the 2.5L LK0 turbo four (more power and torque than the V6), C1-2 platform with GM's Global B electrical architecture enabling OTA updates, a standard 17.7-inch touchscreen, and a Z71 off-road variant.

enthusiast.label: Enthusiast
enthusiast.text: No cult following — the Traverse community is owners' forums focused on the LLT timing-chain job and 9T65 behavior. The 2.0T RS (2018–2019) is a curiosity for LTG tuners. The 3rd-gen Z71 is earning respect as a family adventure rig. DIY community is strong because timing-chain and trans work is expensive at dealers.

comparison.label: Comparison
comparison.text: Rivals: Honda Pilot, Toyota Highlander/Grand Highlander, Ford Explorer, Kia Telluride, Hyundai Palisade. The Traverse consistently wins on interior/cargo space per dollar; the Telluride/Palisade beat it on refinement and warranty, the Highlander on reliability reputation and resale. 3rd-gen power/fuel-economy gains (328 hp, 23 mpg combined FWD) make it more competitive on paper; long-term LK0 durability is the open question.

chevrolet-tahoe.subtitle: Full-size body-on-frame SUV, 2015–2020 (4th gen, GMT K2XX), 2021–2026 (5th gen, GMT T1XX; 2025 mid-cycle refresh)

engine.label: Engine
engine.text: 4th gen (2015–2020): 5.3L L83 EcoTec3 V8, NA, DI, AFM (355 hp @ 5,600 rpm / 383 lb-ft @ 4,100) — sole engine 2015–2017; 6.2L L86 EcoTec3 V8 (420 hp @ 5,600 / 460 lb-ft @ 4,100) added for RST 6.2L Performance Edition (2018) and Premier (2019–2020). 5th gen (2021–2026): 5.3L L84 V8 (DFM, 355/383); 6.2L L87 V8 (DFM, 420/460); 3.0L LM2 Duramax turbo-diesel I6 (277 hp @ 3,750 / 460 lb-ft @ 1,500, 2021–2024); 3.0L LZ0 Duramax I6 (305 hp / 495 lb-ft, 2025–2026).

transmission.label: Transmission
transmission.text: 4th gen: Hydra-Matic 6L80 6-speed automatic (5.3L, all years); 10L90 10-speed automatic (6.2L, 2018–2020). 5th gen: Hydra-Matic 10L80 10-speed automatic (RPO MHS gas / MQC diesel) across the board.

drivetrain.label: Drivetrain
drivetrain.text: RWD standard, 4WD optional every year/trim (AutoTrac 2-speed transfer case; Z71 off-road package both gens). No AWD-only variant. Max towing 8,200–8,400 lbs depending on engine/drivetrain (5th gen).

dimensions.label: Dimensions
dimensions.text: 4th gen: 204.0 L x 80.5 W x 74.4 H in; wheelbase 116.0 in; curb 5,308–5,631 lbs. 5th gen: 210.7 L x 81.1 W x 75.8 H in; wheelbase 120.9 in; curb 5,473–5,845 lbs.

reliability.label: Reliability
reliability.text: 4th gen: fundamentally durable (6L80 + L83 is a proven combo), but shares the AFM lifter/oil-consumption exposure of the K2XX trucks; A/C condenser failures are a well-known service item. 5th gen: elevated complaint/recall exposure — the 6.2L L87 is covered by NHTSA recall 25V-274 plus two open federal investigations (RQ26001, EA25007); DFM lifter failures documented on L84/L87; sibling-platform NHTSA data (T1XX Yukon: 1,238 complaints/20 recalls over 2021–2025, L87 = 894 of them) indicates the complaint load is concentrated in the 6.2L, with the 5.3L and especially the 3.0L diesel materially lower. Consumer Reports / J.D. Power / IIHS scores: Not verified — needs OEM source.

knownIssues.label: Known issues
knownIssues.text: 4th gen: AFM lifter collapse/oil consumption (5.3L); A/C condenser and refrigerant-line failures (very common service item); 6L80 torque-converter shudder at mileage; cracked dash (cosmetic, widely reported); brake vacuum-pump recall-era issues on late K2 trucks/SUVs. 5th gen: L87 connecting-rod/crankshaft recall 25V-274 (2021–2024 builds, ~598K US vehicles incl. Tahoe; engines built 3/2021–5/2024) and post-remedy failure investigation RQ26001 (Jan 2026); EA25007 probing additional 2019–2021 L87s; DFM lifter failures (shared with Silverado); 10L80 valve-body/harsh-shift complaints; 3.0L LM2 long-crank/fuel-prime complaints. Diesel LZ0 currently under a production constraint (2026) — availability, not a defect.

maintenance.label: Maintenance
maintenance.text: Oil per OLM; dexos1 Gen 2/Gen 3 5W-30 (L83/L84 gas V8s) — L87 recall remedy updated inspected engines to Mobil 1 Supercar 0W-40 fill spec; dexosD 0W-20 for 3.0L diesels. Spark plugs: iridium ~97,500 mi. Coolant: Dex-Cool 5 yr/150,000 mi. Transmission fluid: 6L80/10L80 — severe-service 45,000 mi (towing = severe service). Transfer-case and front/rear axle fluids per severe schedule (~50,000 mi). Brake fluid: inspect, no fixed GM interval. Air-suspension (Air Ride Adaptive, 5th gen) and Magnetic Ride Control require no scheduled fluid service but inspect lines/struts. All engines timing-chain.

performance.label: Performance
performance.text: 0–60: Not verified — needs OEM source (period instrumented tests: 6.2L RST ~5.7–5.9 sec; 5.3L ~7.1 sec; diesel ~7.8 sec). Performance variants: RST (2018+, 6.2L + 10L90 + Magnetic Ride on Performance Edition), Z71 (off-road), 5th-gen RST with available 6.2L, and dealer/aftermarket supercharger packages. Max tow 8,400 lbs (5.3L 2WD, 5th gen).

mods.label: Modifications
mods.text: Strong scene: Whipple/Magnuson/ProCharger supercharger kits for 5.3/6.2 (600+ hp builds are common), ECM/TCM tunes, AFM/DFM disablers and delete kits, long-tube headers/cat-backs, lowering kits (street Tahoes), 2–4-in lifts and 33–35-in tires on Z71s, brake upgrades. The 6.2L/10-speed RST is the community's factory-hot-rod baseline.

engineering.label: Engineering
engineering.text: 4th gen K2XX was a traditional body-on-frame SUV on the Silverado K2 architecture — boxed frame, EcoTec3 DI V8s, solid rear axle with 5-link coil suspension, Magnetic Ride Control available. The 5th gen T1XX (2021) was the Tahoe's biggest redesign in decades: ~4.9 in longer wheelbase, an independent rear suspension (first on a Tahoe) unlocking a much larger third row and cargo floor, available four-corner Air Ride Adaptive Suspension, and the addition of the 3.0L Duramax inline-six diesel. The 2025 refresh brought the 17.7-inch screen, updated LZ0 diesel, and new driver-assist content.

enthusiast.label: Enthusiast
enthusiast.text: The Tahoe has a genuine dual personality in the scene: street/performance crowd (lowered 6.2L builds, superchargers) and overland/tow crowd (Z71 lifts). The 2018–2020 RST 6.2L Performance Edition is a modern cult trim — the closest thing to a factory Tahoe SS. Diesel 5th-gens are prized by high-mileage towers. Forum consensus: buy the 5.3L unless you want the 6.2's power — the L87 recall history hangs over 2021–2024 6.2s.

comparison.label: Comparison
comparison.text: Rivals: Ford Expedition, Toyota Sequoia, Nissan Armada, GMC Yukon (twin), Jeep Wagoneer. The Tahoe leads the class in cargo/packaging since the IRS redesign and in powertrain choice (only body-on-frame SUV with a diesel option); Expedition counters with a stronger twin-turbo V6 torque curve, Sequoia with hybrid-only powertrain and Toyota resale. Reliability-wise the 5.3L/diesel Tahoes are competitive; the 6.2L's recall record is a 2021–2024 used-buy checkpoint.

chevrolet-colorado.subtitle: Midsize pickup, 2015–2022 (2nd gen, GMT31XX platform; 2021 facelift), 2023–2026 (3rd gen)

engine.label: Engine
engine.text: 2nd gen (2015–2022): 2.5L LCV Ecotec I4, NA, DI (200 hp / 191 lb-ft); 3.6L LFX High-Feature V6, NA, DI (304 hp / 269 lb-ft, 2015–2016); 3.6L LGZ V6, NA, DI (308 hp / 275 lb-ft, 2017–2022); 2.8L LWN Duramax turbo-diesel I4, DOHC, common-rail, VGT (181 hp / 369 lb-ft, 2016–2022). 3rd gen (2023–2026): 2.7L L2R "2.7 Turbo" turbo I4 (237 hp / 260 lb-ft, WT/LT, 2023–2024, dropped for 2025); 2.7L L3B TurboMax turbo I4 — 310 hp / 391 lb-ft (2023–2024 Z71/Trail Boss "Turbo Plus" calibration) and 310 hp @ 5,600 / 430 lb-ft @ 3,000 (ZR2 2023–2024; sole tune on all trims 2025–2026).

transmission.label: Transmission
transmission.text: 2nd gen: Hydra-Matic 6L50 6-speed automatic (2.5L, 3.6L LFX, 2.8L diesel); 6-speed manual available on 2.5L WT (2015–2019, EPA-listed); Hydra-Matic 8L45 8-speed automatic (3.6L LGZ, 2017–2022). 3rd gen: second-generation GM 8-speed automatic (RPO MFC) on all trims.

drivetrain.label: Drivetrain
drivetrain.text: RWD standard, 4WD optional both generations (2.5L RWD-only in later 2nd-gen years; Z71/ZR2 4WD-focused). ZR2 (2017+): front and rear electronic locking differentials, Multimatic DSSV dampers; ZR2 Bison (2019+, AEV armor); 3rd-gen Trail Boss (2-in lift, 3-in-wider track) and ZR2 (3-in lift, DSSV). Max towing: 3,500 lbs (2.5L), 7,000 lbs (V6), 7,700 lbs (diesel 2nd gen and TurboMax 3rd gen).

dimensions.label: Dimensions
dimensions.text: 2nd gen: length 212.4–212.5 in; wheelbase 128.3–140.5 in (extended/crew cab, box dependent); width 74.3 in; height 70.3–70.4 in; curb 3,935–4,864 lbs (diesel crew 4WD heaviest). 3rd gen (crew cab only): wheelbase 131.4 in; length 213.0 in (WT/LT/Z71), 213.2 in (Trail Boss), 212.7 in (ZR2); width 74.9 in (76.4 in Trail Boss/ZR2, mirrors excluded); height ~75.7–79.6 in by trim; curb 4,164 lbs (WT 2WD) to 4,940 lbs (ZR2).

mpg.label: Fuel economy (MPG)
mpg.text: (EPA city/hwy/combined, fueleconomy.gov) 2nd gen 2WD: 2.5L auto 19–20/25–26/22; 2.5L manual 19–20/25–26/22; 3.6L 6-spd (2015) 18/25/21; 3.6L 8-spd (2017+) 18/25/20–21; 2.8L diesel 20–22/30/23–25. 2nd gen 4WD: 2.5L 19/24/21; 3.6L 8-spd 17/24/19; diesel 19–20/28/22–23. 3rd gen (2.7L turbo): 2WD 18–20/23–25/20–22; 4WD 17–19/21–23/19–21 (worst figures are Trail Boss/ZR2 mud-terrain fitments).

reliability.label: Reliability
reliability.text: 2nd gen: solid overall — the LCV/6L50 and LGZ/8L45 powertrains are proven, and the diesel was the segment's only oil-burner; the 8L45 is the weak link (class-action coverage, below). Consumer Reports lists 3 NHTSA recalls on the 2024 (battery hardware, seat-belt, plus one more). 3rd gen: early-build quality actions (below) but no pattern failure; sales/recall record still maturing as of mid-2026. Consumer Reports / J.D. Power / IIHS scores: Not verified — needs OEM source.

knownIssues.label: Known issues
knownIssues.text: 2nd gen: 8L45 8-speed torque-converter shudder/harsh shifts — 2017–2019 Colorado explicitly named in the 8L90/8L45 class actions (Francis v. GM, Speerly v. GM); 2.8L LWN diesel — EGR cooler/valve soot clogging, NOx-sensor and DEF/reductant-system faults (well documented in the diesel service trade; large aftermarket EGR-delete and reductant-reset ecosystem exists — note deletes are emissions-illegal on road vehicles); 2015–2016 electric power-steering assist loss was subject of early recalls/TSBs. 3rd gen: L3B engine-block casting defect — GM Customer Satisfaction Program N232415060 (2023 builds; cracked main oil gallery, remedy = engine replacement); 2023 fuel-injector campaign (per lemon-law/legal documentation of GM's two 2024 repair campaigns covering 2023 Colorado/Silverado/Sierra/Canyon); early battery and seat-belt recalls on 2023–2024 builds.

maintenance.label: Maintenance
maintenance.text: Oil per OLM; dexos1 Gen 2/Gen 3 — 0W-20 (2.5L LCV, 2.7L L2R/L3B), 5W-30 (3.6L LFX/LGZ); dexos2/dexosD 5W-30 for the 2.8L LWN diesel (oil capacity 5.9 qt; 5.0 qt LCV; 6.0 qt LGZ). Spark plugs: iridium ~97,500 mi (gas engines). Coolant: Dex-Cool 5 yr/150,000 mi. Transmission fluid: 6L50/8L45/8-spd — severe-service 45,000 mi (8L45 shudder remedy is a specific fluid exchange per TSB). Transfer case/rear axle fluids per severe schedule. Diesel: fuel filter ~30,000 mi class; DEF refills per consumption. Brake fluid: inspect, no fixed GM interval. All engines timing-chain (LCV/LFX/LGZ/LWN) — the L3B uses a timing chain as well; no belts.

performance.label: Performance
performance.text: 0–60: Not verified — needs OEM source (period tests: 3rd-gen TurboMax ~6.8–7.0 sec; V6 2nd gen ~6.7 sec; diesel ~9 sec). Factory performance/off-road trims: Z71, ZR2 (2017+, DSSV dampers, front/rear lockers), ZR2 Bison (AEV steel bumpers, skid plates), 3rd-gen Trail Boss and ZR2 (Bison continues). Max tow 7,700 lbs.

mods.label: Modifications
mods.text: Very active off-road scene: suspension lifts/leveling kits, 32–35-in tires, re-gearing, aftermarket bumpers/armor (AEV — which builds the factory Bison parts), winches, lockers. Powertrain: OBD tunes for 2.7L TurboMax (and the L3B shares tuning support with the Silverado 2.7L), diesel LWN tunes/EGR deletes (off-road only), cat-back exhausts. The ZR2 is the anchor of the whole midsize off-road aftermarket.

engineering.label: Engineering
engineering.text: The 2nd gen (2015) resurrected GM's US midsize truck on the GMT31XX architecture (Wentzville, MO) with an unusually broad powertrain spread — NA four, HFV6, and the segment's only diesel — plus a 2017 powertrain upgrade (LGZ V6 + 8-speed) and 2021 facelift. The 3rd gen (2023) is a clean-sheet crew-cab-only truck: new chassis with 3.1-in-longer wheelbase and shortened front overhang, a single 2.7L turbo four in three calibrations replacing both V6 and diesel, an 11.3-inch Google-based cockpit, and a trim ladder (WT/LT/Z71/Trail Boss/ZR2) that mirrors the full-size trucks.

enthusiast.label: Enthusiast
enthusiast.text: The ZR2 made Colorado the default midsize off-road platform — Multimatic DSSV dampers and factory front/rear lockers are still unmatched by the Tacoma TRD Pro's spec sheet, and the Bison/AEV tie-in gives it factory-backed armor. Diesel 2nd-gens have a loyal tow/efficiency niche. The 3rd-gen TurboMax has quickly gained tuner support. Forum consensus: 2017+ V6 or diesel 2nd-gens are the sweet used buys; check 8L45 service history.

comparison.label: Comparison
comparison.text: Rivals: Toyota Tacoma, Ford Ranger, Jeep Gladiator, Nissan Frontier, GMC Canyon (twin). The Colorado beats the Tacoma on powertrain refinement and on-road manners (especially 3rd gen vs. the Tacoma's carryover feel until 2024), matches the Ranger on capability, and the ZR2 out-specs most rivals off-road; Toyota still wins on resale and long-term reliability reputation. Canyon is the same truck in GMC trim.

chevrolet-suburban.subtitle: Full-size extended-length body-on-frame SUV, 2015–2020 (11th gen, GMT K2XX), 2021–2026 (12th gen, GMT T1XX; 2025 mid-cycle refresh)

engine.label: Engine
engine.text: 4th gen (2015–2020): 5.3L L83 EcoTec3 V8, NA, DI, AFM (355 hp @ 5,600 rpm / 383 lb-ft @ 4,100) — sole engine 2015–2017; 6.2L L86 EcoTec3 V8 (420 hp @ 5,600 / 460 lb-ft @ 4,100) added for RST 6.2L Performance Edition (2018) and Premier (2019–2020). 5th gen (2021–2026): 5.3L L84 V8 (DFM, 355/383); 6.2L L87 V8 (DFM, 420/460); 3.0L LM2 Duramax turbo-diesel I6 (277 hp @ 3,750 / 460 lb-ft @ 1,500, 2021–2024); 3.0L LZ0 Duramax I6 (305 hp / 495 lb-ft, 2025–2026). Powertrains are shared mechanically with the Tahoe.

transmission.label: Transmission
transmission.text: 4th gen: Hydra-Matic 6L80 6-speed automatic (5.3L, all years); 10L90 10-speed automatic (6.2L, 2018–2020). 5th gen: Hydra-Matic 10L80 10-speed automatic (RPO MHS gas / MQC diesel) across the board.

drivetrain.label: Drivetrain
drivetrain.text: RWD standard, 4WD optional every year/trim (AutoTrac 2-speed transfer case; Z71 off-road package). Max towing up to ~8,300 lbs (5th gen, configuration dependent); the Suburban tows slightly less than the equivalent Tahoe due to its longer wheelbase/weight.

dimensions.label: Dimensions
dimensions.text: 4th gen: 224.4 L x 80.5 W x 74.4 H in; wheelbase 130.0 in; curb 5,586–5,822 lbs. 5th gen: 225.7 L x 81.1 W x 75.7 H in; wheelbase 134.1 in; curb 5,616–6,016 lbs.

reliability.label: Reliability
reliability.text: Mirrors the Tahoe mechanically. 4th gen: durable L83/6L80 core with AFM valvetrain exposure and common A/C condenser failures. 5th gen: the 6.2L L87 is covered by NHTSA recall 25V-274 (Suburban explicitly included, 2021–2024 builds) plus open investigations RQ26001 (post-remedy failures) and EA25007 (additional 2019–2021 L87 engines); DFM lifter failures documented; the 5.3L and 3.0L diesel carry materially lower complaint loads on sibling-platform NHTSA data. Consumer Reports / J.D. Power / IIHS scores: Not verified — needs OEM source.

knownIssues.label: Known issues
knownIssues.text: 4th gen: AFM lifter collapse/oil consumption (5.3L L83); A/C condenser and refrigerant-line failures (very common); 6L80 torque-converter shudder at mileage; cracked dashboard (cosmetic, widely reported); late-K2 brake vacuum-pump recall coverage. 5th gen: L87 connecting-rod/crankshaft defect — recall 25V-274 (~598K US vehicles incl. Suburban, engines built 3/2021–5/2024), NHTSA Recall Query RQ26001 (Jan 2026), Engineering Analysis EA25007 (Oct 2025); DFM lifter failures (L84/L87); 10L80 valve-body/harsh-shift complaints; LM2 long-crank/fuel-prime complaints; 2026 LZ0 diesel under a production constraint (availability issue, not a defect).

maintenance.label: Maintenance
maintenance.text: Oil per OLM; dexos1 Gen 2/Gen 3 5W-30 (gas V8s) — L87 recall remedy updated inspected engines to Mobil 1 Supercar 0W-40 fill spec; dexosD 0W-20 for 3.0L diesels. Spark plugs: iridium ~97,500 mi. Coolant: Dex-Cool 5 yr/150,000 mi. Transmission fluid: 6L80/10L80 — severe-service 45,000 mi (towing = severe service). Transfer-case and axle fluids per severe schedule (~50,000 mi). Brake fluid: inspect, no fixed GM interval. Air Ride Adaptive Suspension and Magnetic Ride Control (5th gen): no scheduled fluid service; inspect lines/struts. All engines timing-chain.

performance.label: Performance
