enthusiast.text: Not a tuner car — the community is overland/build-out focused (r/rav4club, r/battlecars). The Prime has sleeper status ("family crossover that runs 5.5 to 60") and the TRD Off-Road has a loyal following for soft-roading. Resale values are exceptional, which fuels the used market more than the mod scene.

comparison.label: Comparison
comparison.text: Rivals: Honda CR-V, Mazda CX-5/CX-50, Nissan Rogue, Hyundai Tucson, Subaru Forester/Outback. The RAV4 matches the CR-V on practicality, beats it on hybrid mpg (41/38/40), and trails only the Mazda on driving feel; best-in-segment resale and reliability reputation made it the US's best-selling non-pickup vehicle.

engine.label: Engine
engine.text: N200 (2015): 2.7L 2TR-FE naturally aspirated I4; 4.0L 1GR-FE naturally aspirated V6. N300: 2.7L 2TR-FE I4; 3.5L 2GR-FKS naturally aspirated V6 (D-4S, VVT-iW Atkinson-capable). N400: 2.4L T24A-FTS turbocharged I4 (i-FORCE); 2.4L T24A-FTS turbo + motor (i-FORCE MAX hybrid).

transmission.label: Transmission
transmission.text: N200: 5-speed manual, 6-speed manual (V6), 4/5-speed automatic (I4/V6). N300: 6-speed AC60F automatic; 6-speed RC62F manual (V6 4x4); 5-speed manual (I4, 2016–2017). N400: 8-speed automatic (code not verified — needs OEM source; fluid listings reference "L580F"); 6-speed intelligent manual (i-FORCE, SR/TRD Sport/TRD Off-Road).

horsepower.label: Horsepower
horsepower.text: N200: 2TR-FE 159 hp @ 5,200 rpm; 1GR-FE 236 hp @ 5,200 rpm. N300: 2TR-FE 159 hp @ 5,200 rpm; 2GR-FKS 278 hp @ 6,000 rpm. N400: T24A-FTS 228 hp @ 6,000 rpm (SR, auto) / 270 hp (manual) / 278 hp @ 6,000 rpm (all other trims, auto); i-FORCE MAX 326 hp @ 6,000 rpm combined.

torque.label: Torque
torque.text: N200: 2TR-FE 180 lb-ft @ 3,800 rpm; 1GR-FE 266 lb-ft @ 4,000 rpm. N300: 2TR-FE 180 lb-ft @ 3,800 rpm; 2GR-FKS 265 lb-ft @ 4,600 rpm. N400: 243 lb-ft @ 1,600 rpm (SR) / 310 lb-ft (manual) / 317 lb-ft @ 1,700 rpm (278-hp auto); i-FORCE MAX 465 lb-ft @ 1,700 rpm combined.

drivetrain.label: Drivetrain
drivetrain.text: N200/N300: RWD (4x2) or part-time 4WD with 2-speed transfer case; TRD Off-Road adds locking rear diff, Multi-Terrain Select and Crawl Control; TRD Pro adds Fox/Old Man Emu suspension. N400: RWD or part-time 4WD; Limited offers full-time 4WD with center diff (hybrid only); TRD Pro/Trailhunter are hybrid 4WD with rear locker, disconnecting front sway bar (TRD Pro).

reliability.label: Reliability
reliability.text: N200: Consumer Reports standout — one of the most reliable trucks ever tracked; frame-rust warranty extensions on earlier N200 years (2005–2010 mostly; inspect 2015 frames in the Rust Belt). N300: J.D. Power midsize-pickup dependability leader multiple years; Consumer Reports above average except 2016 (first-model-year complaints); best-in-class resale value year after year. N400: too new for verified long-term data.

knownIssues.label: Known issues
knownIssues.text: N200 (2015): fewer issues than earlier years; verify frame condition on any truck from salt states (family-wide corrosion history, warranty extensions/buybacks on 2005–2010). N300: crank-position sensor recall (NHTSA 17V285 — 2016–2017 V6, engine stall risk); AC60F 6-speed automatic gear-hunting/harsh-shift complaints addressed by multiple TSB reflashes (widely documented on CarComplaints/TacomaWorld); accessory drive-belt chirp (TSB T-SB-0098-20, 2016–2020 2GR-FKS); third-brake-light gasket water leaks (widely documented); rear leaf-spring squeak/corrosion service campaigns; Denso fuel-pump recall family touches 2018–2019. N400: early NHTSA complaints center on 8-speed automatic shift quality; no established failure pattern yet — too new.

maintenance.label: Maintenance
maintenance.text: Oil 0W-20 every 10,000 mi/12 mo (5,000 mi severe/off-road/towing). Iridium plugs 120,000 mi. SLLC coolant 100,000/50,000 mi. ATF WS lifetime fill — inspect; 60,000 mi drain-and-fill if towed/off-road. Front/rear diff and transfer-case gear oil (GL-5 75W-85) every 15,000 mi if submerged/off-road use, else inspect. Grease propeller-shaft spiders (N300/N400). Brake fluid inspect; 2–3 yr replacement common. All engines timing chain.

performance.label: Performance
performance.text: N300: V6 auto 4x4 ~7.7 s 0–60 (C&D); I4 ~10+ s. N400: gas 278-hp ~7.5 s; i-FORCE MAX ~6.9 s (C&D, TRD Off-Road). Factory performance/off-road trims: TRD Sport, TRD Off-Road, TRD Pro (all gens in range — 2015 TRD Pro, 2017–2023 TRD Pro, 2024+ TRD Pro), plus 2024+ Trailhunter (ARB/Old Man Emu factory overland build).

mods.label: Modifications
mods.text: Deepest aftermarket in the segment: ECU tunes for 2GR-FKS and T24A-FTS (OVTune/VCU, KDMax, COBB Accessport for 2024+ including TCM flash); Magnuson supercharger kit for N300 V6; suspension from leveling kits to long-travel (Bilstein 6112, OME, King, Fox); 33–35 in tires with regear (4.88/5.29); steel bumpers, sliders, skid plates, winches; rooftop tents, bed racks, onboard air. N400 turbo responds well to tuning — 400+ lb-ft reported on stage tunes.

engineering.label: Engineering
engineering.text: N200 ended in 2015 on the F2 platform. N300 brought a high-strength-steel frame, the D-4S 2GR-FKS with VVT-iW (Atkinson cycle under light load), and GoPro windshield mount; 2020 facelift added LED lamps, CarPlay/Android Auto, and a power driver seat. N400 moves to the TNGA-F body-on-frame platform (shared with Tundra/Sequoia/Land Cruiser 300), swaps the V6 for a twin-scroll turbo four, offers coil-spring rear suspension on upper trims (leafs on SR/SR5/PreRunner), and adds the 465-lb-ft i-FORCE MAX hybrid.

enthusiast.label: Enthusiast
enthusiast.text: The Tacoma is the enthusiast midsize truck — huge communities (TacomaWorld, r/ToyotaTacoma), factory TRD halo trims, and unmatched tuner/armor/overland support. 2nd-gen trucks (especially 2012–2015) have cult resale; the N300 manual-transmission V6 is a modern collectible; N400 Trailhunter/TRD Pro are the current grails.

comparison.label: Comparison
comparison.text: Rivals: Ford Ranger, Chevy Colorado/GMC Canyon, Nissan Frontier, Jeep Gladiator, Honda Ridgeline. The Ranger and Colorado out-power and out-tow the N300; the Tacoma counters with the best reliability record, resale value, and aftermarket. The N400 closes the powertrain gap — the hybrid's 465 lb-ft beats every rival's torque figure.

engine.label: Engine
engine.text: XU50: 2.7L 1AR-FE naturally aspirated I4 (base, FWD); 3.5L 2GR-FE naturally aspirated V6 (2014–2016); 3.5L 2GR-FKS V6 (2017–2019, D-4S); Hybrid: 3.5L 2GR-FXE (2014–2016) / 2GR-FXS (2017–2019) Atkinson V6 + motors. XU70: 3.5L 2GR-FKS V6 (2020–2022); 2.4L T24A-FTS turbo I4 (2023+, replaces V6); Hybrid: 2.5L A25A-FXS Atkinson I4 (THS II).

transmission.label: Transmission
transmission.text: XU50: 6-speed U760E automatic (2014–2016); 8-speed UA80E/UA80F automatic (2017–2019); e-CVT (hybrid). XU70: 8-speed UA80E/F "Direct Shift" automatic (V6 and turbo); Aisin T110 e-CVT (hybrid).

horsepower.label: Horsepower
horsepower.text: XU50: 1AR-FE 185 hp @ 5,800 rpm; 2GR-FE 270 hp @ 6,200 rpm; 2GR-FKS 295 hp @ 6,600 rpm; hybrid 280 hp combined (2014–2016) / 306 hp combined (2017–2019). XU70: 2GR-FKS 295 hp @ 6,600 rpm; T24A-FTS 265 hp @ ~6,000 rpm; hybrid 243 hp combined @ 6,000 rpm.

torque.label: Torque
torque.text: XU50: 1AR-FE 184 lb-ft @ 4,200 rpm; 2GR-FE 248 lb-ft @ 4,700 rpm; 2GR-FKS 263 lb-ft @ 4,700 rpm. XU70: 2GR-FKS 263 lb-ft @ 4,700 rpm; T24A-FTS 310 lb-ft @ 1,700–3,600 rpm; hybrid engine 175 lb-ft @ 4,400 rpm.

drivetrain.label: Drivetrain
drivetrain.text: XU50: FWD or on-demand AWD (V6); I4 is FWD only; Hybrid AWD standard (rear motor, 4WD-i). XU70: FWD or AWD (gas — Limited/Platinum get Dynamic Torque Vectoring AWD); Hybrid FWD or AWD-i (rear motor); 2023+ turbo AWD widely fitted.

reliability.label: Reliability
reliability.text: XU50: Consumer Reports above average; IIHS Top Safety Pick+ (2017–2019 with TSS-P); NHTSA 5-star overall; J.D. Power midsize-SUV dependability leader multiple years. XU70: Consumer Reports recommended; IIHS Top Safety Pick+ (2020–2023); NHTSA 5-star overall. The 2GR V6 family is regarded by technicians as one of Toyota's most durable engines.

knownIssues.label: Known issues
knownIssues.text: XU50: power-liftgate strut/motor failures widely documented (2014–2016); U760E 6-speed torque-converter shudder reports; Denso low-pressure fuel-pump recall covers 2018–2019 (NHTSA 20V012 family); Takata airbag recalls; Hybrid: earlier 2GR-FXE/FXS inverter coolant-pump failures documented at high mileage. XU70: Denso fuel-pump recall (2020–2021); 2020–2021 fuel-tank shield/vapor-line recall family — verify by VIN; some early UA80 shift complaints; T24A-FTS (2023+) too new for established patterns — watch oil consumption notes in owner forums, no systemic defect documented yet.

maintenance.label: Maintenance
maintenance.text: Oil 0W-20 every 10,000 mi/12 mo (turbo: strict 10,000 mi max, 5,000 mi if towing). Iridium plugs 120,000 mi. SLLC coolant 100,000/50,000 mi. ATF WS lifetime fill — inspect; 60,000 mi drain-and-fill if towing (5,000-lb tow rating V6/turbo; hybrid 3,500 lb). AWD rear diff/transfer gear oil GL-5 75W-85 — inspect/replace. Brake fluid inspect; 2–3 yr replacement common. All engines timing chain.

performance.label: Performance
performance.text: XU50: V6 ~7.1–7.3 s 0–60; hybrid ~7.3 s. XU70: V6 ~7.2 s (C&D); turbo ~7.3–7.5 s; hybrid ~7.8 s. No factory performance trim — XSE (2021+) is the sport-styled grade with sport-tuned suspension.

mods.label: Modifications
mods.text: Light scene: 20-in wheel/tire packages, lowering springs (rare), roof boxes and crossbars, towing accessories (this is the Highlander's real use case), PPF/tint. 2GR-FKS ECU tunes exist but uptake is small. Overland-lite builds (all-terrain tires, small lifts) appear but the Grand Highlander now absorbs much of that crowd.

engineering.label: Engineering
engineering.text: XU50 (Toyota K platform) grew to eight seats with a sliding second row. The 2017 facelift brought the 2GR-FKS with D-4S injection and an 8-speed, plus standard TSS-P. XU70 moves to TNGA GA-K with a stiffer body, multilink rear suspension, standard TSS 2.5+, and swaps the V6 for the higher-torque T24A-FTS turbo in 2023 while the hybrid switches from the V6-based system to the 2.5L A25A THS II — cutting combined fuel use by roughly a third versus the old V6 hybrid.

enthusiast.label: Enthusiast
enthusiast.text: Minimal enthusiast following — it's a family appliance with a stellar durability reputation. The community (ToyotaNation, r/ToyotaHighlander) focuses on towing setups, road-trip builds, and high-mileage maintenance bragging rights; 300,000-mile 2GR examples are common. XSE has mild curb-appeal following.

comparison.label: Comparison
comparison.text: Rivals: Honda Pilot, Kia Telluride, Hyundai Palisade, Mazda CX-90, Ford Explorer, Nissan Pathfinder. The Telluride/Palisade and CX-90 feel richer inside; the Highlander counters with the best hybrid mpg in class (36 combined), top reliability scores (Consumer Reports/J.D. Power), and stronger resale — the safe choice rather than the exciting one. Buyers needing more third-row space are directed to the Grand Highlander (2024+).

engine.label: Engine
engine.text: N280: 4.0L 1GR-FE naturally aspirated V6 with Dual VVT-i — the sole US engine for 2015–2024 (the 2.7L 2TR-FE I4 was dropped after 2010). N500: 2.4L T24A-FTS turbo I4 (i-FORCE); i-FORCE MAX hybrid adds a 48-hp motor inside the transmission and a 1.87-kWh 288V NiMH battery.

transmission.label: Transmission
transmission.text: N280: 5-speed A750F automatic (ECT). N500: 8-speed "Direct Shift" ECTi automatic.

horsepower.label: Horsepower
horsepower.text: N280: 270 hp @ 5,600 rpm. N500: i-FORCE 278 hp @ 6,000 rpm; i-FORCE MAX 326 hp @ 6,000 rpm.

torque.label: Torque
torque.text: N280: 278 lb-ft @ 4,400 rpm. N500: i-FORCE 317 lb-ft @ 1,700 rpm; i-FORCE MAX 465 lb-ft @ 1,700 rpm.

drivetrain.label: Drivetrain
drivetrain.text: N280: RWD (SR5/Limited); part-time 4WD (SR5/TRD trims); full-time 4WD with Torsen limited-slip center differential (Limited). A-TRAC standard; TRD Off-Road/TRD Pro add locking rear differential, Crawl Control and Multi-Terrain Select. N500: RWD; part-time 4WD; full-time 4WD (Limited); stabilizer-bar disconnect available on i-FORCE MAX TRD Off-Road Premium, standard on TRD Pro/Trailhunter.

mpg.label: Fuel economy (MPG)
mpg.text: N280: 2WD 17/22/18; 4WD 17/21/18 — essentially unchanged 2015–2024 (fueleconomy.gov). N500: i-FORCE 2WD 20/26/22; 4WD ~19/24/21 (EPA estimates); i-FORCE MAX up to 23 combined.

reliability.label: Reliability
reliability.text: N280 is one of the most durable vehicles sold in America — iSeeCars' 2019 longest-lasting-vehicles study ranked it #5 overall with 3.9% exceeding 200,000 miles; Consumer Reports reliability consistently above average. IIHS (2015): moderate-overlap Good, small-overlap Marginal, side and roof Good. N500: too new for long-term data; the T24A-FTS family (shared with N400 Tacoma) has no systemic defect documented yet.

knownIssues.label: Known issues
knownIssues.text: N280: no litigation-grade defect documented for 2015+ trucks; frame rust inspection is essential in the Rust Belt (family-wide Toyota truck issue); Takata airbag recalls apply; KDSS line leaks and X-REAS shock leaks are owner-reported at high mileage. The 5-speed powertrain is dated but considered bulletproof. N500: too new for patterns; early complaints mirror the N400 Tacoma's 8-speed shift quality — monitor as data accumulates.

maintenance.label: Maintenance
maintenance.text: Oil 0W-20 every 10,000 mi/12 mo (5,000 mi severe; N500 turbo — strict 10,000 mi max). Iridium plugs 120,000 mi. SLLC coolant 100,000/50,000 mi. ATF WS — inspect; drain-and-fill ~60,000 mi if towing or off-roading. Front/rear differential and transfer-case gear oil GL-5 75W-85 — inspect/replace. Tow rating: N280 5,000 lb; N500 6,000 lb max. All engines timing chain.

performance.label: Performance
performance.text: N280: 0–60 ~7.5–7.7 s (C&D) — slow by modern standards; off-road geometry, durability and the roll-down rear window are the point. TRD Pro (introduced MY2015) adds Bilstein remote-reservoir shocks, TRD springs and a skid plate. N500: no instrumented 0–60 figure verified in this pack — Not verified — needs OEM/instrumented source; the hybrid's 465 lb-ft transforms low-range work and highway passing versus N280.

mods.label: Modifications
mods.text: One of the strongest aftermarket ecosystems in the US: suspension lifts (Old Man Emu, Bilstein, Fox, Icon, King), 33–35-in tires, steel bumpers and winches (C4, CBI, Victory 4x4), skid plates/armor, roof racks and rooftop tents, regearing for larger tires, snorkels, auxiliary lighting; Magnuson offered a supercharger for the 1GR-FE. N500 support is ramping quickly thanks to shared GA-F hardware with the Tacoma.

engineering.label: Engineering
engineering.text: N280 dates to the MY2010 redesign: body-on-frame, solid rear axle, available KDSS. MY2014 facelift brought projector lamps, Optitron gauges, brake upgrades and Trailer Sway Control; MY2017 renamed Trail to TRD Off-Road; MY2020 made TSS-P and Apple CarPlay/Android Auto standard; MY2022 added TRD Sport (X-REAS, 20-in wheels); MY2023 offered the 40th Anniversary edition (4,040 units). N500 moves to TNGA GA-F (shared with N400 Tacoma and J250 Land Cruiser Prado) with a stiffer frame, retains the solid rear axle and roll-down rear window, and adds an available 14-in screen; TSS 3.0 is standard for 2026 across nine grades priced from $41,570 (Toyota pressroom).

enthusiast.label: Enthusiast
enthusiast.text: Huge community — r/4Runner, Toyota-4Runner.org, T4R.org; N280 TRD Pro resale values are famously strong; "mall crawler vs trail build" culture; 300,000-mile 1GR-FE examples are common bragging rights. N500 reception has been positive; the Trailhunter grade (Old Man Emu shocks, 33-in tires, snorkel) targets factory-built overlanders.

comparison.label: Comparison
comparison.text: Rivals: Jeep Wrangler, Ford Bronco, Jeep Grand Cherokee, Honda Passport, Land Rover Defender. N280 conceded power and refinement to Bronco/Wrangler but countered with unmatched durability and resale; N500 closes the powertrain gap — the hybrid's 465 lb-ft beats the Bronco 2.7L's 415 lb-ft — while keeping the Toyota reliability reputation. Buyers needing a third row are steered to the Highlander/Grand Highlander.

toyota-prius.subtitle: 5-door compact hybrid liftback — 2016–2022 (XW50, incl. Prius Prime PHEV 2017–2022), 2023–2026 (XW60, incl. Prime, renamed Plug-in Hybrid for 2025)

engine.label: Engine
engine.text: XW50: 1.8L 2ZR-FXE Atkinson-cycle I4 (95 hp / 105 lb-ft) + MG1/MG2 motor-generators, power-split Hybrid Synergy Drive, engine thermal efficiency over 40%; Prime uses the same engine with a larger traction motor and an 8.8-kWh Li-ion pack. XW60: 2.0L M20A-FXS Atkinson I4 (150 hp / 139 lb-ft @ 4,400–5,200 rpm, 5th-gen Toyota Hybrid System); Prime/Plug-in Hybrid pairs it with a larger motor and a 13.6-kWh Li-ion pack (10.9 kWh usable).

transmission.label: Transmission
transmission.text: All: planetary e-CVT (power-split — no conventional gearbox, no belts).

horsepower.label: Horsepower
horsepower.text: XW50: 121 hp combined (hybrid and Prime). XW60: 194 hp combined FWD / 196 hp AWD (hybrid); 220 hp combined (Prime/Plug-in Hybrid).

torque.label: Torque
torque.text: XW50: engine 105 lb-ft @ 3,600 rpm. XW60: engine 139 lb-ft @ 4,400–5,200 rpm; combined system torque not published by Toyota — Not verified — needs OEM source.

drivetrain.label: Drivetrain
drivetrain.text: XW50: FWD standard; AWD-e (rear 7-hp motor, 1.2-kWh NiMH battery) added for MY2019. XW60: FWD standard; electronic on-demand AWD (rear motor) optional on all US hybrid trims (standard in Canada); Prime/Plug-in Hybrid is FWD only.

mpg.label: Fuel economy (MPG)
mpg.text: XW50: standard 54/50/52; Eco 58/53/56; AWD-e ~50 combined (fueleconomy.gov). Prime (XW50): 133 MPGe combined, 55/53/54 gas-only, 25-mi EPA electric range. XW60: LE FWD 57/56/57; XLE/Limited FWD 52 combined; AWD 49–53 combined by trim (fueleconomy.gov). Prime/Plug-in Hybrid (XW60): SE 127 MPGe, 53/51/52 gas-only, 44-mi electric range; XSE/XSE Premium 114 MPGe, 50/47/48 gas-only, 39-mi electric range.

reliability.label: Reliability
reliability.text: XW50: Consumer Reports above average; a 2019 iSeeCars study ranked the Prius the #2 longest-kept vehicle among US drivers; IIHS 2022 Good in nearly all tests (passenger small-overlap and headlights Acceptable). XW60: IIHS Top Safety Pick+ (2023) with Good across crash tests; 2024 MotorTrend Car of the Year and 2024 North American Car of the Year; too new for long-term dependability data.

knownIssues.label: Known issues
knownIssues.text: XW50: no systemic defect documented; Denso low-pressure fuel-pump recall family (NHTSA 20V012 and expansions) covers 2018–2019 cars; Takata airbag recalls; owner-reported 12V battery and EGR/coolant quirks at high mileage. Prime (XW50): same recall families; early cars are 4-seat (5th seat restored for 2020). XW60: April 2024 recall of ~211,000 2023–2024 Prius/Prime worldwide — water can short the electronic rear door latches and let an unlocked rear door open while moving (remedied with revised switches).

maintenance.label: Maintenance
maintenance.text: Oil 0W-20 (XW50) / 0W-16 (XW60) every 10,000 mi/12 mo. Iridium plugs 120,000 mi. SLLC coolant 100,000/50,000 mi (engine and inverter loops). No conventional transmission service — e-CVT fluid inspect; hybrid-battery cooling-fan filter cleaning is cheap insurance (XW50). Brake fluid inspect; pads routinely last 100,000+ mi thanks to regenerative braking. All engines timing chain.

performance.label: Performance
performance.text: XW50: 0–60 ~9.8–10.5 s depending on trim/test (C&D/MT) — economy first. XW60: ~7.1–7.2 s (hybrid); Prime ~6.4–6.7 s — the quickest Prius ever. No factory performance trim; Nightshade (MY2022 XW50, MY2025 XW60) is cosmetic.

mods.label: Modifications
mods.text: Thin scene: lowering springs, wheels/tires, tint/PPF, roof racks; the economy-focused hypermiling community (PriusChat, CleanMPG) dominates over power mods. GR/Gazoo Racing aero and suspension parts exist for the XW60 in Japan; US uptake is minimal.

engineering.label: Engineering
engineering.text: XW50 was the first TNGA car (GA-C) — lower center of gravity, double-wishbone rear suspension, >40% thermal-efficiency engine; the Dec 2018 facelift (MY2019) restyled the lamps, renamed trims One/Two/Three/Four to L/LE/XLE/Limited and added AWD-e. XW60 rides on GA-C with a sleeker body (Cd 0.27 with lower frontal area), the 5th-gen hybrid system's higher-output motors, standard TSS 3.0, and a 44-mi-range Prime that offered a solar-charging roof option on XSE Premium (2023–2024).

enthusiast.label: Enthusiast
enthusiast.text: PriusChat is one of the oldest model forums on the internet; the culture centers on hypermiling, longevity (multiple documented 300,000–500,000-mile cars) and DIY battery care. The XW60's styling and the 220-hp Prime won over the enthusiast press (C&D 10Best 2024), softening the nameplate's appliance image.

comparison.label: Comparison
comparison.text: Rivals: Hyundai Elantra Hybrid, Honda Civic Hybrid (2025+), Kia Niro, and in-house Corolla Hybrid; Prime rivals: Kia Niro PHEV and price-overlapping entry EVs. The Prius counters with best-in-class mpg (57 combined), available AWD the Elantra/Civic hybrids don't offer, the strongest hybrid durability/resale record, and — for the Prime — a 44-mi electric range that covers most US commutes gas-free.

volkswagen-jetta.subtitle: 4-door compact sedan; Mk6/Mk6.5 (A6, Typ 1B, PQ35) 2015–2018; Mk7 (A7, MQB A1) 2019–2026 (facelifts 2022 and 2025)

engine.label: Engine
engine.text: Mk6 2015: 2.0L MPI EA113 8v I4 (115 hp, base S), 1.8T EA888 Gen3 1,798cc turbo I4 (170 hp), 2.0 TDI EA288 1,968cc turbo-diesel I4 (150 hp, 2015–2016 only — Dieselgate stop-sale), 1.4T EA211 Hybrid (170 hp combined, 2015–2016), GLI 2.0T EA888 Gen3 1,984cc (210 hp). Mk6.5 2016–2018: 1.4T EA211 1,395cc turbo I4 (150 hp) replaces 2.0L; 1.8T EA888 (170 hp) through 2017; GLI 2.0T (210 hp). Mk7 2019–2021: 1.4T EA211 (147 hp) only; GLI 2.0T EA888 Gen3 (228 hp). Mk7 facelift 2022–2026: 1.5T EA211 evo 1,498cc Miller-cycle VTG turbo I4 (158 hp); GLI 2.0T EA888 (228 hp).

transmission.label: Transmission
transmission.text: Mk6: 5-speed manual, 6-speed Aisin torque-converter automatic (09G Tiptronic) on 1.4T/1.8T, 6-speed DSG DQ250 on TDI/GLI, 7-speed DSG DQ200 on Hybrid. Mk7: 6-speed manual (S/R-Line/Sport, GLI), 8-speed Aisin AWF8F35 torque-converter automatic; GLI: 6MT or 7-speed DSG DQ381. Manual dropped from non-GLI Jetta for 2025 refresh; GLI manual ends after 2026 MY (DSG-only 2027).

drivetrain.label: Drivetrain
drivetrain.text: FWD only, all generations and trims (no AWD Jetta offered in the US 2015–2026).

dimensions.label: Dimensions
dimensions.text: Mk6: L 182.2–182.8 in / W 70.0 in / H 57.2–58.3 in / WB 104.3 in / curb 2,870–3,208 lb. Mk7: L 185.1 in / W 70.8 in / H 57.4 in / WB 105.7 in / curb 2,888–2,970 lb (base powertrain; GLI ≈3,300 lb — Not verified, needs OEM source).

reliability.label: Reliability
reliability.text: Mk6: J.D. Power 2015 Jetta 79/100 overall, Quality & Reliability 78/100 ("Average"); IIHS 2015 Top Safety Pick+; the simple 2.0L MPI/6AT is the durable low-risk powertrain. Mk7: J.D. Power 2020 Jetta consumer score 86/100; 742 NHTSA owner complaints logged for 2019–2025 (top areas: electrical system, engine, transmission); IIHS 2022 crashworthiness all "Good," headlights Marginal/Poor by trim.

knownIssues.label: Known issues
knownIssues.text: Mk6: EA888 Gen3 1.8T water pump/thermostat housing assembly leaks (common fail point from ~4 years/60k; OE part family 06L121111/06L121012); TDI models subject to Dieselgate stop-sale/buyback (EA288); panoramic/sunroof drain maintenance per factory schedule. Mk7: in-tank low-pressure fuel pump failures and recall coverage on 2019–2024 cars (stall risk; shares part family with Taos); NHTSA complaint clusters in electrical, engine, and transmission categories; early MIB infotainment glitches. The 8-speed Aisin automatic has proven robust.

maintenance.label: Maintenance
maintenance.text: VW factory schedule (US): oil/filter every 10,000 mi/12 mo alternating minor/standard services (VW 502 00 5W-40 on 1.4T Mk6.5; 508 00 0W-20 on later EA211 evo/EA888); DSG fluid + filter every 40,000 mi (DQ250/DQ381/DQ200); brake fluid first change at 3 years then every 2 years; spark plugs every 40,000 mi on 2.0T (40k–60k on 1.4T/1.5T — confirm against model-year booklet); coolant is long-life G12evo/G13 — check level/freeze protection at each standard service, no fixed US replacement interval; dust/pollen filter every 40,000 mi/2 yr; sunroof drains checked at services. Timing: EA888/EA113 chain-driven (no scheduled replacement); EA211 1.4T/1.5T belt-driven — inspect per schedule, no fixed US interval listed.

performance.label: Performance
performance.text: 0–60: Mk6 1.8T ≈7.9 s (C&D); Mk7 1.5T 7.6 s (C&D, 2022 SEL); Mk7 GLI 6.1 s (C&D, 2022 Autobahn 6MT); Mk6 GLI ≈6.1–6.4 s. Performance trims: GLI (all years; Mk7 GLI adds multi-link rear axle and VAQ electronically controlled limited-slip diff), Jetta Sport 1.8T (2015–2017, sport suspension), GLI Edition 30 (2014), GLI 35th Anniversary (2019), GLI 40th Anniversary Edition (2024).

mods.label: Modifications
mods.text: GLI is the tuner focus: APR/Unitronic/Cobb Stage 1–2 ECU tunes on the EA888 Gen3 (large gains, IS20/IS38 turbo swaps), downpipes, intercoolers, cat-back exhausts; lowering springs/coilovers and sway bars from the VW aftermarket (ShopDAP, UroTuning catalogs); 1.4T/1.5T EA211 tunes exist but gains are modest. Factory performance variants: GLI Autobahn (DCC adaptive dampers on Mk7), anniversary editions above.

engineering.label: Engineering
engineering.text: The Mk6 (NCS) was VW's cost-optimized North American sedan on a PQ35 derivative with a twist-beam rear axle on lower trims (multi-link restored across the US range for the 2015 facelift; GLI always multi-link). The Mk7 moved to MQB A1 with a longer wheelbase and body, standard LED lighting, and available Digital Cockpit, but reverted non-GLI trims to a torsion-beam rear axle; GLI keeps multi-link + VAQ e-LSD. The 2022 facelift brought the EA211 evo 1.5T (Miller cycle, variable-geometry turbo) replacing the long-serving 1.4T.

enthusiast.label: Enthusiast
enthusiast.text: The GLI is viewed as "a GTI with a trunk" and has deep aftermarket support; it was also the last manual-transmission VW sold in North America. Base Jettas are seen as value commuters — the Mk6 drew criticism for decontented interiors, while the Mk7 is respected for refinement-per-dollar but ignored by most enthusiasts outside the GLI.

comparison.label: Comparison
comparison.text: Rivals: Honda Civic, Toyota Corolla, Mazda3, Hyundai Elantra. The Jetta counters with low-end turbo torque, a roomy back seat/trunk, and autobahn-flavored ride tuning; long-term reliability trails Corolla/Civic, but the GLI undercuts the Civic Si and GTI on price while matching their pace.

volkswagen-passat.subtitle: 4-door midsize sedan; NMS Gen 1 (A32/A33, PQ46, Chattanooga) 2015–2019 (2016 facelift); NMS Gen 2 (2020 reskin, still PQ46) 2020–2022 — US Passat discontinued after 2022 MY (Limited Edition, 1,973 units)

engine.label: Engine
engine.text: 2015–2017: 1.8T EA888 Gen3 1,798cc turbo I4 (170 hp); 2015–2016: 2.0 TDI EA288 1,968cc turbo-diesel I4 (150 hp — Dieselgate stop-sale); 2012–2018: 3.6 VR6 FSI EA390 3,597cc narrow-angle V6 (280 hp); 2018–2019: 2.0T EA888 Gen3 1,984cc (174 hp) replaces 1.8T; 2020–2022: 2.0T EA888 Gen 3B "B-cycle" (174 hp) only engine. (2.5L EA855 I5 ended 2013 — pre-scope.)

transmission.label: Transmission
transmission.text: 1.8T: 5-speed manual (2014–2015) or 6-speed Aisin torque-converter automatic (09G); 2.0T (2018–2022): 6-speed Aisin automatic only; VR6: 6-speed DSG DQ250; TDI: 6-speed manual or 6-speed DSG DQ250. No 8-speed or DSG on the 2020–2022 car.

torque.label: Torque
torque.text: 1.8T: 184 lb-ft. 2.0T: 184 lb-ft (2018–19, Wikipedia spec table); 2020–22 advertised at 206 lb-ft — Not verified against retrieved OEM source, confirm on VW media site. TDI: 236 lb-ft @ 1,750. VR6: 258 lb-ft.

drivetrain.label: Drivetrain
drivetrain.text: FWD only, all years/trims (no AWD US Passat NMS).

dimensions.label: Dimensions
dimensions.text: Gen 1: L 191.7 in / W 72.2 in / H 58.0 in / WB 110.4 in / curb ≈3,260 lb (1.8T, 1,480 kg) to 3,571 lb (VR6, 1,620 kg) per Carfolio conversions. Gen 2: L 191.9 in / W 72.2 in / H 58.5 in / WB 110.4 in / curb 3,276–3,369 lb (1,486–1,528 kg, Carfolio).

mpg.label: Fuel economy (MPG)
mpg.text: 2016–2017 1.8T 6AT: 25/38/29 (highway 38 per EPA figure cited in NMS documentation). 2018 2.0T: 25/36/29. 2018 VR6: 19/28/22. 2020 2.0T: 23/34/27 (fueleconomy.gov). TDI (2015): EA288 ~31/42/35 manual — Not verified this session, needs OEM source.

reliability.label: Reliability
reliability.text: The NMS is regarded as one of VW's more trouble-free US products of the era — simple, proven powertrains and a conventional automatic; Motor Trend 2012 Car of the Year; Car and Driver midsize comparison winner (March 2012). Weak points tracked below. NHTSA recall: 2016–2019 front seat-back adjustment levers may not engage (injury risk in crash). Takata airbag recalls touch most model years (check VIN at nhtsa.gov/recalls).

knownIssues.label: Known issues
knownIssues.text: EA888 Gen3 water pump/thermostat housing assembly leaks (common fail point, as early as 4 years; 06L121111/06L121012 part family) on 1.8T/2.0T; intake-valve carbon buildup on direct-injection EA888 at higher mileage (owner-documented); TDI models caught in Dieselgate (buyback/fix; verify emissions campaign completion before service upsell); 2016–2019 seat-recliner lever recall; the discontinued 2.5L I5 (pre-2015) was the durable fleet favorite.

maintenance.label: Maintenance
maintenance.text: VW factory schedule (US): oil/filter every 10,000 mi/12 mo (VW 502 00 spec); DSG fluid + filter every 40,000 mi (VR6/TDI DQ250); brake fluid first at 3 years then every 2 years; spark plugs every 40,000 mi (2.0T; 1.8T per booklet 40k–60k — confirm by model year); long-life coolant, level/freeze check each standard service; Passat-specific: sunroof water drains checked/cleaned at every service per factory schedule. Timing: all NMS engines chain-driven (no scheduled replacement). TDI additionally: DEF/AdBlue service (SCR system on EA288).

performance.label: Performance
performance.text: 0–60: 1.8T ≈7.7–7.9 s; 2020–2022 2.0T ≈7.8–8.0 s; VR6 ≈6.4 s (period MotorTrend test — Not re-verified this session, needs OEM source). Performance-adjacent trims: 2018 Passat GT (VR6, sport suspension, 19-in wheels, black roof), R-Line appearance trims.

mods.label: Modifications
mods.text: Thin enthusiast scene. ECU tunes exist for the 2020–2024 2.0T EA888 Gen 3B (e.g., Boost Dynamic Tuning Stage 1–2, remote OBD flash — VW detuned this engine to 174 hp, leaving headroom); VR6 cars get intake/exhaust work; otherwise mostly maintenance-oriented ownership.

engineering.label: Engineering
engineering.text: The NMS was purpose-built for North America on a stretched PQ46 platform in Chattanooga with 80% US-sourced content — 3.9 in longer than the European B7 with a huge rear seat. The 2016 facelift added MIB II infotainment, LED lighting options, and driver assists. Notably, the 2020 "new" Passat was a reskin on the same 2011-vintage PQ46 architecture rather than MQB — an explicit cost decision late in the nameplate's life.

enthusiast.label: Enthusiast
enthusiast.text: Minimal enthusiast following; the VR6 and 2018 GT have a small niche as sleepers. The community values NMS Passats as cheap, roomy highway cars, and Dieselgate-era TDIs have a fuel-miser cult (2013 TDI held a Guinness 48-state economy record).

comparison.label: Comparison
comparison.text: Rivals: Toyota Camry, Honda Accord, Nissan Altima, Ford Fusion, Hyundai Sonata. The Passat wins on rear-seat space and ride comfort; it loses on resale and long-term reliability perception versus Camry/Accord, which is why clean examples are bargain used buys — verify water pump and carbon-cleaning history.

volkswagen-tiguan.subtitle: 5-door compact SUV; Mk1 (5N, PQ46) 2015–2018 (sold 2017–2018 as Tiguan Limited alongside Mk2); Mk2 (AD/BW, MQB A2, LWB-only US) 2018–2024 (2022 facelift); Mk3 (CT1, MQB Evo) 2025–2026

engine.label: Engine
engine.text: Mk1 (2015–2018 incl. Limited): 2.0 TSI EA888 Gen 2 (CCTA/CBFA) 1,984cc turbo I4. Mk2 (2018–2024): 2.0 TSI EA888 Gen 3B "B-cycle" (Budack) 1,984cc turbo I4. Mk3 (2025–2026): 2.0 TSI EA888 evo4 1,984cc turbo I4. Single engine offered per generation in the US.

transmission.label: Transmission
transmission.text: Mk1: 6-speed Aisin torque-converter automatic (09M). Mk2: 8-speed Aisin torque-converter automatic (only US gearbox — no DSG). Mk3: 8-speed automatic. All with FWD standard; no manual offered in the US.

horsepower.label: Horsepower
horsepower.text: Mk1: 200 hp (US rating; 200 PS/197 hp metric). Mk2: 184 hp. Mk3: 201 hp @ 5,000.

drivetrain.label: Drivetrain
drivetrain.text: FWD standard, 4Motion AWD (Haldex coupling) optional on all generations. Mk2: third-row seat standard on FWD, optional on AWD until it was dropped for the 2022 facelift; Mk3 is two-row/5-seat only in the US.

dimensions.label: Dimensions
dimensions.text: Mk1: L 174.3–174.5 in / W 71.2 in / H 65.6–66.4 in / WB 102.5 in / curb weight — Not verified, needs OEM source (≈3,400–3,800 lb est.). Mk2 (US LWB): L 185.5–185.8 in / W 72.4–73.2 in / H 65.9 in / WB 109.9 in / curb 3,825 lb (AWD, 1,735 kg); FWD lighter — exact figure needs OEM source. Mk3: L 184.4 in / W 73.0 in / H 66.5 in / WB 109.9 in / curb 3,801 lb (AWD base, J.D. Power); ~170 lb lighter than Mk2 per VW (aluminum suspension content).

mpg.label: Fuel economy (MPG)
mpg.text: Mk1/Tiguan Limited 2018 (same powertrain): 20/26/23 (FWD), 19/25/22 (AWD), premium fuel. Mk2 2018: 22/27/24 (FWD), 21/27/23 (AWD). Mk2 2024 SEL R-Line 4Motion: 22/29 EPA C/H label (C&D observed 26). Mk3 2025: 26/34/29 (FWD S, fueleconomy.gov), 22/30 (AWD; combined 25–26).

reliability.label: Reliability
reliability.text: Mk1: weakest of the family — Consumer Reports reliability below average for the era; NHTSA 2016 AWD 5-star overall; IIHS small-overlap "Marginal." Mk2: improved but still mid-pack; Mexican-built LWB scored 5 stars Latin NCAP (2019) with AEB/pedestrian awards; MIB2 electronics complaints common. Mk3: too new for meaningful data; IIHS 2025 testing returned a "Poor" updated moderate-overlap front rating (other crashworthiness tests Good) — noteworthy for a family SUV.

knownIssues.label: Known issues
knownIssues.text: Mk1: timing chain tensioner failure on the 2.0T CCTA/CBFA — documented in VW TSB (2012) and a US class-action settlement (timingchain-settlement.com) covering 2008–2013 vehicles; revised tensioner fitted from ~2014, but chain/tensioner inspection remains mandatory diligence on any Mk1; water pump failures also documented. Mk2: water pump/thermostat housing leaks (EA888 Gen 3 family part issue); MIB2 touchscreen delamination/digitizer failure on 2018–2021 cars (ghost touch/dead touch — aftermarket digitizer fixes exist, dealer replaces head unit); panoramic sunroof drains need periodic cleaning per schedule. Mk3: none documented yet (new model).

maintenance.label: Maintenance
maintenance.text: VW factory schedule (US): oil/filter every 10,000 mi/12 mo (502 00; 508 00 0W-20 on Mk3); brake fluid first at 3 years then every 2 years; spark plugs ~40,000 mi on 2.0T (confirm per model year); long-life coolant with level/freeze checks; DSG not fitted to US cars — no DSG service needed; 4Motion models: Haldex coupling oil service every 3 years (confirm against model-year booklet); transfer/rear-final-drive checks at standard services; sunroof drains checked at services. Timing: all engines chain-driven (Mk1 tensioner is a wear item, see Known issues).

performance.label: Performance
performance.text: 0–60: Mk1 — Not verified, needs OEM source (period tests ≈8 s); Mk2 8.2 s (C&D, 2024 SEL R-Line 4Motion); Mk3 7.7 s (C&D, 2025 SE). Factory performance variant: Tiguan R (320 PS/316 hp, torque-vectoring AWD, 21-in wheels, Akrapovič option) — Europe only, never sold in the US; US gets R-Line appearance/trim packages only. Mk3 tows 1,800 lb (AWD).

mods.label: Modifications
mods.text: Light but real scene: Stage 1 ECU tunes on the Mk2 EA888 Gen 3B (APR/Unitronic) unlock meaningful torque; Mk1 2.0T responds to the mature EA888 Gen 2 tuning catalog; typical bolt-ons are intake, intercooler, cat-back, lowering springs, and wheels from the VW aftermarket (ShopDAP/UroTuning). Mk3 support is emerging.

engineering.label: Engineering
engineering.text: The Mk1 sat on PQ46 (Passat B6-related — commonly misreported as PQ35) and was imported from Germany. The Mk2 moved to MQB A2 and, in the US, is sold only as the Mexico-built long-wheelbase body with a conventional 8-speed automatic replacing the DSGs used elsewhere — a deliberate durability/cost choice. The Mk3 adopts MQB Evo with more aluminum in the suspension and high-strength hot-formed steel, cutting ~170 lb, and moves to MIB4 electronics with a 12.9-in screen.

enthusiast.label: Enthusiast
enthusiast.text: Not a cult vehicle — the community sees it as the practical family VW with GTI-adjacent drivetrain DNA; the forbidden-fruit Tiguan R is the enthusiast talking point. Mk1 Limiteds are cheap used entries with known tensioner baggage; Mk2s are valued for space and refinement rather than speed.

comparison.label: Comparison
comparison.text: Rivals: Toyota RAV4, Honda CR-V, Mazda CX-5, Hyundai Tucson, Subaru Forester. The Tiguan counters with the best ride/refinement balance and standard turbo torque; it trails RAV4/CR-V on reliability record and (pre-2025) on fuel economy — the Mk3's 26/34 FWD rating largely closes the MPG gap.

volkswagen-golf.subtitle: 5-door hatchback; Mk7/Mk7.5 (MQB) 2015–2021 (incl. GTI, Golf R, SportWagen/Alltrack, e-Golf, 2015 TDI); Mk8/Mk8.5 (MQB Evo) 2022–2026 — US Mk8 range is GTI and Golf R only (facelift/Mk8.5 from 2025 MY)

engine.label: Engine
engine.text: Mk7 Golf (2015–2017): 1.8T EA888 Gen3 1,798cc turbo I4 (170 hp); 2015 only: 2.0 TDI EA288 (150 hp — Dieselgate stop-sale); e-Golf BEV (115 hp/136 hp). Mk7.5 Golf (2018): 1.8T carried over; (2019–2021): 1.4T EA211 1,395cc (147 hp) replaces 1.8T. GTI (all Mk7/Mk7.5): 2.0T EA888 Gen3 1,984cc (210 hp 2015–2017; 220 hp 2018; 228 hp 2019–2021). Golf R Mk7/7.5: 2.0T EA888 Gen3 (292 hp US, 2015–2019). Mk8 GTI (2022–2024): 2.0T EA888 evo4 (241 hp). Mk8 Golf R (2022–2024): 2.0T EA888 evo4 (315 hp). Mk8.5 (2025–2026): GTI 261 hp; Golf R 328 hp. SportWagen/Alltrack (2015–2019): 1.8T 170 hp, FWD or 4Motion.

transmission.label: Transmission
transmission.text: Golf 1.8T: 5MT or 6-speed Aisin auto; Golf 1.4T (2019+): 6MT or 8-speed Aisin auto. GTI: 6MT or 6-speed DSG DQ250 (2015–2018), 7-speed DSG DQ381 (2019+); Mk8 GTI 6MT/7DSG — manual discontinued after 2024 (2024 GTI 380 special edition marked the MT farewell; Mk8.5 GTI is DSG-only). Golf R: 6MT or 6DSG (Mk7)/7DSG (Mk7.5+); Mk8 R 6MT (NA-only) or 7DSG — manual dropped for Mk8.5. SportWagen/Alltrack 4Motion: 6MT or 6-speed DSG DQ250.

drivetrain.label: Drivetrain
drivetrain.text: Golf/GTI: FWD only. Golf R: 4Motion AWD — Haldex 5th-gen (Mk7), updated system with torque-vectoring rear differential and Drift Mode (Mk8). SportWagen: FWD standard, 4Motion optional from 2017; Alltrack: 4Motion standard.

dimensions.label: Dimensions
dimensions.text: Mk7 hatch: L 167.5–168.5 in / W 70.5–70.8 in / H 56.5–59.6 in / WB 103.6–103.8 in (103.1–103.5 facelift) / curb 2,657–3,307 lb (family incl. wagons/AWD). Mk8 hatch: L 168.7 in / W 70.4 in / H 57.3 in / WB 103.8 in / curb 2,767–3,230 lb.

reliability.label: Reliability
reliability.text: Mk7 is one of the stronger VW reliability stories of the decade — 2015 North American Car of the Year and Motor Trend Car of the Year; IIHS 2021 Golf all-"Good" crashworthiness (headlights Poor); CR rated Mk7 Golf/GTI average to above average. Mk8: strong crash/safety showing — 2022 GTI IIHS Top Safety Pick, 2022 Golf R Top Safety Pick+ — but dragged by electronics: the MIB3 infotainment and haptic controls are widely criticized ("frustrating infotainment system" — U.S. News; "a step backwards" — Which?). JD Power/CR place Mk8 at about average, with complaints concentrated in software, not powertrain.

knownIssues.label: Known issues
knownIssues.text: Mk7/7.5: EA888 Gen3 water pump/thermostat housing leaks (1.8T, GTI, R — the generation's signature repair, OE 06L121111/06L121012 family); intake-valve carbon buildup on DI engines at mileage; sunroof drain clogs causing water ingress (documented on GTI/Golf/R); 2015 TDI Dieselgate (stop-sale/buyback/fix); DQ250 DSG mechatronic wear at high mileage. Mk8: MIB3 software glitches (lag, blank screens, CarPlay dropouts), touch-slider/haptic control frustration (Mk8.5 restores physical steering-wheel buttons), early build software recalls/updates; powertrain (EA888 evo4) has been largely trouble-free to date.

maintenance.label: Maintenance
maintenance.text: VW factory schedule (US): oil/filter every 10,000 mi/12 mo (502 00 on Mk7; 508 00 0W-20 on Mk8); DSG fluid + filter every 40,000 mi (DQ250/DQ381 — GTI/R/SportWagen/Alltrack); brake fluid first at 3 years then every 2 years; spark plugs every 40,000 mi on 2.0T (GTI/R), 1.8T/1.4T per booklet (40k–60k — confirm by model year); long-life coolant, level/freeze checks at services; AWD models: Haldex coupling oil every 3 years. Timing: EA888 chain-driven; EA211 1.4T belt-driven (inspect per schedule, no fixed US interval). e-Golf: EV-specific schedule, no engine service.

performance.label: Performance
performance.text: 0–60: GTI Mk7 5.9–6.3 s (official 0–100 km/h 6.3–6.5); Mk8 GTI 5.2–5.9 s across instrumented tests (C&D/MT data points: 5.2 best, 5.6–5.9 typical); Golf R Mk7 4.9 s (official, DSG); Mk8 R 4.7 s official (0–100 km/h) with magazine tests in the 3.9–4.6 s range. Performance variants: GTI Performance Package (VAQ LSD, bigger brakes — standard from 2019 in the US), GTI Rabbit Edition (2019), GTI 380 (2024, MT send-off), Golf R 20 Years (2022)/R 333 (2023, not US); Mk7 GTI Clubsport/Clubsport S/TCR not sold in the US.

mods.label: Modifications
mods.text: The deepest tuner ecosystem in the segment: Cobb Accessport (Mk8 support), APR/Unitronic Stage 1–3 ECU/TCU tunes, IS38 turbo swaps on GTI, downpipes, intercoolers, cat-backs; coilovers (e.g., Godspeed MonoRS Mk8), sway bars, camber plates; DSG tunes to hold added torque. Golf R Stage 2 cars commonly exceed 400 hp. e-Golf and 1.4T Golf see light mods (wheels, springs).

engineering.label: Engineering
engineering.text: Mk7 was the first MQB-platform Golf — lighter and roomier than Mk6, with the EA888 Gen3 family across GTI/R; the 2018 facelift (Mk7.5) added digital cockpit options and bumped GTI output. Mk8 moved to MQB Evo (Cd 0.275 vs 0.29), fully digital cockpit, shift-by-wire DSG, Travel Assist, and Car2X networking, plus a Vehicle Dynamics Manager integrating VAQ diff and DCC dampers; the 2025 Mk8.5 facelift added MIB4, more power (GTI 261 hp, R 328 hp), and restored physical steering-wheel buttons after customer backlash.

enthusiast.label: Enthusiast
enthusiast.text: The GTI is the benchmark hot hatch and the center of the VW scene in North America — plaid seats, annual shows, and unmatched parts support; the Golf R is the cult all-weather weapon (473 Spektrum custom-color Mk7 Rs built for NA). The Mk8's software stung its reputation early, but the Mk8.5 power bump and button restoration have rehabilitated it; losing the manual after 2024 is the community's big grievance.

comparison.label: Comparison
comparison.text: Rivals: Honda Civic Si/Type R, Hyundai Elantra N, Subaru WRX, Mazda3, Toyota GR Corolla (R territory). GTI remains the refinement/balance pick — quieter and more adult than Elantra N, sharper than WRX; Golf R trades the Type R's edge for year-round usability and stealth. Reliability is mid-pack — better than older VWs' reputation, behind the Hondas/Toyotas, with electronics, not engines, the main complaint on Mk8.

volkswagen-atlas.subtitle: 3-row midsize SUV (Atlas) and 2-row fastback midsize SUV (Atlas Cross Sport); Gen 1 (CA1, MQB) 2018–2026, built Chattanooga, TN; facelift 2021, major refresh 2024; Cross Sport added 2020 MY

engine.label: Engine
engine.text: 2018–2023: 2.0T EA888 Gen3 (DCGA) 1,984cc turbo I4 (235 hp) or 3.6 VR6 EA390 (CDVC) 3,598cc narrow-angle V6 (276 hp). VR6 dropped after 2023 (EPA emissions rules); 2024 refresh+: updated 2.0T EA888 evo4 (269 hp) is the only engine on both body styles.

transmission.label: Transmission
transmission.text: 8-speed torque-converter automatic only, all years and trims (2024+ cars switch to a Hyundai-sourced 8-speed unit per Wikipedia); no manual, no DSG.

drivetrain.label: Drivetrain
drivetrain.text: FWD standard; 4Motion AWD optional on all trims and both body styles, all years.

dimensions.label: Dimensions
dimensions.text: Atlas: L 198.3 in / W 78.3 in / H 70.0 in / WB 117.3 in / curb 4,109–4,511 lb (2024 SEL Premium R-Line 4Motion tested at 4,623 lb — C&D). Atlas Cross Sport: L 195.5 in / H 67.7 in, same 117.3-in WB. Max towing 5,000 lb with factory tow package; base 4-cylinder tow rating — Not verified, needs OEM source.

mpg.label: Fuel economy (MPG)
mpg.text: 2018: 2.0T FWD 22/26/24; VR6 FWD 18/25/20; VR6 AWD 17/23/19. 2021: Atlas 2.0T FWD 21/24/22; 2.0T 4Motion 20/24/22; VR6 FWD 17/23/19; VR6 4Motion 16/22/18; Cross Sport 2.0T FWD 21/24/22, 2.0T 4Motion 20/24/22, VR6 FWD 18/23/20, VR6 4Motion 17/23/19. 2024 (2.0T only): FWD 20/27/23 (Atlas and Cross Sport); 4Motion SE 19/26/22, SEL 19/25/21, Peak Edition 18/24/20, Cross Sport 4Motion 19/26/22.

reliability.label: Reliability
reliability.text: IIHS Top Safety Pick for 2018; heavy first-generation recall footprint (see Known issues) concentrated in airbags/occupant detection and fuel-system hardware; mechanically the VR6 is regarded as robust, while the EA888's water pump is the known wear item. J.D. Power/Consumer Reports scores — Not verified this session, needs OEM source.

knownIssues.label: Known issues
knownIssues.text: Recalls: 18V284 (2018) fuel tank may leak from a manufacturing error — replace tank; September 2018 campaign on 9,685 Atlas SUVs for water ingress that could trigger inadvertent airbag deployment (plus related airbag/brake/seat-anchor actions per Consumer Reports); 69AL (2018) mis-built airbag crash sensors — delayed or absent frontal airbag deployment; 69PZ passenger occupant detection system (PODS) contact fault switches off the passenger airbag — 2021–2024 Atlas and 2020–2024 Atlas Cross Sport (action announced by NHTSA April 2023). Mechanical: EA888 Gen3 water pump/thermostat housing leaks (family issue, same OE part family 06L121111/06L121012 as Jetta/Passat/Tiguan).

maintenance.label: Maintenance
maintenance.text: VW factory schedule (US): oil/filter every 10,000 mi/12 mo (VW 508 00 0W-20 on later EA888); brake fluid first change at 3 years then every 2 years; spark plugs per model-year booklet (40k–60k by engine — confirm against booklet); coolant long-life G12evo/G13 — check level/freeze protection at each standard service, no fixed US replacement interval; dust/pollen filter every 40,000 mi/2 yr. Both engines chain-driven — no timing-belt service. 8-speed automatic fluid service interval not listed in the retrieved schedule — Not verified, needs OEM source.

performance.label: Performance
performance.text: 0–60: 2.0T 7.2 s (2018 TSI SE — C&D, fastest recorded Atlas); VR6 4Motion 7.8–8.0 s (2019 SEL V6, C&D); 2024 269-hp SEL Premium R-Line 4Motion 7.3–7.5 s, quarter-mile 15.4 s @ 92 mph (C&D/Motor Trend/Motor Week). No factory performance variant; R-Line is cosmetic; Peak Edition (2024+) adds off-road styling and all-terrain tires.

mods.label: Modifications
mods.text: Aftermarket is modest next to VW's cars: the 2.0T shares the EA888 tuning ecosystem (stage-1/2 ECU tunes, downpipes, intercoolers) with GTI/GLI/Tiguan; VR6 tuning support is limited; most builds are wheel/tire packages, leveling kits, and tow accessories. Not verified against a dedicated Atlas tuning source this session.

engineering.label: Engineering
engineering.text: First VW designed primarily for North America, built on MQB at Chattanooga with transverse engines; strut front/multi-link rear suspension. The Cross Sport (2020) is a 2-row fastback derivative on the same wheelbase; the 2021 facelift synchronized exterior styling. The 2024 refresh brought a revised interior (standard digital cockpit, larger infotainment), the 269-hp EA888 evo4 replacing both prior engines, and the Hyundai-sourced 8-speed; a second generation (Teramont Pro, MQB Evo, 2027 MY) was unveiled in 2026 — outside this pack's scope.

enthusiast.label: Enthusiast
enthusiast.text: Not an enthusiast model — the appeal is space, value, and US assembly; community interest centers on towing, family road trips, and light overlanding-style builds, and the VR6's retirement was mourned more for its simplicity than its speed.

comparison.label: Comparison
comparison.text: Rivals: Honda Pilot, Toyota Highlander/Grand Highlander, Hyundai Palisade, Kia Telluride, Ford Explorer, Chevrolet Traverse. The Atlas counters with generous three-row space and a composed ride, but offers no hybrid (rival hybrids beat it on fuel economy) and its recall record trails the segment's Japanese/Korean benchmarks.

volkswagen-id-4.subtitle: 5-seat battery-electric compact SUV; MEB platform; US 2021–2026; built Zwickau, Germany (2021–2022) and Chattanooga, TN (2023 MY on)

engine.label: Engine
engine.text: Battery-electric — no combustion engine. Rear permanent-magnet synchronous motor: APP 310 (201 hp) on 2021–2023 82-kWh RWD and all 62-kWh Standard cars; AWD adds a front asynchronous motor for 295 hp combined (2021–2023). 2024+: APP 550 rear motor (282 hp RWD; 335 hp AWD combined).

transmission.label: Transmission
transmission.text: Single-speed reduction gear (direct drive) at each driven axle — no multi-gear transmission, no clutch.

drivetrain.label: Drivetrain
drivetrain.text: RWD (single rear motor) standard; dual-motor AWD (front induction + rear permanent-magnet) optional on 82-kWh trims.

dimensions.label: Dimensions
dimensions.text: L 180.5 in / W 72.9 in / H 64.4 in / WB 108.9 in / curb 4,169–4,903 lb depending on battery/drivetrain.

reliability.label: Reliability
reliability.text: IIHS Top Safety Pick+ 2021–2023; 2021 World Car of the Year; recall record (below) is the main drag — concentrated in door handles, software, and the 2023–2025 high-voltage battery action; a 100,000-mile standard battery warranty is referenced in recall-coverage discussions (VWIDTalk) — confirm terms against the OEM warranty booklet.

knownIssues.label: Known issues
knownIssues.text: Recalls: door-handle water ingress can let doors open unexpectedly while driving — 2021–2024, VW recall 57J9 (handles replaced + software update, paired with software campaign 919A; expands earlier door-handle campaigns); high-voltage battery module thermal-propagation/fire risk — 2023–2025, VW 93EA / NHTSA 26V030, 43,881 vehicles (dealer software update + battery replacement if needed; owner notifications from March 2026); 2021 HV-battery cell soldering defect (22V-162). Non-recall: widespread early MIB infotainment/software glitches (improved via OTA updates); capacitive controls criticized. Sealed rear drum brakes are maintenance-friendly but surprise some owners.

maintenance.label: Maintenance
maintenance.text: No oil changes, spark plugs, or timing belt/chain. Routine items: cabin dust/pollen filter, brake fluid (first change at 3 years, then every 2), battery/inverter coolant system checks, and reduction-gear oil per the EV service schedule — itemized intervals beyond brake fluid Not verified against the VW BEV booklet this session, needs OEM source. Tires, the 12-V battery, and wiper blades are the regular consumables; service-brake wear is low (regenerative braking; sealed rear drums).

performance.label: Performance
performance.text: 0–60: RWD 7.7 s (201 hp, 2021–2023); RWD 5.9 s (282 hp, 2024+); AWD 5.7 s (295 hp, 2021–2023); AWD 4.9 s (335 hp, 2024+). No US performance variant offered in this period.

mods.label: Modifications
mods.text: Minimal by VW standards — no ECU-tune market like the EA888 cars; typical mods are wheel/tire packages, lowering springs from the shared MEB aftermarket, roof boxes, and wraps. Not verified against a dedicated ID.4 tuning source this session.

engineering.label: Engineering
engineering.text: MEB dedicated-BEV skateboard platform with a rear-motor/RWD-biased layout; 62-kWh (58 usable, SK On cells) and 82-kWh (77 usable) packs; 400-V architecture; sealed rear drum brakes sized for low wear with regen. US production moved to Chattanooga for the 2023 MY (62-kWh Standard added, 209-mi EPA); the 2024 refresh brought the stronger APP 550 motor and revised software/infotainment.

enthusiast.label: Enthusiast
enthusiast.text: Regarded as a comfortable, spacious commuter EV rather than an enthusiast machine; the owner community (VWIDTalk) is highly active on software/OTA versions and recall campaigns; value after federal/lease incentives is its strongest talking point.

comparison.label: Comparison
comparison.text: Rivals: Tesla Model Y, Hyundai Ioniq 5, Kia EV6, Ford Mustang Mach-E, Toyota bZ4X. The ID.4 counters with ride comfort, interior space, and aggressive pricing/incentives; it trails the Model Y and the 800-V Korean EVs on charging speed and software polish, and its recall cadence has been heavier.

volkswagen-taos.subtitle: 5-seat subcompact SUV; MQB A1; built Puebla, Mexico; US 2022–2026; mid-cycle refresh for 2025 MY

engine.label: Engine
engine.text: 1.5L EA211 evo2 TSI 1,498cc Miller-cycle VTG turbo I4 — 158 hp (2022–2024); updated to 174 hp for the 2025 refresh (same EA211 evo family).

transmission.label: Transmission
transmission.text: 2022–2024: FWD = 8-speed Aisin torque-converter automatic; 4Motion = 7-speed DSG. 2025+: 8-speed automatic standard across the range (DSG dropped).

torque.label: Torque
torque.text: 184 lb-ft @ 1,750, all years.

drivetrain.label: Drivetrain
drivetrain.text: FWD standard; 4Motion AWD optional on all trims.

dimensions.label: Dimensions
dimensions.text: L 175.8 in / W 72.5 in / H 63.1–64.4 in / WB 105.9 in / curb 3,175 lb (2022 FWD, JATO); 2025 test weights 3,208 lb (SE Black FWD, C&D) and 3,462 lb (SEL 4Motion, Motor Trend).

reliability.label: Reliability
reliability.text: IIHS 2022 crashworthiness mostly "Good," passenger-side small overlap "Acceptable"; Latin NCAP 5 stars (2021); the owner-complaint pattern centers on transmission behavior rather than hardware failures (see Known issues). J.D. Power/Consumer Reports scores — Not verified this session, needs OEM source.

knownIssues.label: Known issues
knownIssues.text: 1.5 TSI low-speed hesitation/"punching" on tip-in (software-related; documented by CarAraC); 4Motion 7-speed DSG low-speed jerkiness and inconsistent shifts (KBB owner reviews); in-tank low-pressure fuel pump failures shared with the 2019–2024 Jetta part family (coverage on 2022–2025 Taos per go-parts).

maintenance.label: Maintenance
maintenance.text: VW factory schedule (US): oil/filter every 10,000 mi/12 mo (VW 508 00 0W-20); DSG fluid + filter every 40,000 mi (4Motion, 2022–2024); brake fluid first change at 3 years then every 2 years; coolant long-life — check at standard services, no fixed US replacement interval; dust/pollen filter every 40,000 mi/2 yr. EA211 is belt-driven — inspect per schedule, no fixed US interval listed.

performance.label: Performance
performance.text: 0–60: 7.4 s (2022 SEL; 2023 SEL 4Motion — Motor Trend, fastest recorded; quarter-mile 15.7 s @ 87.4 mph); 2025 refresh: 8.0–8.2 s (SE Black FWD, C&D), 8.9 s (SEL 4Motion, MT). No factory performance variant.

mods.label: Modifications
mods.text: Light aftermarket: shares the Mk7 Jetta 1.5T's EA211 tuning ecosystem (stage-1 ECU tunes, intake/exhaust) with modest gains; springs/coilovers and wheel/tire packages from the VW aftermarket are the common path.

engineering.label: Engineering
engineering.text: MQB A1 sibling of the Mk7 Jetta, named after Taos, New Mexico; slots below the Tiguan as VW's US entry SUV with an unusually long 105.9-in wheelbase for the class; standard Digital Cockpit. The 2025 refresh revised styling, raised output to 174 hp, and replaced the 4Motion DSG with the 8-speed automatic across the board — answering the main drivability criticism.

enthusiast.label: Enthusiast
enthusiast.text: Not an enthusiast model; respected as a roomy, efficient entry SUV, with community discussion dominated by low-speed drivability fixes (software updates; some owners report smoother behavior on 89-octane fuel).

comparison.label: Comparison
comparison.text: Rivals: Honda HR-V, Toyota Corolla Cross, Mazda CX-30, Hyundai Kona, Kia Seltos, Subaru Crosstrek. The Taos counters with class-above rear-seat and cargo room and strong highway MPG; pre-refresh 4Motion DSG drivability trails rivals' conventional automatics/CVTs — a gap the 2025 8-speed swap was meant to close.

acura-tlx-type-s.subtitle: Midsize sport sedan — 2015–2020 (1st gen UB1/UB2/UB3, facelifted 2018) · 2021–2026 (2nd gen) · Type S trim covered in parent section

engine.label: Engine
engine.text: 2015–2020: 2.4L K24W7 DOHC i-VTEC inline-4, direct injection (FWD only); 3.5L J35Y6 SOHC i-VTEC V6, direct injection, VCM. 2021–2026: 2.0L K20C4 DOHC VTEC Turbo inline-4; Type S (2021+): 3.0L J30AC DOHC V6, single twin-scroll turbo.

transmission.label: Transmission
transmission.text: 2015–2020 2.4: Honda 8-speed dual-clutch (8DCT) with torque converter. 2015–2020 3.5: ZF 9HP 9-speed automatic. 2021–2026: Honda 10-speed automatic (both engines).

drivetrain.label: Drivetrain
drivetrain.text: 2015–2020: FWD standard with P-AWS (Precision All-Wheel Steer) on all FWD cars; SH-AWD available on V6 only. 2021–2026: FWD standard, SH-AWD optional on 2.0T; Type S SH-AWD standard.

mpg.label: Fuel economy (MPG)
mpg.text: 2015–2020: 2.4 FWD 24/35/28; V6 FWD 21/34/25; V6 SH-AWD 21/31/25. 2021–2026: 2.0T FWD 22/31/25 (A-Spec FWD 22/30/25); 2.0T SH-AWD 21/29/24; Type S 19/25/21. Premium fuel recommended (required for rated output on turbos).

reliability.label: Reliability
reliability.text: Gen 1 is defined by its transmissions: the Honda 8DCT is durable, the ZF 9HP is the weak link (class action below). Gen 2 shows improved early reliability over gen 1 with no systemic powertrain pattern as the fleet ages (Tedious Repairs, 2026). 2022 TLX earned IIHS Top Safety Pick+ (Acura Newsroom).

knownIssues.label: Known issues
knownIssues.text: 2015–2020 V6: ZF 9HP harsh/erratic shifts, delayed engagement, neutral-drop — subject of class action Moore et al. v. American Honda (2015 TLX, 2016 MDX) and widely documented complaints; speed-sensor recall on 2017 V6/9AT cars (Center for Auto Safety). 2.4 8DCT: low-speed hesitation/judder; VTC actuator cold-start rattle carried over from the TL (Tedious Repairs). 2021–2023: fuel tank assembly recall (Honda Part 573 filing, MY2021–2023); 2018–2021/2023 TLX included in front-passenger seat weight sensor recall (Center for Auto Safety).

maintenance.label: Maintenance
maintenance.text: Honda Maintenance Minder. Oil 0W-20 (~7,500–10,000 mi). K24W7: timing CHAIN; J35Y6: timing BELT ~105k mi/7 yr with water pump; K20C4/J30AC: timing CHAIN. Iridium plugs ~105k mi (K24/J35); turbos per Minder. 8DCT: Honda DCTF-1 fluid, Minder-driven (~30–60k mi). 9HP: dealer-specified ZF Lifeguard 9–equivalent ATF — service proactively (~30–40k mi) given failure history. 10AT: ATF Type 3.1, Minder code 3. SH-AWD rear diff (DPSF) early first service then ~30k mi. Coolant Type 2: 120k/10 yr then 60k/5 yr. Brake fluid every 3 yr.

performance.label: Performance
performance.text: 0–60: 2015 2.4 ≈7.0 s; V6 FWD ≈5.7 s; V6 SH-AWD ≈5.8 s (ZeroTo60Times compilations of period tests). 2021+ 2.0T ≈5.9–6.2 s; Type S ≈4.9–5.1 s (Car and Driver/MotorTrend period instrumented tests — flagged as press-test figures, not re-verified against the source articles this session). Type S adds Brembo front brakes, adaptive dampers, quicker steering ratio, Sport+ mode, quad exhaust, 20-in wheels.

mods.label: Modifications
mods.text: The gen-2 TLX is the current darling of the Honda/Acura tuner scene: KTuner/Hondata-supported ECU tunes for the K20C4 (sharing Accord 2.0T calibration knowledge), intakes, downpipes, intercoolers, lowering springs/coilovers, and 19–20 in wheels. Type S owners add exhausts and mild tunes. Gen-1 V6 cars see basic bolt-ons; the 2.4/8DCT has niche appeal (8DCT behaves like an automated manual).

engineering.label: Engineering
engineering.text: Gen 1 (2015) merged the TL and TSX lines onto an Accord-derived platform with P-AWS rear steering on FWD cars and Honda's in-house 8DCT (a DCT with a torque converter for smooth launches). Gen 2 (2021) moved to a dedicated Acura sedan platform with a double-wishbone front suspension, 56% more torsional rigidity, longer dash-to-axle proportions, the K20C4 turbo + 10AT powertrain shared with the RDX, and the J30AC turbo V6 in the Type S — the most rigid body in Acura sedan history.

enthusiast.label: Enthusiast
enthusiast.text: The community views the gen-2 Type S as the spiritual successor to the TL Type-S and the best-handling Acura sedan short of the NSX era. Tuner support is strong because the K20C4/J30AC ecosystem overlaps with Civic Type R/Accord 2.0T development. Gen-1 V6 SH-AWD cars are cheap used performance bargains — provided the 9-speed has been serviced.

comparison.label: Comparison
comparison.text: Rivals: BMW 3 Series, Audi A4/S4, Mercedes C-Class, Genesis G70, Lexus IS, Infiniti Q50. The TLX Type S matches the M340i/S4 on pace for less money with a better reliability outlook; the base 2.0T trails German turbo fours on fuel economy but wins on standard equipment and resale. Gen-1 cars trail all of the above on transmission refinement (9HP).

acura-mdx-type-s.subtitle: Midsize luxury SUV, 3 rows — 2015–2020 (3rd gen YD3/YD4, facelifted 2017) · no 2021 model year · 2022–2026 (4th gen YD9, facelifted 2025) · Type S trim covered in parent section

engine.label: Engine
engine.text: 2015–2020: 3.5L J35Y5 SOHC i-VTEC V6, direct injection, VCM (290 hp). 2017–2020 Sport Hybrid: 3.0L J30 V6 + 3 electric motors (NSX-derived hybrid system). 2022–2026: 3.5L J35 V6 DI (290 hp); Type S: 3.0L J30AC DOHC V6, single twin-scroll turbo, direct injection.

transmission.label: Transmission
transmission.text: 2014–2015: Honda 6-speed automatic. 2016–2020 gas: ZF 9HP 9-speed automatic. 2017–2020 Sport Hybrid: 7-speed DCT (NSX-derived, motor-integrated). 2022–2026: Honda 10-speed automatic (Type S: sport-tuned 10AT).

horsepower.label: Horsepower
horsepower.text: 2015–2020 gas: 290 hp @ 6,200 rpm. Sport Hybrid: 321 hp system combined. 2022–2026: 290 hp @ 6,200 rpm; Type S 355 hp @ 5,500 rpm.

torque.label: Torque
torque.text: 2015–2020 gas: 267 lb-ft @ 4,700 rpm. Sport Hybrid: 289 lb-ft (engine) @ 5,000 rpm. 2022–2026: 267 lb-ft @ 4,700 rpm; Type S 354 lb-ft @ 1,400 rpm.

drivetrain.label: Drivetrain
drivetrain.text: 2015–2020: FWD standard, SH-AWD optional (Sport Hybrid is SH-AWD only, with twin-motor rear axle). 2022–2026: FWD standard, SH-AWD optional; Type S SH-AWD standard.

reliability.label: Reliability
reliability.text: 2015 (6AT) cars are the pick of the 3rd gen; 2016–2020 9-speed cars carry the ZF 9HP baggage (see Known issues). J.D. Power Quality & Reliability: 2022 MDX 73/100 ("Average"), 2024 MDX 80/100. 2017 MDX earned a 5-star NHTSA overall rating (Acura Newsroom); 2022 MDX earned IIHS Top Safety Pick+ (Acura Newsroom).

knownIssues.label: Known issues
knownIssues.text: 2014–2015 6AT: lock-up clutch judder tied to degraded ATF. 2016 9AT: shift-to-neutral events with DTC P0657 (TSB B15-034), harsh/jerky shifts, internal leak in the 9-speed transmission warmer allowing coolant/ATF mixing (Transmission Audit). ZF 9HP behavior (delayed/harsh shifts, neutral-drop) is the subject of a class action — Moore et al. v. American Honda (covers 2015 TLX and 2016 MDX). SH-AWD rear differential pump connector corrosion triggering AWD DTCs (Transmission Audit). 2017–2020 models included in Honda's Denso low-density fuel-pump-impeller recalls (NHTSA 21V215 and the Dec 2023 expansion, Honda). 2022–2026: front-passenger seat weight sensor recall (Honda/NHTSA via Center for Auto Safety).

maintenance.label: Maintenance
maintenance.text: Honda Maintenance Minder. Oil 0W-20 (~7,500–10,000 mi). J35 V6: timing BELT ~105k mi/7 yr with water pump; J30AC turbo: timing CHAIN. Iridium plugs ~105k mi (J35). ATF: DW-1 (6AT) / ZF Lifeguard 9–spec fluid via dealer (9HP) / ATF Type 3.1 (10AT); Minder code 3, ~30k mi severe service — 9HP fluid discipline is critical given its failure history. SH-AWD rear diff fluid (DPSF; hybrid rear twin-motor unit has its own fluid) Minder-driven, early first service then ~30k mi. Coolant Type 2: 120k/10 yr then 60k/5 yr. Brake fluid every 3 yr. Sport Hybrid: additional inverter/battery cooling checks per Minder.

performance.label: Performance
performance.text: 0–60: 2014–2020 gas ≈6.1–6.4 s; Sport Hybrid ≈5.7–5.8 s; 2022 gas ≈6.0–6.4 s (Car and Driver 6.4); Type S ≈5.4–5.5 s (ZeroTo60Times/C&D compilations, TopSpeed). Type S adds double-wishbone front suspension, Brembo front brakes, adaptive air suspension, Sport+ mode, quad exhaust. Tow rating: 3,500 lbs FWD / 5,000 lbs SH-AWD.

mods.label: Modifications
mods.text: Mostly appearance and utility — wheels (20–21 in), tint, roof racks, trailer hitches; the J35 has no meaningful aftermarket tune support. The Type S is the factory enthusiast variant; mild intake/exhaust mods exist for the J30AC but the platform is too new/heavy for a serious tuner scene.

engineering.label: Engineering
engineering.text: 3rd gen (2014) went on a lighter, more rigid Pilot-related platform with the direct-injected J35 and added the NSX-tech Sport Hybrid in 2017 (7-speed DCT + twin-motor rear unit, no mechanical driveshaft). 4th gen (2022) moved to a new light-truck platform with a double-wishbone front suspension (first for MDX), wider body, Honda 10AT replacing the ZF 9-speed, and — in Type S form — Acura's first turbocharged SUV engine, the J30AC. No 2021 model year was sold; 2025 brought a styling/infotainment facelift (touchscreen replacing the touchpad).

enthusiast.label: Enthusiast
enthusiast.text: The MDX is a family hauler first, but the 2017+ Sport Hybrid has a quiet cult following as an NSX-tech sleeper, and the Type S is genuinely respected as the best-driving 3-row in its price bracket. Forum consensus: avoid early 9-speed cars without documented transmission service.

comparison.label: Comparison
comparison.text: Rivals: BMW X5, Mercedes GLE, Audi Q7, Lexus GX/RX-L, Genesis GV80, Volvo XC90. The MDX wins on resale, reliability history (V6 + Honda 10AT cars), and price-per-feature; it gives up badge prestige and interior richness to the Germans, and off-road/towing credibility to the GX. The Type S undercuts an X5 M50i by a wide margin.
